Kikuyu: Ici nĩ thimo cia Solomoni mũrũ wa Daudi, ũrĩa warĩ mũthamaki wa Isiraeli:
English: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:

Kikuyu: nĩ thimo cia gũtũma mũndũ agĩe na ũũgĩ, na ataarĩke; nĩ cia gũtũma ahote gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ ciugo cia ũũgĩ;
English: to know wisdom and instruction; to discern the words of understanding;

Kikuyu: nĩ cia gũtũma mũndũ ataarĩke ahote kũbarĩrĩra mũtũũrĩre wake, ekage maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire, na ma kĩhooto, na marĩa mega;
English: to receive instruction in wise dealing, in righteousness, justice, and equity;

Kikuyu: nĩ cia gũtũma ũrĩa ũtarĩ na ũũgĩ agĩe na ũmenyo, na cia gũtũma mũndũ mwĩthĩ agĩe na ũmenyo na ũgereri:
English: to give prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the young man—

Kikuyu: arĩa oogĩ-rĩ, nĩmathikĩrĩrie na mongerere wĩruti wao, nao arĩa akũũrani maũndũ metĩkĩre gũtongorio.
English: that the wise man may hear, and increase in learning; that the man of understanding may attain to sound counsel;

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩ cia gũtũma mũndũ ataũkĩrwo nĩ thimo na ngerekano, na ataũkĩrwo nĩ ciugo cia andũ arĩa oogĩ, na ndaĩ ciao.
English: to understand a proverb and parables, the words and riddles of the wise.

Kikuyu: Kĩambĩrĩria kĩa ũmenyo nĩ gwĩtigĩra Jehova, no arĩa akĩĩgu nĩ mathũire ũũgĩ na ũtaaro.
English: The fear of Yahweh† is the beginning of knowledge, but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, thikĩrĩria mataaro ma thoguo, na ndũgatigane na ũrutani wa maitũguo.
English: My son, listen to your father’s instruction, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching;

Kikuyu: Maũndũ macio magaatuĩka ithaga rĩa kũgemia mũtwe waku, na ta mũgathĩ wa kũgemia ngingo yaku.
English: for they will be a garland to grace your head, and chains around your neck.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ehia mangĩgakũheenereria-rĩ, ndũkanetĩkanie nao.
English: My son, if sinners entice you, don’t consent.

Kikuyu: Mangĩgakwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka ũthiĩ hamwe na ithuĩ; reke tuoherie mũndũ tũite thakame yake, reke tũcemaceeme mũndũ o na ũtehĩtie;
English: If they say, “Come with us. Let’s lie in wait for blood. Let’s lurk secretly for the innocent without cause.

Kikuyu: ningĩ reke tũmamerie marĩ muoyo, o ta ũrĩa mbĩrĩra ĩĩkaga, na marĩ agima, o ta arĩa maharũrũkaga irima;
English: Let’s swallow them up alive like Sheol,‡ and whole, like those who go down into the pit.

Kikuyu: Na ithuĩ-rĩ, tũkĩgĩe na indo cia goro cia mĩthemba yothe, na tũiyũrie nyũmba ciitũ na indo cia ndaho;
English: We’ll find all valuable wealth. We’ll fill our houses with plunder.

Kikuyu: rutithania wĩra na ithuĩ, tũkorwo na mũhuko ũmwe ithuothe”;
English: You shall cast your lot among us. We’ll all have one purse”—

Kikuyu: mũrũ wakwa, tiga gũtwarana nao, ndũkagerere njĩra ciao;
English: my son, don’t walk on the path with them. Keep your foot from their path,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo magũrũ mao mahanyũkaga mathiĩ mehia-inĩ, nao mahiũhaga magaite thakame.
English: for their feet run to evil. They hurry to shed blood.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ nĩ wĩra wa tũhũ kũiga gĩkerenge nyoni ciothe igĩkuonaga-ĩ!
English: For the net is spread in vain in the sight of any bird;

Kikuyu: Andũ aya mohagĩria thakame yao ene; maceemaga o mĩoyo yao ene!
English: but these lay in wait for their own blood. They lurk secretly for their own lives.

Kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo mũthia wa arĩa othe magĩaga na indo na njĩra ya ungumania; indo icio iniinaga mĩoyo ya arĩa magĩaga nacio.
English: So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain. It takes away the life of its owners.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩ aretana anĩrĩire njĩra-inĩ, ningĩ agakayũrũrũka arĩ ihaaro-inĩ cia mũingĩ;
English: Wisdom calls aloud in the street. She utters her voice in the public squares.

Kikuyu: Aragũthũka na mũgambo mũnene arĩ magomano-inĩ ma njĩra kũu kũrĩ inegene, o na itoonyero rĩa itũũra inene akoria atĩrĩ:
English: She calls at the head of noisy places. At the entrance of the city gates, she utters her words:

Kikuyu: “Inyuĩ mũtarĩ ũũgĩ-rĩ, nĩ nginya rĩ mũgũtũũra mwendete njĩra cianyu itarĩ cia ũũgĩ? Nĩ nginya rĩ anyũrũrania megũtũũra makenagĩra kĩnyũrũri, na arĩa akĩĩgu mathũire ũmenyo?
English: “How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity? How long will mockers delight themselves in mockery, and fools hate knowledge?

Kikuyu: Korwo nĩmwetĩkĩrire kũigua ũtaaro wakwa-rĩ, nĩingĩamũhingũrĩire ngoro yakwa, ndũme mũmenye meciiria makwa.
English: Turn at my reproof. Behold,§ I will pour out my spirit on you. I will make known my words to you.

Kikuyu: No tondũ nĩmwaregire kũnjigua rĩrĩa ndetanire, na gũtirĩ mũndũ werũmbũirie hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndatambũrũkirie guoko gwakwa,
English: Because I have called, and you have refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no one has paid attention;

Kikuyu: na mũkĩagĩra mataaro makwa mothe bata, na mũkĩaga gwĩtĩkĩra ikaanania rĩakwa-rĩ,
English: but you have ignored all my counsel, and wanted none of my reproof;

Kikuyu: niĩ na niĩ nĩngamũthekerera hĩndĩ yanyu ya mwanangĩko, na ndĩmũnyũrũrie rĩrĩa mũgaakorererwo nĩ mũtino:
English: I also will laugh at your disaster. I will mock when calamity overtakes you,

Kikuyu: ĩĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa guoya ũkaamũkorerera ta kĩhuhũkanio, rĩrĩa mwanangĩko ũkaamũhaata ta rũhuho rwa kĩhuhũkanio, rĩrĩa mũgaakinyĩrĩrwo nĩ mĩnyamaro na ruo rwa ngoro.
English: when calamity overtakes you like a storm, when your disaster comes on like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come on you.

Kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakangaĩra, no ndikametĩka; nĩmakanjetha na kĩyo, no matikanyona.
English: Then they will call on me, but I will not answer. They will seek me diligently, but they will not find me,

Kikuyu: Tondũ nĩmathũũrire ũmenyo, na matiigana gũthuura gwĩtigĩra Jehova,
English: because they hated knowledge, and didn’t choose the fear of Yahweh.

Kikuyu: tondũ matietĩkĩrire mataaro makwa, na nĩmamenire ikaanania rĩakwa,
English: They wanted none of my counsel. They despised all my reproof.

Kikuyu: makaarĩa maciaro ma mĩthiĩre yao, na mahũũnio nĩ maciaro ma mathugunda mao.
English: Therefore they will eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own schemes.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũgenyũ wa arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ nĩũkamooraga, nakuo kwĩgangara kwa arĩa akĩĩgu nĩgũkamaniina;
English: For the backsliding of the simple will kill them. The careless ease of fools will destroy them.

Kikuyu: no ũrĩa wothe ũnjiguaga arĩtũũraga na thayũ, na atũũre ahooreire, atarĩ ũgwati angĩĩtigĩra.” 2
English: But whoever listens to me will dwell securely, and will be at ease, without fear of harm.” 2

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ũngĩtĩkĩra ciugo ciakwa, na ũige maathani makwa ta mũthiithũ thĩinĩ waku,
English: My son, if you will receive my words, and store up my commandments within you,

Kikuyu: nakuo gũtũ gwaku ũgũtegere ũũgĩ, na werekerie ngoro yaku kũgĩa na ũmenyi,
English: so as to turn your ear to wisdom, and apply your heart to understanding;

Kikuyu: na ũngĩgaakayũrũrũka na ũhooe gũkũũrana maũndũ, na wĩtane wanĩrĩire ũheo ũtaũku,
English: yes, if you call out for discernment, and lift up your voice for understanding;

Kikuyu: na ũngĩkawetha ta betha, na ũũcarie ta kĩgĩĩna kĩhithe-rĩ,
English: if you seek her as silver, and search for her as for hidden treasures;

Kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgataũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro wa gwĩtigĩra Jehova, na ũgĩe na ũmenyo wa Ngai.
English: then you will understand the fear of Yahweh, and find the knowledge of God.†

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova aheanaga ũũgĩ, na kanua gake koimaga ũmenyo na ũtaũku.
English: For Yahweh gives wisdom. Out of his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru amaigĩire ũhootani, nĩwe ngo ya arĩa matarĩ ũcuuke mĩthiĩre-inĩ yao,
English: He lays up sound wisdom for the upright. He is a shield to those who walk in integrity,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩarangagĩra njĩra cia arĩa marũmagĩrĩra kĩhooto, na akagitĩra njĩra ya andũ ake arĩa ehokeku.
English: that he may guard the paths of justice, and preserve the way of his saints.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgakũũrana maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire, na ma kĩhooto, na marĩa mega: ũgĩkũũrane njĩra o yothe njega.
English: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, equity and every good path.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũũgĩ nĩũgatoonya ngoro yaku, naguo ũmenyo ũkenagie muoyo waku.
English: For wisdom will enter into your heart. Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.

Kikuyu: ũgereri nĩũrĩkũgitagĩra, naguo ũtaũku ũkũrangagĩre.
English: Discretion will watch over you. Understanding will keep you,

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩũgakũhonokia kuuma njĩra-inĩ cia andũ arĩa aaganu, ũkũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa maaragia maũndũ ma waganu,
English: to deliver you from the way of evil, from the men who speak perverse things,

Kikuyu: o arĩa matigaga njĩra iria nũngĩrĩru, magathiĩra njĩra cia nduma,
English: who forsake the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness,

Kikuyu: arĩa makenaga magĩĩka ũũru, na magakenagio nĩ ũũru wa waganu,
English: who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the perverseness of evil,

Kikuyu: na no-o arĩa njĩra ciao ciogomete, na mĩthiĩre yao ĩrĩ ya ungumania.
English: who are crooked in their ways, and wayward in their paths,

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩũgakũhonokia kuuma harĩ mũtumia ũrĩa mũtharia, ũkũhonokie harĩ ngʼenyũ ya mũtumia na ciugo ciake cia kũheenereria andũ,
English: to deliver you from the strange woman, even from the foreigner who flatters with her words,

Kikuyu: ũcio ũtigĩte mũratawe wa ũnini wake, na agatiganĩria kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa aarĩkanĩire na mũthuuriwe arĩ mbere ya Ngai.
English: who forsakes the friend of her youth, and forgets the covenant of her God;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nyũmba yake ĩroragia andũ gĩkuũ-inĩ, nacio njĩra ciake ikamerekeria kũrĩ maroho ma arĩa akuũ.
English: for her house leads down to death, her paths to the departed spirits.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũthiiaga harĩ we agacooka, kana akegwatĩra njĩra cia muoyo.
English: None who go to her return again, neither do they attain to the paths of life.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, wee nĩũrĩthiiaga na mĩthiĩre ya andũ arĩa ega, na ũgeragĩre njĩra cia arĩa athingu.
English: Therefore walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩo magaatũũra bũrũri-inĩ, na arĩa matarĩ ũcuuke nĩo magaatigara kuo;
English: For the upright will dwell in the land. The perfect will remain in it.

Kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmakeeherio bũrũri-inĩ, nao arĩa matarĩ ehokeku nĩmakamunywo maingatwo kuo. 3
English: But the wicked will be cut off from the land. The treacherous will be rooted out of it. 3

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ndũkariganĩrwo nĩ ũrutani wakwa, no ngoro yaku nĩĩrũmagie maathani makwa,
English: My son, don’t forget my teaching, but let your heart keep my commandments,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩmagakuongerera matukũ ũtũũre mĩaka mĩingĩ, na matũme ũgaacĩre.
English: for they will add to you length of days, years of life, and peace.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanareke wendani na wĩhokeku ikweherere; cioherere ngingo yaku, ũciandĩke kĩhengere-inĩ kĩa ngoro yaku.
English: Don’t let kindness and truth forsake you. Bind them around your neck. Write them on the tablet of your heart.

Kikuyu: Weka ũguo nĩũgeetĩkĩrĩka, na ũgĩe na rĩĩtwa rĩega maitho-inĩ ma Ngai o na ma andũ.
English: So you will find favor, and good understanding in the sight of God and man.

Kikuyu: Wĩhoke Jehova na ngoro yaku yothe, na ndũkanehoke ũmenyo waku mwene;
English: Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding.

Kikuyu: mũmenyithagie mĩthiĩre yaku yothe, na nĩarĩrũngaga njĩra ciaku.
English: In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.

Kikuyu: Tiga kwĩonaga ũrĩ mũũgĩ maitho-inĩ maku mwene; wĩtigĩre Jehova na ũtheemage maũndũ marĩa mooru.
English: Don’t be wise in your own eyes. Fear Yahweh, and depart from evil.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio nĩũrĩkũrehagĩra ũgima wa mwĩrĩ, na mahĩndĩ maku makoragwo marĩ na hinya.
English: It will be health to your body, and nourishment to your bones.

Kikuyu: Tĩĩaga Jehova na ũtonga waku, ningĩ ũmũtĩĩage na maciaro mothe ma mbere ma irio ciaku cia mĩgũnda;
English: Honor Yahweh with your substance, with the first fruits of all your increase;

Kikuyu: namo makũmbĩ maku nĩmakaiyũrĩrĩra, nayo mĩtũngi yaku ĩiyũrĩrĩre ndibei ya mũhihano.
English: so your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will overflow with new wine.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, tiga kũnyarara ihũũra rĩa Jehova, na ndũgathũũre irũithia rĩake,
English: My son, don’t despise Yahweh’s discipline, neither be weary of his correction;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Jehova arũithagia arĩa endete, o ta ũrĩa ithe wa mũndũ arũithagia mũriũ ũrĩa akenagĩra.
English: for whom Yahweh loves, he corrects, even as a father reproves the son in whom he delights.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wonete ũũgĩ, mũndũ ũcio wĩgĩĩrĩire na ũmenyo,
English: Happy is the man who finds wisdom, the man who gets understanding.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ũũgĩ nĩ wa bata mũno gũkĩra betha, na ũciaraga uumithio ũkĩrĩte wa thahabu.
English: For her good profit is better than getting silver, and her return is better than fine gold.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ ũrĩ goro gũkĩra ruru iria ndune; kĩrĩa gĩothe ũngĩĩrirĩria gũtirĩ kĩngĩringithanio naguo.
English: She is more precious than rubies. None of the things you can desire are to be compared to her.

Kikuyu: Guoko-inĩ gwake kwa ũrĩo harĩ ũingĩ wa matukũ; guoko-inĩ gwake kwa ũmotho harĩ na ũtonga na gĩtĩĩo.
English: Length of days is in her right hand. In her left hand are riches and honor.

Kikuyu: Mĩthiĩre ya ũũgĩ nĩ mĩthiĩre ya kwendeka, na njĩra ciaguo ciothe nĩ cia thayũ.
English: Her ways are ways of pleasantness. All her paths are peace.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩ mũtĩ wa muoyo kũrĩ arĩa maũhĩmbagĩria; arĩa megĩagĩra naguo-rĩ, nĩmarĩrathimagwo.
English: She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her. Happy is everyone who retains her.

Kikuyu: Jehova aahandire mĩthingi ya thĩ na ũũgĩ, na akĩrũmia igũrũ na ũmenyo wake;
English: By wisdom Yahweh founded the earth. By understanding, he established the heavens.

Kikuyu: ũmenyo wake nĩguo wagayanirie kũrĩa kũriku, namo matu magatatagia ime.
English: By his knowledge, the depths were broken up, and the skies drop down the dew.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa tũũria matua mega na ũkũũrani maũndũ, ndũkanareke ciehere maitho-inĩ maku;
English: My son, let them not depart from your eyes. Keep sound wisdom and discretion,

Kikuyu: nĩcio igaagũtũũria muoyo, na nĩ ithaga rĩa kũgemagia ngingo yaku.
English: so they will be life to your soul, and grace for your neck.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ ũrĩthiiaga na njĩra yaku ũrĩ na thayũ, na kũgũrũ gwaku gũtikahĩngwo;
English: Then you shall walk in your way securely. Your foot won’t stumble.

Kikuyu: Wakoma-rĩ, ndũgetigĩra; wakoma-rĩ, ũrĩkomaga toro mwega.
English: When you lie down, you will not be afraid. Yes, you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.

Kikuyu: Ndũgetigĩre mwanangĩko wa narua, kana kĩhuhũkanio kĩrĩa gĩkoragĩrĩra andũ arĩa aaganu,
English: Don’t be afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it comes;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩwe ũgaatuĩka mwĩhoko waku, na amenyagĩrĩre kũgũrũ gwaku gũtikanyiitwo nĩ mũtego.
English: for Yahweh will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from being taken.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanagirĩrĩrie ũndũ mwega ũkinyĩre arĩa maagĩrĩirwo nĩgũkinyĩrĩrwo nĩguo, rĩrĩa ũrĩ na ũhoti wa kũhingia ũndũ ũcio.
English: Don’t withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in the power of your hand to do it.

Kikuyu: Ndũkaneere mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ ũgooka rĩngĩ; kĩu ũrenda nĩngakũhe rũciũ,” o rĩrĩa ũrĩ na kĩndũ kĩu hakuhĩ.
English: Don’t say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again; tomorrow I will give it to you,” when you have it by you.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanaciirĩre gwĩka mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku ũũru, ũcio ũtũũraga hakuhĩ nawe akwĩhokete.
English: Don’t devise evil against your neighbor, since he dwells securely by you.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanarũithie mũndũ tũhũ, rĩrĩa atarĩ ũndũ mũũru agwĩkĩte.
English: Don’t strive with a man without cause, if he has done you no harm.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanacumĩkĩre maũndũ ma mũndũ wa ngũĩ, kana ũthuure njĩra o na ĩmwe yake,
English: Don’t envy the man of violence. Choose none of his ways.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩathũire mũndũ mwaganu, no nĩagĩĩaga ndundu na andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru.
English: For the perverse is an abomination to Yahweh, but his friendship is with the upright.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba ya arĩa aaganu ĩtũũraga na kĩrumi kĩa Jehova, no mũciĩ wa arĩa athingu nĩaũrathimaga.
English: Yahweh’s curse is in the house of the wicked, but he blesses the habitation of the righteous.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩanyũrũragia andũ arĩa manyũrũranagia na mwĩtĩĩo, no arĩa menyiihagia nĩamaheaga wega wake.
English: Surely he mocks the mockers, but he gives grace to the humble.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa oogĩ magayaga gĩtĩĩo, no andũ arĩa akĩĩgu magayaga njono. 4
English: The wise will inherit glory, but shame will be the promotion of fools. 4

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ ariũ akwa, ta thikĩrĩriai ũrũtani wa thoguo; njiguai nĩguo mwĩgĩĩre na kũmenya maũndũ.
English: Listen, sons, to a father’s instruction. Pay attention and know understanding;

Kikuyu: Tondũ ngũmũhe ũrutani mwega, nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũtigatirike ũrutani wakwa.
English: for I give you sound learning. Don’t forsake my law.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndaarĩ mwana nyũmba-inĩ ya baba, ndĩ o mũndũ mwĩthĩ, na ndĩ mwana wa mũmwe wa maitũ-rĩ,
English: For I was a son to my father, tender and an only child in the sight of my mother.

Kikuyu: baba nĩandutire, akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩgwatĩre ciugo ciakwa na ngoro yaku yothe; rũmia maathani makwa, na nĩũgũtũũra muoyo.
English: He taught me, and said to me: “Let your heart retain my words. Keep my commandments, and live.

Kikuyu: Gĩa na ũũgĩ, gĩa na kũmenya maũndũ; ndũkanariganĩrwo nĩ ciugo ciakwa, kana wahũke kuuma harĩ cio.
English: Get wisdom. Get understanding. Don’t forget, and don’t deviate from the words of my mouth.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanatirike ũũgĩ, naguo nĩũrĩkũgitagĩra;
English: Don’t forsake her, and she will preserve you. Love her, and she will keep you.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ ũkĩrĩte indo ciothe; nĩ ũndũ ũcio wĩgĩĩre na ũũgĩ. Ĩĩ, kaba kũruta indo ciaku ciothe wĩgĩĩre na kũmenya maũndũ.
English: Wisdom is supreme. Get wisdom. Yes, though it costs all your possessions, get understanding.

Kikuyu: Tũũgagĩria ũũgĩ mũno, naguo nĩũrĩkwambararagia; ũhĩmbagĩrie, naguo nĩũrĩgũtĩĩithagia.
English: Esteem her, and she will exalt you. She will bring you to honor when you embrace her.

Kikuyu: Nĩũgagwĩkĩra ithaga rĩa ũtugi mũtwe waku, na ũkũhe thũmbĩ thaka mũno.”
English: She will give to your head a garland of grace. She will deliver a crown of splendor to you.”

Kikuyu: Thikĩrĩria, wee mũrũ wakwa, na wĩtĩkĩre ũrĩa ngũkwĩra, nĩgeetha mĩaka ya muoyo waku ĩingĩhe.
English: Listen, my son, and receive my sayings. The years of your life will be many.

Kikuyu: Nguonetie njĩra ya ũũgĩ, na ngagũtongoria njĩra-inĩ iria nũngarũ.
English: I have taught you in the way of wisdom. I have led you in straight paths.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũgũthiĩ, makinya maku matikahĩngĩcwo; rĩrĩa ũkũhanyũka-rĩ, ndũkahĩngwo.
English: When you go, your steps will not be hampered. When you run, you will not stumble.

Kikuyu: Rũmia ũrutani wakwa, na ndũkanareke ũkweherere; ũgitagĩre wega, nĩgũkorwo nĩguo muoyo waku.
English: Take firm hold of instruction. Don’t let her go. Keep her, for she is your life.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanagerere njĩra ya andũ arĩa aaganu, kana ũthiĩre njĩra ya andũ arĩa ooru.
English: Don’t enter into the path of the wicked. Don’t walk in the way of evil men.

Kikuyu: Mĩĩtheemage, na ndũkanamĩgerere; mĩhutatĩre, na wĩthiĩre na njĩra yaku.
English: Avoid it, and don’t pass by it. Turn from it, and pass on.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ arĩa aaganu matingĩkoma nginya meeke ũũru; mooragwo nĩ toro nginya matũme mũndũ ahĩngwo, agwe.
English: For they don’t sleep unless they do evil. Their sleep is taken away, unless they make someone fall.

Kikuyu: Marĩĩaga irio iria monete na ũmaramari, na makanyua ndibei matunyanĩte na hinya.
English: For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.

Kikuyu: Mĩthiĩre ya andũ arĩa athingu ĩhaana ta ũtheri wa gũgĩkĩa, ũrĩa ũthiiaga ũgĩthererũkaga nginya mũthenya barigici.
English: But the path of the righteous is like the dawning light that shines more and more until the perfect day.

Kikuyu: No mĩthiĩre ya andũ arĩa aaganu ĩhaana ta nduma ndumanu; o matimenyaga kĩrĩa kĩamahĩnga.
English: The way of the wicked is like darkness. They don’t know what they stumble over.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ta thikĩrĩria ũrĩa ngũkwĩra; tega gũtũ ũigue ciugo ciakwa.
English: My son, attend to my words. Turn your ear to my sayings.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanareke ciehere maitho-inĩ maku, ciigage ngoro-inĩ yaku;
English: Let them not depart from your eyes. Keep them in the center of your heart.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩcio itũũragia arĩa maciĩgwatĩire muoyo, na igekĩra mwĩrĩ wao wothe ũgima.
English: For they are life to those who find them, and health to their whole body.

Kikuyu: Makĩria ya macio mothe, rangagĩra ngoro yaku, nĩgũkorwo nĩyo gĩthima kĩrĩa gĩtherũkaga muoyo.
English: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it is the wellspring of life.

Kikuyu: Eheria rũnano kanua-inĩ gaku; nacio ndeto cia ũmaramari-rĩ, nĩciikare kũraya na iromo ciaku.
English: Put away from yourself a perverse mouth. Put corrupt lips far from you.

Kikuyu: Maitho maku nĩmarorage na mbere, ũcũthagĩrĩrie mbere yaku wega.
English: Let your eyes look straight ahead. Fix your gaze directly before you.

Kikuyu: Aragania njĩra iria magũrũ maku marĩthiiagĩra, na ũthiiagĩre o njĩra iria ngiindĩrĩku.
English: Make the path of your feet level. Let all of your ways be established.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanaahũke na mwena wa ũrĩo kana wa ũmotho; eheria kũgũrũ gwaku kuuma ũũru-inĩ. 5
English: Don’t turn to the right hand nor to the left. Remove your foot from evil. 5

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, thikĩrĩria ũũgĩ ũyũ wakwa, tega gũtũ wega ũigue ciugo ciakwa cia ũtaũku,
English: My son, pay attention to my wisdom. Turn your ear to my understanding,

Kikuyu: nĩguo ũtũũrie ũbaarĩrĩri, nayo mĩromo yaku ĩtũũrie ũmenyo.
English: that you may maintain discretion, that your lips may preserve knowledge.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩromo ya mũtumia mũtharia itaataga ũũkĩ, na mĩario yake ĩnyorokete gũkĩra maguta;
English: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey. Her mouth is smoother than oil,

Kikuyu: no marigĩrĩrio nĩmũrũrũ ta maaĩ ma nyongo, nĩ mũũgĩ ta rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩa rũũgĩ mbarĩ cierĩ.
English: but in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, and as sharp as a two-edged sword.

Kikuyu: Magũrũ make maikũrũkaga gĩkuũ-inĩ; makinya make mathiiaga merekeire o mbĩrĩra.
English: Her feet go down to death. Her steps lead straight to Sheol.†

Kikuyu: Ndeciiragia ũhoro wa njĩra ya muoyo; njĩra ciake nĩ njogomu, nowe ndooĩ ũguo.
English: She gives no thought to the way of life. Her ways are crooked, and she doesn’t know it.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ariũ akwa, ta thikĩrĩriai; mũtikanatiganĩrie ũrĩa ngũmwĩra.
English: Now therefore, my sons, listen to me. Don’t depart from the words of my mouth.

Kikuyu: Thiiagĩrai njĩra ĩrĩ kũraya nake, mũtigakuhĩrĩrie mũrango wa nyũmba yake,
English: Remove your way far from her. Don’t come near the door of her house,

Kikuyu: nĩguo mũtikaheane hinya wanyu mwega kũrĩ andũ angĩ, na mĩaka yanyu kũrĩ mũndũ ũtarĩ tha,
English: lest you give your honor to others, and your years to the cruel one;

Kikuyu: nĩguo ageni matikehũũnie na ũtonga wanyu, na kĩrĩa ũnogeire gĩtongie mũciĩ wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ.
English: lest strangers feast on your wealth, and your labors enrich another man’s house.

Kikuyu: Marigĩrĩrio-inĩ ma muoyo waku nĩũgacaaya, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa nyama cia mwĩrĩ waku na mwĩrĩ waku igaakorwo ithirĩte hinya.
English: You will groan at your latter end, when your flesh and your body are consumed,

Kikuyu: Ũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ, kaĩ nĩndathũũrire gũtaarwo-ĩ! Hĩ, kaĩ ngoro yakwa nĩyamenete kũrũithio-ĩ!
English: and say, “How I have hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof.

Kikuyu: Ndingĩathĩkĩire arutani akwa, kana thikĩrĩrie ataarani akwa.
English: I haven’t obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor turned my ear to those who instructed me!

Kikuyu: Nĩnginyĩte hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia mwanangĩko, ndĩ o gatagatĩ-inĩ ga kĩũngano gĩothe.”
English: I have come to the brink of utter ruin, among the gathered assembly.”

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nyuuaga maaĩ ma itangi rĩaku we mwene, maaĩ marĩa mathereraga moimĩte gĩthima-inĩ gĩaku mwene.
English: Drink water out of your own cistern, running water out of your own well.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kwagĩrĩire ithima ciaku ciunagĩre maaĩ barabara-inĩ, kana tũrũũĩ twaku tuunagĩre ihaaro-inĩ cia mũingĩ?
English: Should your springs overflow in the streets, streams of water in the public squares?

Kikuyu: Nĩmagĩtũũre marĩ maku wiki, matigatuĩke maku na mũndũ wa kũngĩ o na rĩ.
English: Let them be for yourself alone, not for strangers with you.

Kikuyu: Gĩthima gĩaku kĩrorathimwo, nawe ũkenagĩre mũtumia wa ũnini waku.
English: Let your spring be blessed. Rejoice in the wife of your youth.

Kikuyu: Athakarĩte ta thwariga ya mũgoma, akaagĩra ta gathiiya ka mũgoma: kĩiganagwo nĩ nyondo ciake hĩndĩ ciothe, ũkenagio nĩ wendo wake hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: A loving doe and a graceful deer— let her breasts satisfy you at all times. Be captivated always with her love.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma ũkenio nĩ mũtumia mũtharia? Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma ũhĩmbĩrie gĩthũri kĩa mũtumia wa mũndũ ũngĩ?
English: For why should you, my son, be captivated with an adulteress? Why embrace the bosom of another?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩthiĩre ya mũndũ nĩyonagwo nĩ Jehova, nake nĩatuĩragia njĩra ciake ciothe.
English: For the ways of man are before Yahweh’s eyes. He examines all his paths.

Kikuyu: Ciĩko njũru cia mũndũ mwaganu nĩcio imũtegaga; nga cia mehia make nĩcio imuohaga, ikamũrũmia biũ.
English: The evil deeds of the wicked ensnare him. The cords of his sin hold him firmly.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio nĩagaakua nĩ kwaga ũtaaro, akoora nĩ ũndũ wa ũrimũ wake mũnene. 6
English: He will die for lack of instruction. In the greatness of his folly, he will go astray. 6

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ũngĩkorwo ũigĩte kĩndũ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku, ũkorwo atĩ nĩwe ũtuĩkĩte wa kũmũrĩhĩra thiirĩ harĩ mũndũ ũngĩ-rĩ,
English: My son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor, if you have struck your hands in pledge for a stranger,

Kikuyu: ũngĩkorwo ũtegeetwo nĩ ũrĩa uugĩte, ũkagwatithio nĩ ciugo cia kanua gaku-rĩ,
English: you are trapped by the words of your mouth; you are ensnared with the words of your mouth.

Kikuyu: gĩĩke atĩrĩ, mũrũ wakwa, nĩguo wĩteithũre, kuona atĩ nĩũgwĩte moko-inĩ ma mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku: Thiĩ wĩnyiihie wee mwene; mũthaithe mũno akuohere.
English: Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, since you have come into the hand of your neighbor. Go, humble yourself. Press your plea with your neighbor.

Kikuyu: Ndũkareke maitho maku mone toro, kana ũreke imone ciaku icũnge.
English: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids.

Kikuyu: Wĩteithũre o ta ũrĩa thwariga ĩĩteithũraga kuuma moko-inĩ ma mũguĩmi, o na kana ta ũrĩa nyoni ĩĩteithũraga kuuma mũtego-inĩ wa mũtegi.
English: Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, like a bird from the snare of the fowler.

Kikuyu: Thiĩ harĩ thigiriri, wee kĩgũũta gĩkĩ; ũcũũranie mĩtugo yayo nĩguo ũũhĩge!
English: Go to the ant, you sluggard. Consider her ways, and be wise;

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩ mũnene, kana mũmĩrũgamĩrĩri, o na kana mũmĩathi,
English: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,

Kikuyu: no nĩĩigagĩra mũthiithũ wa irio hĩndĩ ya riũa, na ĩgacookanĩrĩria irio ciayo hĩndĩ ya magetha.
English: provides her bread in the summer, and gathers her food in the harvest.

Kikuyu: Ũgũkoma hau nginya-rĩ, wee kĩgũũta gĩkĩ? Ũrĩũkĩra rĩ, ũtige toro waku?
English: How long will you sleep, sluggard? When will you arise out of your sleep?

Kikuyu: Komakoma gatoro kanini, na ũcũngacũnge hanini, na ũkũnje moko hanini nĩguo ũhurũke;
English: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep—

Kikuyu: atĩrĩ, ũthĩĩni ũgaagũkorerera ta mũitũ, ũkorererwo nĩ wagi ũhaana ta mũndũ wĩohete indo cia mbaara.
English: so your poverty will come as a robber, and your scarcity as an armed man.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ kĩmaramari na mwaganu-rĩ, ũrĩa ũthiiaga akĩaragia ndeto itarĩ nũngĩrĩru,
English: A worthless person, a man of iniquity, is he who walks with a perverse mouth,

Kikuyu: ũcio uunagĩra andũ riitho, na akaheenereria andũ na makinya make, o na akaheneria andũ na ciara ciake-rĩ,
English: who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, who motions with his fingers,

Kikuyu: ũcio ũthugundaga ũũru arĩ na maheeni ngoro-inĩ yake: ũcio hĩndĩ ciothe ahuraga maũndũ ma nyamũkano.
English: in whose heart is perverseness, who devises evil continually, who always sows discord.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũtino ũkaamũhubanĩria o rĩmwe aterĩgĩrĩire; mwanangĩko ũtarĩ kĩhonia nĩũkamũniina biũ.
English: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly. He will be broken suddenly, and that without remedy.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ maũndũ matandatũ mathũirwo nĩ Jehova, ĩĩ, o na nĩ mũgwanja marĩa onaga marĩ magigi, nĩmo:
English: There are six things which Yahweh hates; yes, seven which are an abomination to him:

Kikuyu: maitho ma mwĩtĩĩo, na rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rũheenanagia, na moko marĩa maitaga thakame ya andũ matehĩtie,
English: arrogant eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,

Kikuyu: na ngoro ĩrĩa ĩthugundaga maũndũ ma waganu, na magũrũ marĩa matengʼeraga gũthiĩ gwĩka ũũru,
English: a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are swift in running to mischief,

Kikuyu: na mũira ũtarĩ wa ma ũrĩa ũthothoraga ndeto cia maheeni, o na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrehaga nyamũkano gatagatĩ ka andũ a nyina ũmwe.
English: a false witness who utters lies, and he who sows discord among brothers.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, tũũra ũrũmĩtie maathani ma thoguo, na ndũgatirike ũrutani wa maitũguo.
English: My son, keep your father’s commandment, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching.

Kikuyu: Moherere ngoro-inĩ yaku nginya tene; makundĩke marigiicĩrie ngingo yaku.
English: Bind them continually on your heart. Tie them around your neck.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũgũthiĩ-rĩ, nĩmarĩgũtongoragia; wakoma-rĩ, nĩmarĩkũmenyagĩrĩra; na rĩrĩa warahũka-rĩ, nĩmarĩkwaragĩria.
English: When you walk, it will lead you. When you sleep, it will watch over you. When you awake, it will talk with you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maathani maya nĩ tawa, na ũrutani ũyũ nĩ ũtheri, namo mataaro ma kũrũnga mũndũ-rĩ, nĩmo njĩra ya muoyo,
English: For the commandment is a lamp, and the law is light. Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,

Kikuyu: magakweheragia harĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio mwaganu, na magakũhonokia kuuma kũrĩ rũrĩmĩ rũnyoroku rwa mũtumia ũcio ngʼenyũ.
English: to keep you from the immoral woman, from the flattery of the wayward wife’s tongue.

Kikuyu: Ndũkerirĩrie ũthaka wake ngoro-inĩ yaku, kana ũreke akũguucĩrĩrie na maitho make,
English: Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart, neither let her captivate you with her eyelids.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũmaraya atũmaga mũndũ athĩĩne nginya ahooyage kenyũ ka mũgate, na mũtumia mũtharia aguĩmaga muoyo waku guo mwene.
English: For a prostitute reduces you to a piece of bread. The adulteress hunts for your precious life.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ no atahĩre mwaki gĩthũri-inĩ gĩake, na nguo ciake itihĩe?
English: Can a man scoop fire into his lap, and his clothes not be burned?

Kikuyu: Mũndũ no agerere igũrũ rĩa makara marĩ na mwaki, na makinya make matihĩe?
English: Or can one walk on hot coals, and his feet not be scorched?

Kikuyu: Ũguo no taguo mũndũ ũrĩa ũkomaga na mũtumia wa mũndũ ũngĩ atariĩ; gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩmũhutia na ndaherithio.
English: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife. Whoever touches her will not be unpunished.

Kikuyu: Andũ matimenaga mũici angĩkorwo aiyĩte nĩguo eniinĩre ngʼaragu rĩrĩa arĩ mũhũũtu.
English: Men don’t despise a thief if he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry,

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, angĩnyiitwo-rĩ, no nginya arĩhe maita mũgwanja, o na angĩkorwo ekũrĩha na ũtonga wa nyũmba yake wothe.
English: but if he is found, he shall restore seven times. He shall give all the wealth of his house.

Kikuyu: No mũndũ ũrĩa ũtharagia nĩagĩte wĩciirio; ũrĩa wothe wĩkaga ũguo nĩwe mwene wĩyanangaga.
English: He who commits adultery with a woman is void of understanding. He who does it destroys his own soul.

Kikuyu: Nguraro na kwĩnyararithia nĩcio igai rĩake, na cio thoni icio ciake gũtirĩ hĩndĩ igaathario ithire;
English: He will get wounds and dishonor. His reproach will not be wiped away.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ũiru nĩguo warahũraga mangʼũrĩ ma mũthuuri, na ndakaigua tha akĩĩrĩhĩria.
English: For jealousy arouses the fury of the husband. He won’t spare in the day of vengeance.

Kikuyu: Ndagetĩkĩra irĩhi o na rĩrĩkũ; nĩakaarega ihaki o na rĩrĩ inene atĩa. 7
English: He won’t regard any ransom, neither will he rest content, though you give many gifts. 7

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, tũũra ũrũmĩtie ciugo ciakwa, na wĩigĩre maathani makwa thĩinĩ waku.
English: My son, keep my words. Lay up my commandments within you.

Kikuyu: Rũmia maathani makwa, na nĩũgatũũra muoyo; gitagĩra morutani makwa o ta kĩũma kĩa riitho rĩaku.
English: Keep my commandments and live! Guard my teaching as the apple of your eye.

Kikuyu: Mohagĩrĩre ciara-inĩ ciaku; ũmaandĩke kĩhengere-inĩ kĩa ngoro yaku.
English: Bind them on your fingers. Write them on the tablet of your heart.

Kikuyu: Ĩra ũũgĩ atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe mwarĩ wa maitũ,” naguo ũtaũku ũwĩtage mũndũ wa nyũmba yanyu;
English: Tell wisdom, “You are my sister.” Call understanding your relative,

Kikuyu: nĩcirĩkweheragia harĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio mũtharia, o na kuuma kũrĩ mũtumia ũcio ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku, hamwe na ciugo ciake cia kũheenanĩrĩria.
English: that they may keep you from the strange woman, from the foreigner who flatters with her words.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ ndirica-inĩ ya nyũmba yakwa-rĩ, nĩndacũthĩrĩirie na nja kamwanya-inĩ,
English: For at the window of my house, I looked out through my lattice.

Kikuyu: ngĩona kĩmwana gatagatĩ ka arĩa matarĩ oogĩ, na ngĩkũũrana gatagatĩ ka aanake, mũndũ mwĩthĩ ũtaarĩ na ũũgĩ wa gũkũũrana maũndũ.
English: I saw among the simple ones. I discerned among the youths a young man void of understanding,

Kikuyu: Aikũrũkaga na njĩra ĩrĩa yarĩ hakuhĩ na koine ya kwa mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio, agĩthiĩ na njĩra ĩyo yerekeire na gwake mũciĩ,
English: passing through the street near her corner, he went the way to her house,

Kikuyu: na kwarĩ na mairia o kahwaĩ-inĩ gũgĩtukatuka, gũkĩambĩrĩria kũgĩa nduma ya ũtukũ.
English: in the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the middle of the night and in the darkness.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hagĩũka mũndũ-wa-nja kũmũtũnga, wehumbĩte nguo ta mũmaraya, arĩ na wara ngoro-inĩ.
English: Behold, there a woman met him with the attire of a prostitute, and with crafty intent.

Kikuyu: (Nĩ mũnegeni na mũngʼathia, na magũrũ make matikiragĩrĩria mũciĩ;
English: She is loud and defiant. Her feet don’t stay in her house.

Kikuyu: rĩmwe arĩ njĩra-inĩ, na rĩrĩa rĩngĩ arĩ ihaaro-inĩ, ehithagĩre andũ koine-inĩ ciothe cia njĩra.)
English: Now she is in the streets, now in the squares, and lurking at every corner.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩnyiita mwanake ũcio, akĩmũmumunya, na akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ ategũconoka,
English: So she caught him, and kissed him. With an impudent face she said to him:

Kikuyu: “Ndĩ na maruta ma ngwatanĩro mũciĩ, tondũ ũmũthĩ nĩhingirie mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa.
English: “Sacrifices of peace offerings are with me. Today I have paid my vows.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndoimagara, ndooka gũgũtũnga; ngũgũcarĩtie mũno, na rĩu nĩndakuona!
English: Therefore I came out to meet you, to diligently seek your face, and I have found you.

Kikuyu: Nĩnjarĩte ũrĩrĩ wakwa na macuka ma gatani ma marangi kuuma Misiri.
English: I have spread my couch with carpets of tapestry, with striped cloths of the yarn of Egypt.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩrĩ ũcio wakwa nĩndĩũitĩrĩirie indo nungi wega ta manemane, na thubiri, o na mũndarathini.
English: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

Kikuyu: Ũka twĩhũũnie wendo nginya rũciinĩ; reke twĩkenie na wendo!
English: Come, let’s take our fill of loving until the morning. Let’s solace ourselves with loving.

Kikuyu: Mũthuuri wakwa ndarĩ mũciĩ; nĩathiĩte rũgendo rũraihu.
English: For my husband isn’t at home. He has gone on a long journey.

Kikuyu: Nĩathiire na mũhuko wake ũiyũrĩte mbeeca, na akainũka mũciĩ o rĩrĩa mweri ũkaiganana.”
English: He has taken a bag of money with him. He will come home at the full moon.”

Kikuyu: Agĩkĩmũheenereria na ciugo, akĩmũhĩtithia; agĩkĩmuuha na mĩario yake mĩnyoroku.
English: With persuasive words, she led him astray. With the flattering of her lips, she seduced him.

Kikuyu: O rĩmwe akĩmũrũmĩrĩra ta ndegwa ĩgĩthiĩ gũthĩnjwo, kana ta thwariga ĩgĩtoonya kĩana-inĩ kĩa mũtego,
English: He followed her immediately, as an ox goes to the slaughter, as a fool stepping into a noose.

Kikuyu: nginya akaaya gũtheecwo ini nĩ mĩguĩ, akahaana ta nyoni ĩguthũkĩte mũtego-inĩ, ĩtekũmenya o na hanini atĩ ũndũ ũcio no ũmĩrute muoyo.
English: Until an arrow strikes through his liver, as a bird hurries to the snare, and doesn’t know that it will cost his life.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ariũ akwa, ta thikĩrĩriai; tegai matũ mũigue ũrĩa ngũmwĩra.
English: Now therefore, sons, listen to me. Pay attention to the words of my mouth.

Kikuyu: Mũtikanareke ngoro cianyu ĩrũmĩrĩre mĩthiĩre yake, kana mũingĩre njĩra-inĩ ciake.
English: Don’t let your heart turn to her ways. Don’t go astray in her paths,

Kikuyu: Nĩ andũ aingĩ arĩkĩtie kũrũnda; arĩa oragĩte nĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩingĩ mũno.
English: for she has thrown down many wounded. Yes, all her slain are a mighty army.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba yake nĩ njĩra njariĩ ya gũthiĩ mbĩrĩra, nayo yerekagĩria andũ nyũmba-inĩ cia gĩkuũ. 8
English: Her house is the way to Sheol,† going down to the rooms of death. 8

Kikuyu: Githĩ ũũgĩ ndareetana? Githĩ ũtaũku ndaranĩrĩra na mũgambo wake?
English: Doesn’t wisdom cry out? Doesn’t understanding raise her voice?

Kikuyu: Njĩra-inĩ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, na magomano-inĩ ma njĩra-rĩ, nĩho ũũgĩ arũgamĩte;
English: On the top of high places by the way, where the paths meet, she stands.

Kikuyu: ningĩ hakuhĩ na ihingo iria cierekeire itũũra inene, o hau matoonyero-inĩ-rĩ, nĩho araanĩrĩra akoiga atĩrĩ:
English: Beside the gates, at the entry of the city, at the entry doors, she cries aloud:

Kikuyu: “Inyuĩ andũ nĩ inyuĩ ndĩreeta; ndĩraanĩrĩra na mũgambo wakwa kũrĩ andũ othe a thĩ.
English: “I call to you men! I send my voice to the sons of mankind.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ arĩa mũtarĩ na ũũgĩ, gĩai na ũbaarĩrĩri, inyuĩ arĩa akĩĩgu, gĩai na ũtaũku.
English: You simple, understand prudence! You fools, be of an understanding heart!

Kikuyu: Thikĩrĩriai, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ na maũndũ ma bata ngwaria; ngũtumũra mĩromo yakwa njarie maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire.
English: Hear, for I will speak excellent things. The opening of my lips is for right things.

Kikuyu: Kanua gakwa kaaragia ũhoro wa ma, nĩgũkorwo mĩromo yakwa nĩĩthũire waganu mũno ta kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi.
English: For my mouth speaks truth. Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

Kikuyu: Ciugo ciothe cia kanua gakwa nĩ cia ũthingu; gũtirĩ o na kiugo kĩmwe gĩacio kĩogomu kana gĩa kũhĩtithania.
English: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness. There is nothing crooked or perverse in them.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ arĩa makũũranaga maũndũ ciugo ciothe nĩciagĩrĩire; itirĩ mahĩtia kũrĩ arĩa marĩ na ũmenyo.
English: They are all plain to him who understands, right to those who find knowledge.

Kikuyu: Thuurai mataaro makwa handũ ha betha, mũthuure ũmenyo handũ ha thahabu ĩrĩa njega mũno,
English: Receive my instruction rather than silver, knowledge rather than choice gold.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ũũgĩ nĩ wa goro gũkĩra ruru iria ndune, na gũtirĩ kĩndũ ũngĩĩrirĩria kĩngĩringithanio naguo.
English: For wisdom is better than rubies. All the things that may be desired can’t be compared to it.

Kikuyu: “Niĩ ũũgĩ-rĩ, ndũũranagia na ũbaarĩrĩri; nĩndĩgwatĩire ũmenyo o na gũthugunda maũndũ mega.
English: “I, wisdom, have made prudence my dwelling. Find out knowledge and discretion.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova nĩgũthũũra ũũru; nĩthũire mwĩtĩĩo na mwĩtũũgĩrio, na mĩtugo mĩũru na mĩario ya waganu.
English: The fear of Yahweh is to hate evil. I hate pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the perverse mouth.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩra na ũũgĩ wa gũkũũrana maũndũ nĩ ciakwa; niĩ ndĩ na ũtaũku, na ndĩ na ũhoti.
English: Counsel and sound knowledge are mine. I have understanding and power.

Kikuyu: Athamaki mathamakaga nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wakwa nao aathani magathondeka mawatho ma kĩhooto;
English: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.

Kikuyu: anene mathamakaga nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wakwa, o ũndũ ũmwe na arĩa othe marĩ igweta arĩa maathanaga gũkũ thĩ.
English: By me princes rule, nobles, and all the righteous rulers of the earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩnyendete arĩa manyendete, nao arĩa manjaragia na kĩyo nĩmanyonaga.
English: I love those who love me. Those who seek me diligently will find me.

Kikuyu: Ũtonga na gĩtĩĩo cionekaga harĩ niĩ, o hamwe na ũtonga wa gũtũũra, na ũgaacĩru.
English: With me are riches, honor, enduring wealth, and prosperity.

Kikuyu: Maciaro makwa nĩ mega gũkĩra thahabu ĩrĩa therie; uumithio wakwa nĩũkĩrĩte betha ĩrĩa njega.
English: My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold, my yield than choice silver.

Kikuyu: Niĩ thiiaga na njĩra ya ũthingu, ngathiĩra o njĩra-inĩ cia kĩhooto,
English: I walk in the way of righteousness, in the middle of the paths of justice,

Kikuyu: ngĩheaga andũ arĩa manyendete ũtonga, na ngaiyũragia igĩĩna ciao.
English: that I may give wealth to those who love me. I fill their treasuries.

Kikuyu: “Jehova aanyũmbire o kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ kĩa wĩra wake, mbere ya mawĩra make ma tene;
English: “Yahweh possessed me in the beginning of his work, before his deeds of old.

Kikuyu: ndaathuurirwo kuuma tene wa tene, kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria, thĩ ĩtarĩ yagĩa.
English: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, before the earth existed.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa gũtaagĩte maria manene-rĩ, nĩguo ndaciarirwo, o hĩndĩ ĩrĩa gũtaarĩ matherũkĩro maiyũrĩte maaĩ;
English: When there were no depths, I was born, when there were no springs abounding with water.

Kikuyu: ndaciarirwo irĩma itanahaandwo harĩa irĩ, na tũrĩma tũtanagĩa,
English: Before the mountains were settled in place, before the hills, I was born;

Kikuyu: we atanoomba thĩ kana ithaka ciayo, o na kana rũkũngũ o ruothe rwa thĩ.
English: while as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the beginning of the dust of the world.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndaarĩ o kuo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aathondekire igũrũ na akĩrĩiga handũ ha rĩo, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa eekĩrire rũkiriri rwa kũrĩa kũriku,
English: When he established the heavens, I was there. When he set a circle on the surface of the deep,

Kikuyu: rĩrĩa aahaandire matu kũu igũrũ, na akĩrũmia ithima cia kũrĩa kũriku wega,
English: when he established the clouds above, when the springs of the deep became strong,

Kikuyu: rĩrĩa eekĩrire mũhaka wa iria nĩguo maaĩ matikanae kwagarara watho wake, na rĩrĩa aahandire mĩthingi ya thĩ.
English: when he gave to the sea its boundary, that the waters should not violate his commandment, when he marked out the foundations of the earth,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndaarĩ o hakuhĩ nake ndĩ ta mũbundi mũũgĩ. Ndaaiyũrĩtwo nĩ gĩkeno mũthenya o mũthenya, na ngakenaga hĩndĩ ciothe ndĩ mbere yake,
English: then I was the craftsman by his side. I was a delight day by day, always rejoicing before him,

Kikuyu: ngakenagĩra thĩ yake yothe, na ngakenagio nĩ andũ othe a thĩ.
English: rejoicing in his whole world. My delight was with the sons of men.

Kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ ariũ akwa, ta thikĩrĩriai; kũrathimwo nĩ arĩa marũmagia njĩra ciakwa.
English: “Now therefore, my sons, listen to me, for blessed are those who keep my ways.

Kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩriai mataaro makwa nĩguo mũhĩge; mũtikamarege.
English: Hear instruction, and be wise. Don’t refuse it.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũnjiguaga, agacũthagĩrĩria mĩrango-inĩ yakwa o mũthenya, akanjetagĩrĩra hingĩro-inĩ cia mĩrango yakwa.
English: Blessed is the man who hears me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at my door posts.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe ũgĩte na niĩ nĩ muoyo agĩte naguo, nake akagĩa na agetĩkĩrĩka harĩ Jehova.
English: For whoever finds me finds life, and will obtain favor from Yahweh.

Kikuyu: No ũrĩa wothe ũhĩtanĩtie na niĩ nĩ ũũru eĩkaga; nao arĩa othe maathũire nĩ gĩkuũ mendete.” 9
English: But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul. All those who hate me love death.” 9

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩakĩte nyũmba yake; nĩaicũhĩtie itugĩ ciayo mũgwanja.
English: Wisdom has built her house. She has carved out her seven pillars.

Kikuyu: Nĩahaarĩirie nyama, na akahaarĩria ndibei,* o na akaara metha yake.
English: She has prepared her meat. She has mixed her wine. She has also set her table.

Kikuyu: Nĩatũmĩte ndungata ciake cia airĩtu nja, na nĩaretana arĩ harĩa hatũũgĩru mũno itũũra-inĩ rĩrĩa inene, akoiga atĩrĩ:
English: She has sent out her maidens. She cries from the highest places of the city:

Kikuyu: “Andũ arĩa othe matarĩ na ũũgĩ nĩmoke gũkũ!” Ningĩ akeera andũ arĩa maagĩĩte ũtaũku atĩrĩ,
English: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” As for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,

Kikuyu: Ũkai mũrĩe irio ciakwa, na mũnyue ndibei ĩrĩa ndĩmũthondekeire.
English: “Come, eat some of my bread, Drink some of the wine which I have mixed!

Kikuyu: Tiganai na mĩthiĩre yanyu ĩtarĩ ya ũũgĩ, na nĩmũgũtũũra muoyo; cookai gũthiiaga na njĩra ya ũtaũku.
English: Leave your simple ways, and live. Walk in the way of understanding.”

Kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe ũtaaraga mũnyũrũrania eĩtagĩria irumi; na ũrĩa wothe ũkaanagia mũndũ mwaganu egĩĩragĩra njono.
English: One who corrects a mocker invites insult. One who reproves a wicked man invites abuse.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanataare mũnyũrũrania, nĩguo ndagagũthũũre; kaania mũndũ mũũgĩ, nake nĩegũkwenda.
English: Don’t reprove a scoffer, lest he hate you. Reprove a wise person, and he will love you.

Kikuyu: Taara mũndũ mũũgĩ na nĩegũkĩrĩrĩria kũũhĩga; mũndũ mũthingu mũrute, na nĩekuongerera ũũgĩ wake.
English: Instruct a wise person, and he will be still wiser. Teach a righteous person, and he will increase in learning.

Kikuyu: “Kĩambĩrĩria kĩa ũũgĩ nĩ gwĩtigĩra Jehova, na kũmenya Ũrĩa Mũtheru nĩkuo gũtaũkĩrwo.
English: The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom. The knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩ ũndũ wakwa matukũ maku nĩmakaingĩha, na wongererwo mĩaka ya gũtũũra muoyo.
English: For by me your days will be multiplied. The years of your life will be increased.

Kikuyu: Ũngĩkorwo ũrĩ mũũgĩ-rĩ, ũũgĩ waku nĩũgakũguna; na ũngĩkorwo ũrĩ mũnyũrũrania-rĩ, we nowe ũkaanyariirĩka.”
English: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself. If you mock, you alone will bear it.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩa wĩtagwo Mũkĩĩgu nĩ wa inegene; we ndarĩ mũtugo, na ndarĩ ũmenyo.
English: The foolish woman is loud, undisciplined, and knows nothing.

Kikuyu: Aikaraga thĩ mũrango-inĩ wa nyũmba yake, agaikarĩra gĩtĩ harĩa hatũũgĩru mũno itũũra-inĩ rĩrĩa inene,
English: She sits at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,

Kikuyu: ageetaga arĩa marehĩtũkĩra, arĩa marethiĩra na njĩra ciao.
English: to call to those who pass by, who go straight on their ways,

Kikuyu: Ameeraga atĩrĩ, “Andũ arĩa othe matarĩ na ũũgĩ nĩmoke gũkũ!” Nao arĩa maagĩĩte ũtaũku akameera atĩrĩ:
English: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here.” As for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,

Kikuyu: “Maaĩ ma kũiya marĩ mũrĩo; o na irio iria irĩĩagĩrwo hitho-inĩ irĩ cama!”
English: “Stolen water is sweet. Food eaten in secret is pleasant.”

Kikuyu: No andũ acio matikoragwo makĩmenya atĩ arĩa akuũ marĩ kũu, na atĩ ageni ake marĩ o kũrĩa kũriku mbĩrĩra-inĩ. 10 Ici nĩ Thimo cia Solomoni:
English: But he doesn’t know that the departed spirits are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

Kikuyu: Mwana mũũgĩ atũmaga ithe akene, no mwana mũkĩĩgu atũũragia nyina na kĩeha.
English: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a glad father; but a foolish son brings grief to his mother.

Kikuyu: Mĩthiithũ yumanĩte na ungumania ndĩrĩ kĩguni, no ũthingu ũkũũraga mũndũ kuuma kũrĩ gĩkuũ.
English: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing, but righteousness delivers from death.

Kikuyu: Jehova ndarekagĩrĩria mũndũ mũthingu ahũte, no nĩamarigagĩrĩria merirĩria ma mũndũ mwaganu.
English: Yahweh will not allow the soul of the righteous to go hungry, but he thrusts away the desire of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Moko ma kĩgũũta matũmaga mũndũ athĩĩne, no moko ma mũndũ ũrĩ kĩyo marehaga ũtonga.
English: He becomes poor who works with a lazy hand, but the hand of the diligent brings wealth.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũcookanagĩrĩria irio hĩndĩ ya riũa nĩ mwana mũũgĩ, no ũrĩa ũkomaga hĩndĩ ya magetha nĩ mwana wa kũrehe thoni.
English: He who gathers in summer is a wise son, but he who sleeps during the harvest is a son who causes shame.

Kikuyu: Mũtwe wa mũndũ mũthingu ũikaraga ũhumbĩrĩtwo nĩ irathimo, no kanua ka mũndũ mwaganu kaiyũraga ngũĩ.
English: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Kĩririkano kĩa mũndũ mũthingu gĩgatuĩka kĩrathimo, no rĩĩtwa rĩa mũndũ mwaganu nĩrĩkabutha.
English: The memory of the righteous is blessed, but the name of the wicked will rot.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na ũũgĩ wa ngoro nĩetĩkagĩra mawatho, no mũndũ wa kũhũhũtĩka nĩakanangĩka.
English: The wise in heart accept commandments, but a chattering fool will fall.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa mĩthiĩre mĩrũngĩrĩru athiiaga ategwĩtigĩra ũgwati, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ mĩthiĩre mĩogomu nĩakamenyeka.
English: He who walks blamelessly walks surely, but he who perverts his ways will be found out.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa uunagĩra ũngĩ riitho na rũnano nĩarehage kĩeha, na mwaria mũkĩĩgu nĩakanangĩka.
English: One who winks with the eye causes sorrow, but a chattering fool will fall.

Kikuyu: Kanua ka mũndũ mũthingu nĩ gĩthima kĩa muoyo, no kanua ka mũndũ mwaganu kaiyũraga ngũĩ.
English: The mouth of the righteous is a spring of life, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Rũmena rũrehaga nyamũkano, no wendani ũhumbagĩra ũũru wothe.
English: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all wrongs.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ ũkoragwo mĩromo-inĩ ya mũndũ ũrĩa mũkũũrani maũndũ, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũtooĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ aagĩrĩirwo nĩ rũthanju ciande-inĩ ciake.
English: Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has discernment, but a rod is for the back of him who is void of understanding.

Kikuyu: Andũ oogĩ meigagĩra ũmenyo, no kanua ga kĩrimũ keĩtagĩria mwanangĩko.
English: Wise men lay up knowledge, but the mouth of the foolish is near ruin.

Kikuyu: Indo cia itonga nĩ gĩtũũro kĩao kĩirigĩre na hinya, no ũkĩa nĩguo mwanangĩko wa andũ arĩa athĩĩni.
English: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city. The destruction of the poor is their poverty.

Kikuyu: Mũcaara wa andũ arĩa athingu ũmarehagĩra muoyo, no indo iria cionagwo nĩ andũ arĩa aaganu imarehagĩra iherithia.
English: The labor of the righteous leads to life. The increase of the wicked leads to sin.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguaga agĩtaarwo arĩ njĩra-inĩ ya muoyo, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũregaga kũrũithio-rĩ, nĩahĩtithagia andũ arĩa angĩ.
English: He is in the way of life who heeds correction, but he who forsakes reproof leads others astray.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhithaga rũmena rwake aaragia na mĩromo ya maheeni, na ũrĩa wothe ũcambanagia nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
English: He who hides hatred has lying lips. He who utters a slander is a fool.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ciugo ciaingĩha mehia matiagaga, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũkiraga nĩ mũũgĩ.
English: In the multitude of words there is no lack of disobedience, but he who restrains his lips does wisely.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwa mũndũ mũthingu nĩ betha njega, no ngoro ya mũndũ mwaganu ndĩrĩ bata.
English: The tongue of the righteous is like choice silver. The heart of the wicked is of little worth.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ya arĩa athingu ĩhũnagia andũ aingĩ, no andũ akĩĩgu makuaga nĩ kwaga gũkũũrana maũndũ.
English: The lips of the righteous feed many, but the foolish die for lack of understanding.

Kikuyu: Kĩrathimo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩo kĩrehaga ũtonga, na ndarĩ kĩeha onganagĩrĩria nakĩo.
English: Yahweh’s blessing brings wealth, and he adds no trouble to it.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu akenagĩra mĩtugo mĩũru, no mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku akenagĩra ũũgĩ.
English: It is a fool’s pleasure to do wickedness, but wisdom is a man of understanding’s pleasure.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩa mũndũ mwaganu etigagĩra nĩkĩo gĩkaamũkora, na kĩrĩa mũndũ mũthingu eriragĩria nĩkĩo akaaheo.
English: What the wicked fear will overtake them, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.

Kikuyu: Kĩhuhũkanio kĩahĩtũka andũ arĩa aaganu maticookaga kuoneka, no andũ arĩa athingu mehaandaga wega nginya tene.
English: When the whirlwind passes, the wicked is no more; but the righteous stand firm forever.

Kikuyu: Ta ũrĩa thiki ĩgagatagĩra magego, na ndogo ĩkarũrĩra maitho-rĩ, noguo kĩgũũta kĩhaana kũrĩ arĩa magĩtũmaga.
English: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova-rĩ, kuongagĩrĩra mũndũ matukũ ma gũtũũra muoyo, no mĩaka ya mũndũ mwaganu nĩkũnyiihio ĩnyiihagio.
English: The fear of Yahweh prolongs days, but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.

Kikuyu: Itanya rĩa mũndũ mũthingu nĩ gĩkeno, no kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩa mũndũ mwaganu no gĩa tũhũ.
English: The prospect of the righteous is joy, but the hope of the wicked will perish.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ya Jehova nĩ rĩũrĩro rĩa andũ arĩa athingu, no nĩ mwanangĩko harĩ arĩa mekaga ũũru.
English: The way of Yahweh is a stronghold to the upright, but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu ndarĩ hĩndĩ akaamunywo, no ũrĩa mwaganu ndagatũũra thĩinĩ wa bũrũri.
English: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not dwell in the land.

Kikuyu: Kanua ka mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu karehaga ũũgĩ, no rũrĩmĩ rwa ũtũrĩka nĩrũkaniinwo.
English: The mouth of the righteous produces wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ya mũndũ mũthingu nĩyũũĩ kwaria ndeto iria ciagĩrĩire, no kanua ka ũrĩa mwaganu kaaragia o rũtũrĩko. 11
English: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked is perverse. 11

Kikuyu: Ratiri itarĩ cia ma nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no nĩakenagĩra mahiga ma gũthima marĩa maigananu.
English: A false balance is an abomination to Yahweh, but accurate weights are his delight.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mwĩtĩĩo woka, ũrũmagĩrĩrwo nĩ thoni, no kwĩnyiihia kũrehaga ũũgĩ.
English: When pride comes, then comes shame, but with humility comes wisdom.

Kikuyu: Ũrũngĩrĩru wa arĩa marũgamĩte wega nĩũmonagĩrĩria njĩra, no andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku, nĩmanangagwo nĩ ũhinga wao.
English: The integrity of the upright shall guide them, but the perverseness of the treacherous shall destroy them.

Kikuyu: Ũtonga ndũrĩ kĩguni mũthenya wa mangʼũrĩ, no ũthingu nĩũkũũraga mũndũ kuuma kũrĩ gĩkuũ.
English: Riches don’t profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu wa andũ arĩa matarĩ na ũcuuke ũmathondekagĩra njĩra nũngarũ, no andũ arĩa aaganu magũithagio nĩ waganu wao ene.
English: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way, but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu wa andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩũmahonokagia, no andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku nĩmategagwo nĩ merirĩria mao mooru.
English: The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them, but the unfaithful will be trapped by evil desires.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũndũ mwaganu aakua, kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩake nĩgũthira gĩthiraga; kĩrĩa gĩothe eerĩgĩrĩire kuuma kũrĩ ũhoti wake gĩtuĩkaga o tũhũ.
English: When a wicked man dies, hope perishes, and expectation of power comes to nothing.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu nĩahonokagio kuuma thĩĩna-inĩ, naguo ũgaacookerera ũrĩa mwaganu.
English: A righteous person is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked takes his place.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtetigĩrĩte Ngai nĩanangaga mũndũ wa itũũra na kanua gake, no arĩa athingu nĩmetharaga nĩ ũndũ wa ũmenyo.
English: With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor, but the righteous will be delivered through knowledge.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ arĩa athingu magaacĩra, itũũra nĩgũkena rĩkenaga; rĩrĩa andũ arĩa aaganu maathira, nĩkũrũhio kũrũũhagio.
English: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices. When the wicked perish, there is shouting.

Kikuyu: Itũũra inene nĩrĩtũũgagĩrio nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrathimo kĩa andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru, no nĩrĩanangagwo nĩ kanua ka andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: By the blessing of the upright, the city is exalted, but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtarĩ ũũgĩ wa gũkũũrana maũndũ anyũrũragia mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake, no mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku nĩ gwĩkirĩra ekiragĩra.
English: One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding holds his peace.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩoimagia ndeto cia ndundu nja, no mũndũ mwĩhokeku nĩaigaga hitho.
English: One who brings gossip betrays a confidence, but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.

Kikuyu: Bũrũri ũngĩaga ũtongoria mwega nĩkũgũa ũgũũaga, no ataarani aingĩ matũmaga kũgĩe na ũhootani kũna.
English: Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls, but in the multitude of counselors there is victory.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũgamagĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ ũngĩ ti-itherũ nĩagathĩĩnĩka, no mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wagaga kũrũgamĩrĩra mũndũ thiirĩ atũũraga na thayũ.
English: He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it, but he who refuses pledges of collateral is secure.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja mũtaana nĩegĩagĩra gĩtĩĩo, no arũme arĩa matarĩ tha no ũtonga magĩaga naguo.
English: A gracious woman obtains honor, but violent men obtain riches.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtaana egunaga we mwene, no mũndũ ũtarĩ tha erehagĩra thĩĩna we mwene.
English: The merciful man does good to his own soul, but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu athũkũmaga mũcaara wa kũmũheenia, no ũrĩa ũhaandaga ũthingu nĩagethaga irĩhi rĩa ma.
English: Wicked people earn deceitful wages, but one who sows righteousness reaps a sure reward.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu kũna nĩagatũũra muoyo, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmagĩrĩra waganu nĩ gũkua agaakua.
English: He who is truly righteous gets life. He who pursues evil gets death.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa marĩ ngoro ya rũtũrĩko nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no nĩakenagio nĩ andũ arĩa njĩra ciao itarĩ ũcuuke.
English: Those who are perverse in heart are an abomination to Yahweh, but those whose ways are blameless are his delight.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ mũndũ mwaganu ndakaaga kũherithio, no andũ arĩa athingu nĩmakahonokio.
English: Most certainly, the evil man will not be unpunished, but the offspring† of the righteous will be delivered.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja mũthaka ũtarĩ ũgereri ahaana ta gĩcũhĩ gĩa thahabu kĩrĩ iniũrũ-inĩ rĩa ngũrwe.
English: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout, is a beautiful woman who lacks discretion.

Kikuyu: Wendi wa mũndũ mũthingu ũrĩkagĩrĩria o wega-inĩ, no kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩa mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu kĩrĩĩkagĩrĩria mangʼũrĩ-inĩ.
English: The desire of the righteous is only good. The expectation of the wicked is wrath.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ mũndũ ũheanaga na ũtaana, nake agakĩrĩrĩria kũgĩa na indo; ũngĩ atuĩkaga mũkarĩ mũno, no agathiĩ o na mbere gũthĩĩna.
English: There is one who scatters, and increases yet more. There is one who withholds more than is appropriate, but gains poverty.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtaana nĩakagaacĩra; ũrĩa ũnyootoraga arĩa angĩ o nake mwene nĩakanyootorwo.
English: The liberal soul shall be made fat. He who waters shall be watered also himself.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũregaga kwenderia andũ mbembe iria aigĩte nĩarumagwo nĩ andũ, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũciendagia, kĩrathimo kĩrĩkoragwo mũtwe-inĩ wake.
English: People curse someone who withholds grain, but blessing will be on the head of him who sells it.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũcaragia maũndũ mega arĩonaga o wega, no ũrĩa ũcaragia maũndũ mooru no mo marĩmũkoraga.
English: He who diligently seeks good seeks favor, but he who searches after evil, it shall come to him.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wĩhokaga ũtonga wake nĩkũgũa akaagũa, no arĩa athingu makaagaacĩra ta ithangũ rĩrĩa iruru.
English: He who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous shall flourish as the green leaf.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũnyamaragia mũciĩ wake akaagaya o rũhuho, nake mũndũ mũkĩĩgu agaatuĩka ndungata ya ũrĩa mũũgĩ.
English: He who troubles his own house shall inherit the wind. The foolish shall be servant to the wise of heart.

Kikuyu: Maciaro ma andũ arĩa athingu nĩ mũtĩ wa muoyo, nake mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ nĩaguucagĩrĩria ngoro cia andũ.
English: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life. He who is wise wins souls.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo andũ arĩa athingu nĩmarĩhagwo marĩ gũkũ thĩ-rĩ, githĩ arĩa matetigĩrĩte Ngai na arĩa ehia matikarĩhwo makĩria! 12
English: Behold, the righteous shall be repaid in the earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner! 12

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wendete ũtaaro nĩendete ũmenyi, no ũrĩa ũthũire gũkaanio nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
English: Whoever loves correction loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwega nĩatugagwo nĩ Jehova, no mũndũ mwara Jehova amũtuaga mũndũ mwĩhia.
English: A good man shall obtain favor from Yahweh, but he will condemn a man of wicked plans.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ndangĩhota kwĩhaanda na maũndũ ma waganu, no ũrĩa mũthingu ndangĩenyenyeka.
English: A man shall not be established by wickedness, but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ũrĩa ngatha nĩwe thũmbĩ ya mũthuuri wake, no mũtumia ũconorithagia mũthuuri wake atariĩ ta ũbuthu mahĩndĩ-inĩ make.
English: A worthy woman is the crown of her husband, but a disgraceful wife is as rottenness in his bones.

Kikuyu: Mĩbango ya andũ arĩa athingu nĩ ya kĩhooto, no ũtaaro wa andũ arĩa aaganu nĩ wa maheeni.
English: The thoughts of the righteous are just, but the advice of the wicked is deceitful.

Kikuyu: Ndeto cia andũ arĩa aaganu nĩ cia kuohia andũ mamaite thakame, no mĩario ya andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩĩmahonokagia.
English: The words of the wicked are about lying in wait for blood, but the speech of the upright rescues them.

Kikuyu: Andũ aaganu mangʼaũranagio magathira biũ, no nyũmba ya mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu ĩtũũraga ĩrĩ nũmu.
English: The wicked are overthrown, and are no more, but the house of the righteous shall stand.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ agaathagĩrĩrio kũringana na ũũgĩ wake, no andũ marĩ meciiria mogomu nĩmamenagwo.
English: A man shall be commended according to his wisdom, but he who has a warped mind shall be despised.

Kikuyu: Kaba mũndũ ũrĩa wonagwo ta atarĩ bata no nĩ arĩ ndungata, gũkĩra ũrĩa wĩtuaga atĩ nĩ mũndũ wa bata, na ndarĩ gĩa kũrĩa.
English: Better is he who is little known, and has a servant, than he who honors himself and lacks bread.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthingu nĩamenyagĩrĩra muoyo wa nyamũ yake, no maũndũ marĩa ma tha ma ũrĩa mwaganu nĩ ma kũnyariirana.
English: A righteous man respects the life of his animal, but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩmaga mũgũnda wake nĩarĩkoragwo na irio nyingĩ, no ũrĩa ũhanyũkanagia na maũndũ ma tũhũ nĩagĩte gũkũũrana maũndũ.
English: He who tills his land shall have plenty of bread, but he who chases fantasies is void of understanding.

Kikuyu: Arĩa aaganu meriragĩria indo iria itahĩtwo nĩ andũ arĩa ooru, no mĩri ya arĩa athingu nĩĩgaacagĩra.
English: The wicked desires the plunder of evil men, but the root of the righteous flourishes.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũru ategagwo nĩ mĩario ya wĩhia wake, no mũndũ mũthingu nĩahonokaga kuuma thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: An evil man is trapped by sinfulness of lips, but the righteous shall come out of trouble.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩaiyũragwo nĩ maciaro mega ma kanua gake, ti-itherũ, o ta ũrĩa agunagwo nĩ wĩra wa moko make.
English: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth. The work of a man’s hands shall be rewarded to him.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ya mũndũ mũkĩĩgu yonekaga yagĩrĩire harĩ we, no mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩathikagĩrĩria mataaro.
English: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but he who is wise listens to counsel.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu onanagia marakara make o rĩmwe, no mũndũ mũbaarĩrĩri aarumwo ndatindanagĩrĩra.
English: A fool shows his annoyance the same day, but one who overlooks an insult is prudent.

Kikuyu: Mũira wa ma arutaga o ũira wa ma, no mũira ũtarĩ wa ma aaragia maheeni.
English: He who is truthful testifies honestly, but a false witness lies.

Kikuyu: Ciugo iteciirĩtio itheecaga ta rũhiũ rwa njora, no rũrĩmĩ rwa mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ nĩkũhonia rũhonagia.
English: There is one who speaks rashly like the piercing of a sword, but the tongue of the wise heals.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia ma ĩtũũraga nginya tene, no rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni nĩ rwa kahinda o kanini.
English: Truth’s lips will be established forever, but a lying tongue is only momentary.

Kikuyu: Ngoro cia arĩa mathugundaga gwĩka ũũru ciyũrĩtwo nĩ maheeni, no gĩkeno nĩ kĩa andũ arĩa matũmaga thayũ ũgaacĩre.
English: Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but joy comes to the promoters of peace.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũũru ũngĩkora andũ arĩa athingu, no arĩa aaganu maiyũrĩtwo nĩ thĩĩna.
English: No mischief shall happen to the righteous, but the wicked shall be filled with evil.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ĩrĩa ĩheenanagia nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no nĩakenagio nĩ andũ arĩa maaragia ma.
English: Lying lips are an abomination to Yahweh, but those who do the truth are his delight.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũbaarĩrĩri nĩahithaga ũmenyi wake, no ngoro cia andũ arĩa akĩĩgu ĩmemerekagia ũrimũ.
English: A prudent man keeps his knowledge, but the hearts of fools proclaim foolishness.

Kikuyu: Moko ma mũndũ ũrĩ kĩyo nĩmo magaathana, no kĩgũũta gĩkaarutithio wĩra wa ũkombo.
English: The hands of the diligent ones shall rule, but laziness ends in slave labor.

Kikuyu: Ũritũ wa ngoro nĩũhatagĩrĩria mũndũ, no ciugo njega nĩitũmaga akene.
English: Anxiety in a man’s heart weighs it down, but a kind word makes it glad.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthingu nĩataaraga arata ake, no njĩra ya arĩa aaganu nĩ kũmahĩtithia ĩmahĩtithagia.
English: A righteous person is cautious in friendship, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ kĩgũũta o na ndahĩhagia nyama cia ũguĩmi wake, no mũndũ ũrĩ kĩyo onaga indo ciake irĩ cia goro.
English: The slothful man doesn’t roast his game, but the possessions of diligent men are prized.

Kikuyu: Muoyo wonekaga na njĩra ya ũthingu; na njĩra-inĩ ĩyo-rĩ, kũrĩ muoyo ũtagaathira. 13
English: In the way of righteousness is life; in its path there is no death. 13

Kikuyu: Mwana mũũgĩ nĩathikagĩrĩria ũrutani wa ithe, no mũnyũrũrania ndathikagĩrĩria agĩkaanio.
English: A wise son listens to his father’s instruction, but a scoffer doesn’t listen to rebuke.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩakenagĩra maciaro mega ma kuuma kanua gake, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku eeriragĩria haaro.
English: By the fruit of his lips, a man enjoys good things, but the unfaithful crave violence.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũmenyagĩrĩra kanua gake nĩ muoyo wake amenyagĩrĩra, no ũrĩa ũhiũhaga kwaria nĩakanangĩka.
English: He who guards his mouth guards his soul. One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ya kĩgũũta ĩĩriragĩria na ndĩone kĩndũ, no merirĩria ma mũndũ ũrĩ kĩyo nĩmakahingio.
English: The soul of the sluggard desires, and has nothing, but the desire of the diligent shall be fully satisfied.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthingu nĩathũire ũhoro ũtarĩ wa ma, no mũndũ mwaganu ekaga maũndũ ma thoni na ma njono.
English: A righteous man hates lies, but a wicked man brings shame and disgrace.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu ũgitagĩra mũndũ ũrĩa mũrũngĩrĩru, no waganu nĩũngʼaũranagia mũndũ mwĩhia.
English: Righteousness guards the way of integrity, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ mũndũ wĩtuaga gĩtonga, no ndarĩ kĩndũ; kũrĩ ũngĩ wĩtuaga mũthĩĩni, no arĩ na ũtonga mũnene.
English: There are some who pretend to be rich, yet have nothing. There are some who pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.

Kikuyu: Ũtonga wa mũndũ wahota gũkũũra muoyo wake, no mũndũ mũthĩĩni ndaiguaga akĩĩhĩtĩrwo.
English: The ransom of a man’s life is his riches, but the poor hear no threats.

Kikuyu: Ũtheri wa mũndũ mũthingu waaraga ũgacangarara, no tawa wa mũndũ mwaganu nĩkũhorio ũhoragio.
English: The light of the righteous shines brightly, but the lamp of the wicked is snuffed out.

Kikuyu: Mwĩtĩĩo ũciaraga njũgitano, no ũũgĩ wonekaga harĩ arĩa metĩkagĩra gũtaarwo.
English: Pride only breeds quarrels, but wisdom is with people who take advice.

Kikuyu: Mbeeca itarĩ cia wĩhokeku ithiiaga igĩthiraga, no ũrĩa ũcookanagĩrĩria mbeeca tũnini o tũnini nĩatũmaga ciongerereke.
English: Wealth gained dishonestly dwindles away, but he who gathers by hand makes it grow.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ mũtaanye ũngĩikario mũno nĩũrwaragia mũndũ ngoro, no kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩhingĩtio gĩtuĩkaga ta mũtĩ wa muoyo.
English: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but when longing is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũthũire ũrutani nĩeyũnũhaga, no ũrĩa ũtĩĩaga maathani nĩakaheo kĩheo.
English: Whoever despises instruction will pay for it, but he who respects a command will be rewarded.

Kikuyu: Ũrutani wa mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ nĩ gĩthima kĩa muoyo, ũhũndũraga mũndũ kuuma mĩtego-inĩ ya gĩkuũ.
English: The teaching of the wise is a spring of life, to turn from the snares of death.

Kikuyu: Gũtaũkĩrwo wega nĩ maũndũ gũtũmaga mũndũ endeke, no njĩra ya arĩa matarĩ ehokeku ti hũthũ.
English: Good understanding wins favor, but the way of the unfaithful is hard.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe mwĩbaarĩrĩri ekaga maũndũ make na ũmenyi, no mũndũ mũkĩĩgu aguũragia ũrimũ wake.
English: Every prudent man acts from knowledge, but a fool exposes folly.

Kikuyu: Mũtũmwo mwaganu atoonyaga thĩĩna-inĩ, no mũtũmwo mwĩhokeku nĩarehaga kĩhonia.
English: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a trustworthy envoy gains healing.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũregaga gũtaarwo anyiitagwo nĩ thĩĩna na thoni, no mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkagĩra gũkaanio atuĩkaga wa gũtĩĩo.
English: Poverty and shame come to him who refuses discipline, but he who heeds correction shall be honored.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ mwĩrirĩrie wakinyanĩra ũiguithagia ngoro wega, no andũ akĩĩgu monaga kũhutatĩra ũũru ta ũndũ ũrĩ magigi.
English: Longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools detest turning from evil.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũceeraga na arĩa oogĩ akĩragĩrĩria kũũhĩga, no ũrĩa ũtwaranaga na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu onaga o ũũru.
English: One who walks with wise men grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.

Kikuyu: Mũtino ũthingataga mũndũ ũrĩa mwĩhia, no ũgaacĩru nĩ kĩheo kĩa arĩa athingu.
English: Misfortune pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwega atigagĩra ciana cia ciana ciake igai, no ũtonga wa mũndũ mwĩhia ũigagĩrwo andũ arĩa athingu.
English: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored for the righteous.

Kikuyu: Mũgũnda wa mũndũ mũthĩĩni no ũciare irio nyingĩ, no kwaga kĩhooto gũciniinaga ciothe.
English: An abundance of food is in poor people’s fields, but injustice sweeps it away.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũigaga rũthanju thĩ nĩ mwana wake athũire, no ũrĩa wendete mwana wake nĩamũherithagia.
English: One who spares the rod hates his son, but one who loves him is careful to discipline him.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu marĩĩaga makahũũna, no nda ya mũndũ mwaganu ĩikaraga o ĩhũtiĩ. 14
English: The righteous one eats to the satisfying of his soul, but the belly of the wicked goes hungry. 14

Kikuyu: Mũtumia mũũgĩ nĩ gwaka akaga nyũmba yake, no mũtumia mũkĩĩgu nĩgũtharia atharagia nyũmba yake na moko make mwene.
English: Every wise woman builds her house, but the foolish one tears it down with her own hands.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũthiiaga na mĩthiĩre mĩrũngĩrĩru nĩwe mwĩtigĩri Jehova, no ũrĩa mĩthiĩre yake ĩrĩ mĩhĩtanu nĩamũmenete.
English: He who walks in his uprightness fears Yahweh, but he who is perverse in his ways despises him.

Kikuyu: Mĩario ya mũndũ mũkĩĩgu nĩ rũthanju rwa kũmũhũũra, no mĩromo ya arĩa oogĩ nĩĩmagitagĩra.
English: The fool’s talk brings a rod to his back, but the lips of the wise protect them.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩa gũtarĩ ndegwa mĩharatĩ ĩkoragwo ĩrĩ mĩtheru, no maciaro maingĩ moimanaga na hinya wa ndegwa.
English: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

Kikuyu: Mũira mwĩhokeku ndaheenanagia, no mũira wa maheeni aaragia o maheeni.
English: A truthful witness will not lie, but a false witness pours out lies.

Kikuyu: Mũnyũrũrania acaragia ũũgĩ na ndangĩwona, no kũgĩa na ũmenyo nĩ ũhũthũ kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkũũranaga maũndũ.
English: A scoffer seeks wisdom, and doesn’t find it, but knowledge comes easily to a discerning person.

Kikuyu: Ikara kũraya na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, nĩgũkorwo ndũrĩ ũmenyo ũngĩona kuuma mĩromo-inĩ yake.
English: Stay away from a foolish man, for you won’t find knowledge on his lips.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ wa arĩa abaarĩrĩri nĩũtũmaga mamenye ũhoro wa njĩra ciao, no ũrimũ wa andũ arĩa akĩĩgu nĩũmahĩtithagia.
English: The wisdom of the prudent is to think about his way, but the folly of fools is deceit.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa akĩĩgu manyũrũragia horohio ya mehia, no ũtugi wonekaga harĩ arĩa arũngĩrĩru.
English: Fools mock at making atonement for sins, but among the upright there is good will.

Kikuyu: Ngoro o yothe nĩyũũĩ ũrũrũ wayo, o na gĩkeno-inĩ kĩayo gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩnyiitanĩra nayo.
English: The heart knows its own bitterness and joy; he will not share these with a stranger.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba ya mũndũ mwaganu nĩĩkanangwo, no hema ya mũndũ mũrũngĩrĩru nĩĩkagaacĩra.
English: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ njĩra mũndũ onaga ta yagĩrĩire, no marigĩrĩrio mayo yerekagĩria mũndũ o gĩkuũ-inĩ.
English: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

Kikuyu: O na hĩndĩ ya gũtheka ngoro no ĩkorwo ĩgĩtuura, na gĩkeno no kĩrigĩrĩrie na kĩeha.
English: Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful, and mirth may end in heaviness.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku nĩmakarĩhwo kũna nĩ ũndũ wa mĩthiĩre yao, nake mũndũ mwega aheo kĩheo kũringana na mĩthiĩre yake.
English: The unfaithful will be repaid for his own ways; likewise a good man will be rewarded for his ways.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtarĩ ũũgĩ etĩkagia ũndũ o wothe, no mũndũ mũbaarĩrĩri nĩacũũranagia ũhoro wa makinya make.
English: A simple man believes everything, but the prudent man carefully considers his ways.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩetigagĩra Jehova na akeherera ũũru, no mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ekaga maũndũ na kwĩgerera atekũmenyerera ũndũ.
English: A wise man fears and shuns evil, but the fool is hot headed and reckless.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũhiũhaga kũrakara ekaga maũndũ ma ũrimũ, nake mũndũ mwara nĩamenagwo.
English: He who is quick to become angry will commit folly, and a crafty man is hated.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ magayaga ũrimũ, no arĩa abaarĩrĩri mahumbagwo thũmbĩ ya ũmenyo.
English: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa ooru nĩmakainamĩrĩra harĩ arĩa ega, na arĩa aaganu mainamĩrĩre ihingo-inĩ cia arĩa athingu.
English: The evil bow down before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athĩĩni mathũũragwo o na nĩ andũ a itũũra rĩao, no itonga irĩ arata aingĩ.
English: The poor person is shunned even by his own neighbor, but the rich person has many friends.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũmenaga mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake nĩ kwĩhia ehagia, no kũrathimwo nĩ ũrĩa ũiguagĩra athĩĩni tha.
English: He who despises his neighbor sins, but he who has pity on the poor is blessed.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mathugundaga ũũru githĩ ti njĩra mahĩtagia? No arĩa mabangaga maũndũ mega monaga ũtugi na wĩhokeku.
English: Don’t they go astray who plot evil? But love and faithfulness belong to those who plan good.

Kikuyu: Wĩra wothe wa hinya ũrehaga uumithio, no mĩario mĩtheri ĩrehaga o ũthĩĩni.
English: In all hard work there is profit, but the talk of the lips leads only to poverty.

Kikuyu: Ũtonga wa andũ arĩa oogĩ nĩguo thũmbĩ yao, no ũrimũ wa andũ akĩĩgu ũciaraga ũrimũ.
English: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools crowns them with folly.

Kikuyu: Mũira mwĩhokeku nĩahonokagia mĩoyo, no mũira wa maheeni nĩ mũheenania.
English: A truthful witness saves souls, but a false witness is deceitful.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtigagĩra Jehova arĩ na kĩĩhitho kĩrũmu, o na ciana ciake nĩho irĩũragĩra.
English: In the fear of Yahweh is a secure fortress, and he will be a refuge for his children.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova nĩ gĩthima kĩa muoyo, kũhũndũraga mũndũ kuuma mĩtego-inĩ ya gĩkuũ.
English: The fear of Yahweh is a fountain of life, turning people from the snares of death.

Kikuyu: Ũingĩ wa andũ nĩguo riiri wa mũthamaki, no mũnene ũtarĩ andũ a gwatha nĩ mwanangĩku.
English: In the multitude of people is the king’s glory, but in the lack of people is the destruction of the prince.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkirĩrĩria arĩ ũmenyo mũingĩ, no mũndũ ũhiũhaga kũrakara onanagia ũrimũ.
English: He who is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a quick temper displays folly.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ĩrĩ na thayũ ĩheaga mwĩrĩ muoyo, no ũiru ũbuthagia mahĩndĩ.
English: The life of the body is a heart at peace, but envy rots the bones.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhinyagĩrĩria athĩĩni nĩkũnyarara anyararaga Ũrĩa wamombire, no ũrĩa wothe ũiguagĩra athĩĩni tha, nĩ Ngai atĩĩaga.
English: He who oppresses the poor shows contempt for his Maker, but he who is kind to the needy honors him.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũtino woka, arĩa aaganu matungumanagio thĩ, no arĩa athingu, o na gĩkuũ-inĩ makoragwo marĩ na rĩũrĩro.
English: The wicked is brought down in his calamity, but in death, the righteous has a refuge.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩ ũikaraga ngoro-inĩ ya ũrĩa ũũĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ, o na gatagatĩ ka irimũ nĩwĩmenyithanagia.
English: Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding, and is even made known in the inward part of fools.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu nĩgũtũũgĩria ũtũũgagĩria rũrĩrĩ, no mehia nĩ gĩconoko harĩ andũ o othe.
English: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩakenagio nĩ ndungata njũgĩ, no nĩangʼũrĩkagio nĩ ndungata ĩrĩa ĩmũconorithagia. 15
English: The king’s favor is toward a servant who deals wisely, but his wrath is toward one who causes shame. 15

Kikuyu: Icookio rĩa ũhooreri nĩrĩeheragia mangʼũrĩ, no kiugo kĩa ũũru kĩarahũraga marakara.
English: A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwa mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ rũgaathagĩrĩria ũmenyo, no kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu gathothokaga ũrimũ.
English: The tongue of the wise commends knowledge, but the mouths of fools gush out folly.

Kikuyu: Maitho ma Jehova marĩ kũndũ guothe, magacũthagĩrĩria andũ arĩa aaganu o na arĩa eega.
English: Yahweh’s eyes are everywhere, keeping watch on the evil and the good.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rũrehaga kĩhonia nĩ ta mũtĩ wa muoyo, no rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni rũthuthaga ngoro.
English: A gentle tongue is a tree of life, but deceit in it crushes the spirit.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu aregaga ũtaaro wa ithe, no mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkagĩra kũrũngwo onanagia ũbaarĩrĩri.
English: A fool despises his father’s correction, but he who heeds reproof shows prudence.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba ya mũndũ mũthingu ĩkoragwo na kĩgĩĩna kĩnene, no uumithio wa andũ aaganu ũmarehagĩra thĩĩna.
English: In the house of the righteous is much treasure, but the income of the wicked brings trouble.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ya andũ arĩa oogĩ ĩtambagia o ũũgĩ, no tiguo ngoro cia andũ arĩa akĩĩgu ciĩkaga.
English: The lips of the wise spread knowledge; not so with the heart of fools.

Kikuyu: Igongona rĩa andũ arĩa aaganu nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no mahooya ma mũndũ ũrĩa mũrũngĩrĩru nĩamakenagĩrĩra.
