English: The sacrifice made by the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.

Kikuyu: Njĩra cia andũ arĩa aaganu nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no nĩendete andũ arĩa mathingataga ũthingu.
English: The way of the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh, but he loves him who follows after righteousness.

Kikuyu: Iherithia rĩtarĩ rĩa tha rĩetereire mũndũ ũrĩa ũtigĩte njĩra, na mũndũ ũrĩa ũthũire kũrũngwo nĩagaakua.
English: There is stern discipline for one who forsakes the way. Whoever hates reproof shall die.

Kikuyu: Gĩkuũ na Mwanangĩko ciikaraga ciĩyanĩkĩte mbere ya Jehova, ĩ nacio ngoro cia andũ-rĩ, ikĩĩanĩkĩte atĩa!
English: Sheol† and Abaddon are before Yahweh— how much more then the hearts of the children of men!

Kikuyu: Mũnyũrũrania nĩathũire kũrũngwo; ndangĩhooya kĩrĩra kũrĩ ũrĩa mũũgĩ.
English: A scoffer doesn’t love to be reproved; he will not go to the wise.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ngenu ĩtũmaga gĩthiithi gĩthere, no ruo rwa ngoro rũthuthaga roho.
English: A glad heart makes a cheerful face, but an aching heart breaks the spirit.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ĩrĩ na gũkũũrana maũndũ nĩĩmaathaga ũmenyo, no kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu kehũũnagia na ũrimũ.
English: The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.

Kikuyu: Matukũ mothe ma andũ arĩa ahatĩrĩrie nĩ ma thĩĩna, no ngoro ĩrĩa njanjamũku ĩkoragwo na ndĩa hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: All the days of the afflicted are wretched, but one who has a cheerful heart enjoys a continual feast.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kĩndũ kĩnini hamwe na gwĩtigĩra Jehova, gũkĩra ũtonga mũnene ũrĩ na kĩnyariirĩko.
English: Better is little, with the fear of Yahweh, than great treasure with trouble.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kũrĩa rwĩga rwa nyeni harĩa harĩ wendo, gũkĩra kũrĩa njaũ noru harĩa harĩ na rũthũũro.
English: Better is a dinner of herbs, where love is, than a fattened calf with hatred.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũhiũhaga kũrakara aarahũraga ngarari, no mũndũ mũkirĩrĩria nĩkũhooreria ahooragĩria haaro.
English: A wrathful man stirs up contention, but one who is slow to anger appeases strife.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ya kĩgũũta nĩkũhingwo ĩhingĩtwo na mĩigua, no njĩra ya mũndũ mũrũngĩrĩru nĩ njĩra njariĩ.
English: The way of the sluggard is like a thorn patch, but the path of the upright is a highway.

Kikuyu: Mwana mũũgĩ atũmaga ithe akene, no mũndũ mũkĩĩgu nĩ nyina anyararaga.
English: A wise son makes a father glad, but a foolish man despises his mother.

Kikuyu: Ũrimũ ũkenagia mũndũ ũrĩa ũtooĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ, no mũndũ ũrĩa mũkuũku nĩarũngagĩrĩria mĩthiĩre yake.
English: Folly is joy to one who is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding keeps his way straight.

Kikuyu: Mĩbango nĩĩthaataga nĩ kwaga kĩrĩra, no kũrĩ na ataarani aingĩ mĩbango nĩĩgaacagĩra.
English: Where there is no counsel, plans fail; but in a multitude of counselors they are established.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩaiguaga gĩkeno agĩcookia ũhoro ũrĩa wagĩrĩire, nakĩo kiugo kĩarĩtio ihinda rĩrĩa rĩagĩrĩire, kaĩ nĩ kĩega-ĩ!
English: Joy comes to a man with the reply of his mouth. How good is a word at the right time!

Kikuyu: Harĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ njĩra ya muoyo ĩrorete na igũrũ, ĩmũgirie gũikũrũka mbĩrĩra-inĩ.
English: The path of life leads upward for the wise, to keep him from going downward to Sheol.‡

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩamomoraga nyũmba ya mũndũ ũrĩa mwĩtĩĩi, no nĩamenyagĩrĩra mĩhaka ya mũtumia wa ndigwa.
English: Yahweh will uproot the house of the proud, but he will keep the widow’s borders intact.

Kikuyu: Meciiria ma mũndũ mwaganu nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no meciiria ma andũ arĩa atheru nĩmamũkenagia.
English: Yahweh detests the thoughts of the wicked, but the thoughts of the pure are pleasing.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkoroku arehagĩra nyũmba yake thĩĩna, no ũrĩa ũthũire mahaki nĩagatũũra muoyo.
English: He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house, but he who hates bribes will live.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ya mũndũ mũthingu nĩĩthimaga macookio mayo, no kanua ka mũndũ mwaganu gathothokaga ũũru.
English: The heart of the righteous weighs answers, but the mouth of the wicked gushes out evil.

Kikuyu: Jehova aikaraga haraaya na andũ arĩa aaganu, no nĩaiguaga mahooya ma andũ arĩa athingu.
English: Yahweh is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.

Kikuyu: Gũtheria gĩthiithi nĩkũrehagĩra ngoro gĩkeno, naguo ũhoro ũrĩa mwega nĩwĩkĩraga mahĩndĩ hinya.
English: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart. Good news gives health to the bones.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũthikagĩrĩria ikaanania rĩrĩa rĩrehaga muoyo nĩagatũũrania na andũ arĩa oogĩ.
English: The ear that listens to reproof lives, and will be at home among the wise.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũregaga kũigua agĩkaanio nĩ kwĩnyarara enyararaga we mwene, no mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wĩtĩkagĩra kũrũngwo agĩĩaga na ũtaũku.
English: He who refuses correction despises his own soul, but he who listens to reproof gets understanding.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova nĩkuo kũheaga mũndũ ũũgĩ, nakuo kwĩnyiihia gũtongoragia gĩtĩĩo. 16
English: The fear of Yahweh teaches wisdom. Before honor is humility. 16

Kikuyu: Mĩbango ya ngoro nĩ ya mũndũ, no icookio rĩa rũrĩmĩ riumaga harĩ Jehova.
English: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ onaga mĩthiĩre yake yothe ĩrĩ mĩega, no Jehova nĩwe ũthimaga matanya.
English: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes, but Yahweh weighs the motives.

Kikuyu: Rekagĩrĩria Jehova maũndũ mothe marĩa ũrĩĩkaga, nayo mĩbango yaku nĩĩrĩgaacagĩra.
English: Commit your deeds to Yahweh, and your plans shall succeed.

Kikuyu: Jehova ombire indo ciothe irĩ na gĩtũmi gĩacio, ĩĩ-ni, o na arĩa aaganu aamoombire nĩ ũndũ wa mũthenya wa thĩĩna.
English: Yahweh has made everything for its own end— yes, even the wicked for the day of evil.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wothe wa ngoro ya mwĩtĩĩo nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova. Menyai na ma atĩrĩ, ndakaaga kũherithio.
English: Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to Yahweh; they shall certainly not be unpunished.

Kikuyu: Mehia nĩmahorohagĩrio nĩ wendo na wĩhokeku, na nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtigĩra Jehova-rĩ, mũndũ nĩeheragĩra ũũru.
English: By mercy and truth iniquity is atoned for. By the fear of Yahweh men depart from evil.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mĩthiĩre ya mũndũ yakenia Jehova-rĩ, atũmaga o na thũ ciake itũũranie nake na thayũ.
English: When a man’s ways please Yahweh, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.

Kikuyu: Kaba indo nini cionekete na njĩra ya ũthingu, gũkĩra uumithio mũnene ũtarĩ wa kĩhooto.
English: Better is a little with righteousness, than great revenues with injustice.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩabangaga na ngoro mũthiĩre wake, no Jehova nĩwe ũroragia makinya make kũrĩa ekũgerera.
English: A man’s heart plans his course, but Yahweh directs his steps.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ya mũthamaki yaragia ũhoro taarĩ ndũmĩrĩri, na kanua gake gatiagĩrĩirwo kuogomia kĩhooto.
English: Inspired judgments are on the lips of the king. He shall not betray his mouth.

Kikuyu: Igeri na ratiri cia ma nĩ cia Jehova; mahiga mothe ma gũthima marĩa marĩ mũhuko-rĩ, o namo no wĩra wake.
English: Honest balances and scales are Yahweh’s; all the weights in the bag are his work.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ athamaki-rĩ, gwĩka ũũru nĩ ũndũ ũrĩ magigi, nĩgũkorwo gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kĩĩhaandaga nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu.
English: It is an abomination for kings to do wrong, for the throne is established by righteousness.

Kikuyu: Athamaki nĩmakenagĩra mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia ma; nĩ marĩ bata na mũndũ waragia ũhoro wa ma.
English: Righteous lips are the delight of kings. They value one who speaks the truth.

Kikuyu: Mangʼũrĩ ma mũthamaki nĩ ta mũtũmwo wa gĩkuũ, no mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩakamahooreria.
English: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death, but a wise man will pacify it.

Kikuyu: Gũthererwo gĩthiithi nĩ mũthamaki nĩ muoyo; ũtugi wake ũtariĩ ta itu rĩa mbura ĩrĩa ĩkũragia irio.*
English: In the light of the king’s face is life. His favor is like a cloud of the spring rain.

Kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ kũgĩa ũũgĩ nĩ kwega gũkĩra thahabu-ĩ, gũthuura ũtaũku gũgakĩra kũgĩa na betha!
English: How much better it is to get wisdom than gold! Yes, to get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.

Kikuyu: Njĩra njariĩ ya arĩa arũngĩrĩru yeheragĩra ũũru; mũndũ ũrĩa ũmenyagĩrĩra mĩthiĩre yake nĩ muoyo wake mwene agitagĩra.
English: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil. He who keeps his way preserves his soul.

Kikuyu: Mwĩtĩĩo ũrũmagĩrĩrwo nĩ mwanangĩko, roho wa mwĩtũũgĩrio ũkarũmĩrĩrwo nĩ kũgũa.
English: Pride goes before destruction, and an arrogant spirit before a fall.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba roho wa kwĩnyiihia na gũkorwo hamwe na arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie, gũkĩra kũgayana indo cia ndaho na arĩa etĩĩi.
English: It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor, than to divide the plunder with the proud.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmbũyagia mataaro nĩagaacagĩra, na kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩhokaga Jehova.
English: He who heeds the Word finds prosperity. Whoever trusts in Yahweh is blessed.

Kikuyu: Arĩa oogĩ ngoro-inĩ metagwo akũũrani maũndũ, na ciugo njega itũũgagĩria ũtaaro.
English: The wise in heart shall be called prudent. Pleasantness of the lips promotes instruction.

Kikuyu: Ũtaũku nĩ gĩthima kĩa muoyo kũrĩ arĩa marĩ naguo, no ũrimũ ũrehaga iherithia kũrĩ andũ arĩa akĩĩgu.
English: Understanding is a fountain of life to one who has it, but the punishment of fools is their folly.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ya mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩyo ĩtongoragia kanua gake, nayo mĩromo yake ĩtũũgagĩria ũtaaro.
English: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.

Kikuyu: Ndeto njega nĩ igua rĩa ũũkĩ, irĩ mũrĩo ngoro-inĩ, na harĩ mahĩndĩ nĩ ũgima.
English: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ njĩra mũndũ onaga ta yagĩrĩire, no marigĩrĩrio mayo yerekagĩra gĩkuũ-inĩ.
English: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

Kikuyu: Kũhũũta kwa mũruti wa wĩra nĩkuo kũmũteithagia, ngʼaragu yake nĩyo ĩmũtindĩkaga.
English: The appetite of the laboring man labors for him, for his mouth urges him on.

Kikuyu: Kĩmaramari gĩthugundaga ũũru, na mĩario yakĩo nĩ ta mwaki wa gũcina.
English: A worthless man devises mischief. His speech is like a scorching fire.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu arehaga ngarari, na mũhuhu ũtigithanagia andũ marĩ ũrata mũnene.
English: A perverse man stirs up strife. A whisperer separates close friends.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa haaro aheenagĩrĩria mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake, akamũtongoria njĩra-inĩ ĩtarĩ njega.
English: A man of violence entices his neighbor, and leads him in a way that is not good.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa uunagĩra andũ riitho akoragwo agĩthugunda waganu, ũrĩa ũtembanagia mĩromo akoragwo agĩthugunda ũũru.
English: One who winks his eyes to plot perversities, one who compresses his lips, is bent on evil.

Kikuyu: Mbuĩ nĩ thũmbĩ ya riiri; cionekaga nĩ ũndũ wa mũtũũrĩre wa ũthingu.
English: Gray hair is a crown of glory. It is attained by a life of righteousness.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba mũndũ mũkirĩrĩria gũkĩra njamba ya ita, na nĩ kaba mũndũ ũrĩa ũrigagĩrĩria marakara make gũkĩra ũrĩa wĩnyiitagĩra itũũra inene.
English: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty; one who rules his spirit, than he who takes a city.

Kikuyu: Mĩtĩ ĩcuukagĩrwo maru-inĩ, no itua o rĩothe riumaga kũrĩ Jehova. 17
English: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from Yahweh. 17

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kĩenyũ kĩũmũ kĩa mũgate kũndũ kũrĩ na thayũ na ũhooreri, gũkĩra nyũmba ĩiyũrĩte ndĩa no ĩrĩ na ngũĩ.
English: Better is a dry morsel with quietness, than a house full of feasting with strife.

Kikuyu: Ndungata ĩrĩ na ũũgĩ ĩgaatuĩka ya gwatha mũriũ wa mwene yo ũrĩa ũrehaga njono, na nĩĩkegwatĩra igai ĩrĩ ta ũmwe wa ariũ a mwathi wayo.
English: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who causes shame, and shall have a part in the inheritance among the brothers.

Kikuyu: Nyũngũ nĩ ya gũthererio betha, na kĩrugutĩro nĩ gĩa gũthererio thahabu, no Jehova nĩwe ũgeragia ngoro cia andũ.
English: The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold, but Yahweh tests the hearts.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu athikagĩrĩria mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia waganu; nake mũndũ wa maheeni athikagĩrĩria rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rwaragia maũndũ ma gũthũkia.
English: An evildoer heeds wicked lips. A liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũthekagĩrĩra athĩĩni nĩkũmena amenaga Ũrĩa wamombire; mũndũ ũrĩa ũkenagĩra mũtino ndakaaga kũherithio.
English: Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker. He who is glad at calamity shall not be unpunished.

Kikuyu: Ciana cia ciana nĩ thũmbĩ harĩ andũ arĩa akũrũ, nao aciari nĩo riiri wa ciana ciao.
English: Children’s children are the crown of old men; the glory of children is their parents.

Kikuyu: Mĩario ya bata ndĩagĩrĩire kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, na makĩria mĩario ya maheeni ndĩagĩrĩire mũndũ ũrĩa wathanaga!
English: Excellent speech isn’t fitting for a fool, much less do lying lips fit a prince.

Kikuyu: Ihaki rĩhaana ta ihiga rĩa kũrehe mũnyaka kũrĩ ũrĩa ũhakanaga; kũrĩa guothe athiiaga nĩagaacagĩra.
English: A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it; wherever he turns, he prospers.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhumbagĩra ihĩtia nĩ wendo acaragia, no ũrĩa ũcookagĩra ũhoro atigithanagia andũ marĩ ũrata mũnene.
English: He who covers an offense promotes love; but he who repeats a matter separates best friends.

Kikuyu: Kũrũithio nĩgũkenagia mũndũ ũrĩa ũkũũranaga maũndũ gũkĩra iboko igana harĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
English: A rebuke enters deeper into one who has understanding than a hundred lashes into a fool.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũru arongooragia o ũremi; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩagatũmĩrwo mũnene ũtarĩ tha amũũkĩrĩre.
English: An evil man seeks only rebellion; therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba gũcemania na nduba ĩtunyĩtwo ciana ciayo gũkĩra gũcemania na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu na ũrimũ wake.
English: Let a bear robbed of her cubs meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩrĩha wega na ũũru, ũũru ndũkeehera nyũmba-inĩ yake.
English: Whoever rewards evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

Kikuyu: Kwambĩrĩria haaro nĩ ta kũhingũrĩra maaĩ mahingĩrĩrie; nĩ ũndũ ũcio tiganaga na ũhoro ũcio ngarari itaanagĩa.
English: The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam, therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.

Kikuyu: Gũtuĩra mũndũ mwaganu atĩ ndehĩtie na gũtuĩra ũrĩa ũtehĩtie atĩ nĩ mwĩhia, maũndũ macio meerĩ nĩ marĩ magigi harĩ Jehova.
English: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Mbeeca nĩ cia bata ũrĩkũ irĩ guoko-inĩ kwa mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, na ndangĩhota kũgũra ũũgĩ, na ndarĩ ngoro ya kwenda gũtaũkĩrwo?
English: Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, since he has no understanding?

Kikuyu: Mũrata wa mũndũ amwendaga hĩndĩ ciothe, nake mũrũ wa nyina na mũndũ aciaragwo nĩ ũndũ wa hĩndĩ ya thĩĩna.
English: A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtooĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ atuĩkaga wa kũrĩha thiirĩ wa mũndũ ũngĩ o na akaiga kĩndũ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake.
English: A man void of understanding strikes hands, and becomes collateral in the presence of his neighbor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wendete haaro nĩ mehia endete; na mũndũ ũrĩa wakaga kĩhingo kĩraihu na igũrũ eĩtagĩria wanangĩki.
English: He who loves disobedience loves strife. One who builds a high gate seeks destruction.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩ ngoro ya ũremi ndagaacagĩra; nake mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ rũrĩmĩ rwa kũheenania agũũaga thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: One who has a perverse heart doesn’t find prosperity, and one who has a deceitful tongue falls into trouble.

Kikuyu: Kũgĩa na mũriũ mũkĩĩgu kũrehaga ihooru; ithe wa mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ndarĩ hĩndĩ akenaga.
English: He who becomes the father of a fool grieves. The father of a fool has no joy.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ngenu nĩ ndawa njega, no roho mũthuthĩku ũngʼaragia mahĩndĩ.
English: A cheerful heart makes good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu amũkagĩra ihaki na hitho, nĩguo ogomie ciira wa kĩhooto.
English: A wicked man receives a bribe in secret, to pervert the ways of justice.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũkũũranaga maũndũ aikaraga arorete o ũũgĩ, no maitho ma mũndũ mũkĩĩgu morũũraga nginya ituri cia thĩ.
English: Wisdom is before the face of one who has understanding, but the eyes of a fool wander to the ends of the earth.

Kikuyu: Mwana mũkĩĩgu arehagĩra ithe kĩeha, na marũrũ kũrĩ nyina ũrĩa wamũciarire.
English: A foolish son brings grief to his father, and bitterness to her who bore him.

Kikuyu: Ti wega kũherithia mũndũ ũtekĩte mahĩtia, kana kũhũũra anene nĩ ũndũ wa ũrũngĩrĩru wao.
English: Also to punish the righteous is not good, nor to flog officials for their integrity.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩ na ũmenyo aragia ciugo athimĩte, nake mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku aikaraga ahooreire.
English: He who spares his words has knowledge. He who is even tempered is a man of understanding.

Kikuyu: O na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu angĩkira nĩonagwo arĩ mũũgĩ, na akoneka arĩ mũtaũku angĩtumia kanua. 18
English: Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is counted wise. When he shuts his lips, he is thought to be discerning. 18

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtendaga gũtũũrania na andũ arũmagĩrĩra o merirĩria make; ningĩ areganaga na matua mothe ma ũũgĩ ũrĩa mũkinyanĩru.
English: A man who isolates himself pursues selfishness, and defies all sound judgment.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ndakenagĩra ũtaũku, no akenagĩra kwaria woni wake mwene.
English: A fool has no delight in understanding, but only in revealing his own opinion.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa waganu woka, ũrehaga ũirano, nacio njono irehaga thoni.
English: When wickedness comes, contempt also comes, and with shame comes disgrace.

Kikuyu: Ciugo cia kanua ka mũndũ nĩ ta ndia ndiku, no gĩthima kĩa ũũgĩ nĩ karũũĩ gatherũkaga.
English: The words of a man’s mouth are like deep waters. The fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.

Kikuyu: Ti wega gũtĩĩra andũ arĩa aaganu maũthĩ, kana kuogomia kĩhooto kĩa arĩa matehĩtie.
English: To be partial to the faces of the wicked is not good, nor to deprive the innocent of justice.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo ya mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ĩmũrehagĩra ngũĩ, nako kanua gake geetagia rũthanju.
English: A fool’s lips come into strife, and his mouth invites beatings.

Kikuyu: Kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu nĩko kamũthũkagĩria maũndũ, nayo mĩromo yake nĩ mũtego wa ngoro yake.
English: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are a snare to his soul.

Kikuyu: Ndeto cia mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩ ta twenyũ twega twa irio; tũmerũkaga nginya tũturi-inĩ twa nda tũrĩa tũrĩ thĩinĩ mũno.
English: The words of a gossip are like dainty morsels: they go down into a person’s innermost parts.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ kĩgũũta wĩra-inĩ wake nĩ wa nyina na ũrĩa wanangaga.
English: One who is slack in his work is brother to him who is a master of destruction.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova nĩ mũthiringo ũrĩ hinya; arĩa athingu moragĩra ho, nao magaikara marĩ agitĩre.
English: Yahweh’s name is a strong tower: the righteous run to him, and are safe.

Kikuyu: Ũtonga wa andũ arĩa atongu nĩguo itũũra rĩao rĩa hinya; meciiragia nĩ rũthingo rũtangĩhaicĩka.
English: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, like an unscalable wall in his own imagination.

Kikuyu: Mbere ya mwanangĩko, ngoro ya mũndũ ĩkoragwo na mwĩtĩĩo, no kwĩnyiihia nĩkuo gũtongoragia gĩtĩĩo.
English: Before destruction the heart of man is proud, but before honor is humility.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũcookagia ũhoro mbere ya kũũthikĩrĩria, ũndũ ũcio nĩ ũrimũ na thoni ciake.
English: He who answers before he hears, that is folly and shame to him.

Kikuyu: Roho wa mũndũ nĩguo ũmũũmagĩrĩria rĩrĩa arĩ mũrũaru, no roho mũthuthĩku nũũ ũngĩhota kũwĩtiiria?
English: A man’s spirit will sustain him in sickness, but a crushed spirit, who can bear?

Kikuyu: Ngoro ya mũndũ mũkũũrani maũndũ nĩĩgĩagĩra ũmenyo; matũ ma arĩa oogĩ nĩmaũcaragĩrĩria.
English: The heart of the discerning gets knowledge. The ear of the wise seeks knowledge.

Kikuyu: Kĩheo mũndũ aheanĩte kĩmuonagia njĩra ya kũhĩtũkĩra, gĩkamũkinyia harĩ andũ arĩa anene.
English: A man’s gift makes room for him, and brings him before great men.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrehaga ciira wake mbere onekaga taarĩ we na ma, o nginya rĩrĩa mũndũ ũrĩa maraciira nake ooka kũmũũria ciũria.
English: He who pleads his cause first seems right— until another comes and questions him.

Kikuyu: Ũcuuki wa mĩtĩ nĩũniinaga ngarari, na ũgateithũrana andũ eerĩ njamba.
English: The lot settles disputes, and keeps strong ones apart.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũrakarie nĩ mũrũ wa nyina nĩ hinya kwĩiguithania nake gũkĩra kwĩgwatĩra itũũra inene rĩirigĩre, nacio ngarari nĩ ta mĩgĩĩko ĩrĩa ĩhingaga njeera.
English: A brother offended is more difficult than a fortified city. Disputes are like the bars of a fortress.

Kikuyu: Nda ya mũndũ nĩĩhũũnagio nĩ maciaro ma kanua gake; nĩaiganagwo nĩ magetha ma mĩromo yake.
English: A man’s stomach is filled with the fruit of his mouth. With the harvest of his lips he is satisfied.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rũrĩ na hinya wa gwathĩrĩra mũndũ muoyo kana gĩkuũ, no arĩa marwendete no marĩĩage maciaro maaruo.
English: Death and life are in the power of the tongue; those who love it will eat its fruit.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩgĩĩagĩra na mũtumia nĩegĩagĩra na kĩndũ kĩega, na nĩgũtugwo atugĩtwo nĩ Jehova.
English: Whoever finds a wife finds a good thing, and obtains favor of Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthĩĩni athaithanaga aiguĩrwo tha, no gĩtonga gĩcookagia ũhoro na ũũru.
English: The poor plead for mercy, but the rich answer harshly.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũceeri na andũ aingĩ aahota kwĩyũnũhithia, no nĩ kũrĩ mũrata ũnyiitanaga na mũndũ gũkĩra mũrũ wa nyina na mũndũ. 19
English: A man of many companions may be ruined, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother. 19

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba mũndũ mũthĩĩni ũrĩa ũthiiaga na mĩthiĩre ĩtarĩ ũcuuke, gũkĩra mũndũ mũkĩĩgu waragia waganu.
English: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than he who is perverse in his lips and is a fool.

Kikuyu: Ti wega kũgĩa na kĩyo hatarĩ na ũmenyo, ningĩ kũhĩkĩka mũno gũtũmaga mũndũ ahĩtie njĩra.
English: It isn’t good to have zeal without knowledge, nor to be hasty with one’s feet and miss the way.

Kikuyu: Ũrimũ wa mũndũ nĩguo wanangaga muoyo wake, nayo ngoro yake ĩtetagia Jehova.
English: The foolishness of man subverts his way; his heart rages against Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Ũtonga nĩwongagĩrĩra mũndũ arata aingĩ, no mũndũ mũthĩĩni nĩeheragĩrwo nĩ mũratawe.
English: Wealth adds many friends, but the poor is separated from his friend.

Kikuyu: Mũira wa maheeni ndakaaga kũherithio, na ũrĩa ũitũrũraga maheeni ndageethara.
English: A false witness shall not be unpunished. He who pours out lies shall not go free.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ macaragia ũrĩa mangĩendwo nĩ mũnene, na mũndũ mũheani iheo-rĩ, nĩ mũrata wa andũ othe.
English: Many will entreat the favor of a ruler, and everyone is a friend to a man who gives gifts.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũndũ mũthĩĩni nĩatiganagio nĩ andũ ao othe-rĩ, ĩ nao arata ake mangĩkĩmwĩtheema atĩa! O na angĩmathingata akĩmathaithaga, nĩmethiĩrĩire, matirĩ ho.
English: All the relatives of the poor shun him; how much more do his friends avoid him! He pursues them with pleas, but they are gone.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩgĩĩagĩra na ũũgĩ nĩ ngoro yake mwene endete; ũrĩa ũkenagĩra ũtaũku nĩagaacagĩra.
English: He who gets wisdom loves his own soul. He who keeps understanding shall find good.

Kikuyu: Mũira wa maheeni ndakaaga kũherithio, na ũrĩa ũitũrũraga maheeni nĩakoorwo nĩ muoyo.
English: A false witness shall not be unpunished. He who utters lies shall perish.

Kikuyu: Gũtiagĩrĩire mũndũ mũkĩĩgu gũikaraga egangarĩte; gũkĩrĩ ũũru makĩria ngombo gwatha anene!
English: Delicate living is not appropriate for a fool, much less for a servant to have rule over princes.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ wa mũndũ nĩũmũheaga ũkirĩrĩria, riiri wake nĩkũrekera andũ arĩa angĩ mahĩtia mao.
English: The discretion of a man makes him slow to anger. It is his glory to overlook an offense.

Kikuyu: Marakara ma mũthamaki nĩ ta mũraramo wa mũrũũthi, no ũtugi wake nĩ ta ime rĩrĩ nyeki-inĩ.
English: The king’s wrath is like the roaring of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.

Kikuyu: Mwana mũkĩĩgu nĩ mwanangĩko harĩ ithe, na mũtumia wa haaro ahaana ta maaĩ maratata mategũtigithĩria.
English: A foolish son is the calamity of his father. A wife’s quarrels are a continual dripping.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba na ũtonga igayagwo kuuma kũrĩ aciari, no mũtumia mũbaarĩrĩri maũndũ oimaga kũrĩ Jehova.
English: House and riches are an inheritance from fathers, but a prudent wife is from Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Ũgũũta ũrehaga toro mũnene, na mũndũ gĩthayo arĩĩagwo nĩ ngʼaragu.
English: Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep. The idle soul shall suffer hunger.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wathĩkagĩra maathani-rĩ, nĩ muoyo wake amenyagĩrĩra, no ũrĩa ũtoonaga bata wa kũmenyerera mĩthiĩre yake nĩagaakua.
English: He who keeps the commandment keeps his soul, but he who is contemptuous in his ways shall die.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguagĩra athĩĩni tha nĩ Jehova akombagĩra indo, nake Jehova nĩakamũrĩha nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ekĩte.
English: He who has pity on the poor lends to Yahweh; he will reward him.

Kikuyu: Rũithagia mũrũguo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa kũrĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro; tiga kwenda gũtũma akooragwo.
English: Discipline your son, for there is hope; don’t be a willing party to his death.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa marakara mahiũ no nginya aherithio; ũngĩmũteithũra rĩmwe no nginya ũkaamũteithũra rĩngĩ.
English: A hot-tempered man must pay the penalty, for if you rescue him, you must do it again.

Kikuyu: Thikĩrĩria mataaro na wĩtĩkĩre ũrutani, marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩũgaatuĩka mũndũ mũũgĩ.
English: Listen to counsel and receive instruction, that you may be wise in your latter end.

Kikuyu: Mĩbango ya ngoro ya mũndũ nĩ mĩingĩ, no muoroto wa Jehova noguo ũkaahinga.
English: There are many plans in a man’s heart, but Yahweh’s counsel will prevail.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩa mũndũ eriragĩria nĩ wendo ũrĩa ũtathiraga, nĩ kaba gũthĩĩna gũkĩra mũndũ wa maheeni.
English: That which makes a man to be desired is his kindness. A poor man is better than a liar.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova gũkinyagia mũndũ muoyo-inĩ: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ akahurũka aiganĩire, atarĩ na thĩĩna.
English: The fear of Yahweh leads to life, then contentment; he rests and will not be touched by trouble.

Kikuyu: Kĩgũũta kĩrikagia guoko gwakĩo thaani-inĩ; gĩgakĩremwo o na nĩgũgũcookia kanua!
English: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish; he will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.

Kikuyu: Hũũra mũnyũrũrania, nao arĩa mateciiragia merute kũgĩa na ũbaarĩrĩri; kaania mũkũũrani maũndũ, na nĩekuongerera ũmenyo.
English: Flog a scoffer, and the simple will learn prudence; rebuke one who has understanding, and he will gain knowledge.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtunyaga ithe indo na akaingata nyina, nĩ mwana wa kũrehe thoni na gĩconoko.
English: He who robs his father and drives away his mother is a son who causes shame and brings reproach.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ũngĩtiga gũthikĩrĩria mataaro-rĩ, nĩũkoora njĩra ũtigane na ciugo cia ũmenyo.
English: If you stop listening to instruction, my son, you will stray from the words of knowledge.

Kikuyu: Mũira wa kũhakwo anyũrũragia kĩhooto, na kanua ka mũndũ mwaganu kameragia o ũũru.
English: A corrupt witness mocks justice, and the mouth of the wicked gulps down iniquity.

Kikuyu: Maherithia maigĩirwo anyũrũrania, nacio iboko ikaigĩrwo mĩgongo ya andũ arĩa akĩĩgu. 20
English: Penalties are prepared for scoffers, and beatings for the backs of fools. 20

Kikuyu: Ndibei nĩ mũnyũrũrania, na njoohi nĩ mũndũ wa ngũĩ; mũndũ ũrĩa ũhĩtithagio nĩcio ti mũũgĩ.
English: Wine is a mocker and beer is a brawler. Whoever is led astray by them is not wise.

Kikuyu: Mangʼũrĩ ma mũthamaki nĩ ta mũraramo wa mũrũũthi; ũrĩa ũmũrakaragia nĩ muoyo wake ateaga.
English: The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion. He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtheema ngũĩ kũrehagĩra mũndũ gĩtĩĩo, no mũndũ o wothe mũkĩĩgu ahiũhaga kũũgitana.
English: It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife, but every fool will be quarreling.

Kikuyu: Kĩgũũta gĩticimbaga hĩndĩ ya irĩma; nĩ ũndũ ũcio hĩndĩ ya magetha gĩathiĩ kũmaatha gĩtirĩ kĩndũ kĩonaga.
English: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the winter; therefore he shall beg in harvest, and have nothing.

Kikuyu: Muoroto wa ngoro ya mũndũ nĩ ta maaĩ ma ndia ndiku, no mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku nĩamatahaga.
English: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water, but a man of understanding will draw it out.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ metuaga marĩ na wendo ũtangĩthira, no mũndũ mwĩhokeku-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩmuona?
English: Many men claim to be men of unfailing love, but who can find a faithful man?

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthingu atũũraga mũtũũrĩre ũtarĩ ũcuuke; kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ ciana ciake iria atigaga.
English: A righteous man walks in integrity. Blessed are his children after him.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũthamaki aikarĩra gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene atue ciira, maitho make mathũngũrũraga ũũru wothe.
English: A king who sits on the throne of judgment scatters away all evil with his eyes.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũngiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjigĩte ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ theru; ndĩ mũtheru, na ndirĩ na mehia”?
English: Who can say, “I have made my heart pure. I am clean and without sin”?

Kikuyu: Mahiga ma ratiri mataiganainie ũritũ, o na ithimi itaiganĩte: cierĩ nĩ indo irĩ magigi harĩ Jehova.
English: Differing weights and differing measures, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

Kikuyu: O na kaana kamenyekaga nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciako, gakamenyeka kana mĩtugo yako nĩ mĩtheru, na kana nĩ mĩagĩrĩru.
English: Even a child makes himself known by his doings, whether his work is pure, and whether it is right.

Kikuyu: Matũ marĩa maiguaga na maitho marĩa monaga-rĩ, cierĩ nĩ Jehova waciũmbire.
English: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, Yahweh has made even both of them.

Kikuyu: Tiga kwenda toro, ndũkae gũthĩĩna; ikaraga wĩiguĩte, na nĩũrĩrĩĩaga irio ũkahũũna.
English: Don’t love sleep, lest you come to poverty. Open your eyes, and you shall be satisfied with bread.

Kikuyu: Mũgũri akĩgũra oigaga atĩrĩ, “Kĩndũ gĩkĩ ti kĩega, ti kĩega!” Agacooka agathiĩ akĩĩrahaga ũrĩa oĩ kũgũra.
English: “It’s no good, it’s no good,” says the buyer; but when he is gone his way, then he boasts.

Kikuyu: Thahabu nĩ kũrĩ, o na ruru no nyingĩ, no mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia ũmenyo nĩ ithaga rĩa goro rĩrĩa rĩtonekaga.
English: There is gold and abundance of rubies, but the lips of knowledge are a rare jewel.

Kikuyu: Oya nguo ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ; mĩige ĩrũgamĩrĩre thiirĩ ũcio wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ.
English: Take the garment of one who puts up collateral for a stranger; and hold him in pledge for a wayward woman.

Kikuyu: Irio cionekete na njĩra ya kũheenania irĩ cama harĩ mũndũ, no thuutha kanua gake kaiyũraga o mũthanga.
English: Fraudulent food is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth is filled with gravel.

Kikuyu: Mĩbango ĩgaacagĩra nĩ ũndũ wa kũhooya kĩrĩra, ũngĩtua gũthiĩ mbaara-inĩ, heo ũtaaro.
English: Plans are established by advice; by wise guidance you wage war!

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩoimagia ndeto cia ndundu nja, nĩ ũndũ ũcio wĩthemage mũndũ waragia mũno.
English: He who goes about as a tale-bearer reveals secrets; therefore don’t keep company with him who opens wide his lips.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩruma ithe kana nyina, tawa wake ũkaahorera nduma-inĩ ĩrĩa ndumanu.
English: Whoever curses his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in blackness of darkness.

Kikuyu: Igai rĩrĩa rĩoneka narua kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ, marigĩrĩrio-inĩ rĩtituĩkaga rĩa kĩrathimo.
English: An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning won’t be blessed in the end.

Kikuyu: Ndũkoige atĩrĩ, “Nĩngerĩhĩria ũũru ũyũ!” Eterera Jehova nake nĩegũkũhonokia.
English: Don’t say, “I will pay back evil.” Wait for Yahweh, and he will save you.

Kikuyu: Mahiga ma ratiri mataiganainie ũritũ nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, nacio ratiri itarĩ cia ma itimũkenagia.
English: Yahweh detests differing weights, and dishonest scales are not pleasing.

Kikuyu: Makinya ma mũndũ matongoragio nĩ Jehova. Mũndũ-rĩ, angĩkĩhota atĩa kũmenya njĩra yake mwene?
English: A man’s steps are from Yahweh; how then can man understand his way?

Kikuyu: Nĩ mũtego harĩ mũndũ kwamũrĩra Ngai kĩndũ na ihenya, acooke gwĩcũũrania ũhoro wa mĩĩhĩtwa yake thuutha-inĩ.
English: It is a snare to a man to make a rash dedication, then later to consider his vows.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki mũũgĩ nĩathũngũrũraga andũ arĩa aaganu; ageragĩria kĩgaragari gĩa gũkonyora ngano igũrũ rĩao.
English: A wise king winnows out the wicked, and drives the threshing wheel over them.

Kikuyu: Roho wa mũndũ nĩ tawa wa Jehova, ũthuthuuragia o nginya tũturi tũrĩa tũrĩ thĩinĩ mũno.
English: The spirit of man is Yahweh’s lamp, searching all his innermost parts.

Kikuyu: Wendo na wĩhokeku nĩcigitagĩra mũthamaki; ũtugi nĩũtũmaga gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene kĩrũme.
English: Love and faithfulness keep the king safe. His throne is sustained by love.

Kikuyu: Hinya wa andũ ethĩ nĩguo riiri wao, nacio mbuĩ nĩcio riiri wa arĩa akũrũ.
English: The glory of young men is their strength. The splendor of old men is their gray hair.

Kikuyu: Mahũũra na nguraro nĩitheragia ũũru wa mũndũ, nacio iboko itheragia o nginya tũturi twa thĩinĩ mũno. 21
English: Wounding blows cleanse away evil, and beatings purge the innermost parts. 21

Kikuyu: Ngoro ya mũthamaki ĩrĩ moko-inĩ ma Jehova; amĩroragia o kũrĩa ekwenda, o ta ũrĩa maaĩ moinithagĩrio mĩtaro-inĩ.
English: The king’s heart is in Yahweh’s hand like the watercourses. He turns it wherever he desires.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ oonaga mĩthiĩre yake yothe ĩrĩ mĩega, no Jehova athimaga ngoro.
English: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but Yahweh weighs the hearts.

Kikuyu: Gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire na ũrĩa ũrĩ kĩhooto nĩ gwĩtĩkagĩrwo nĩ Jehova gũkĩra kũruta igongona.
English: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.

Kikuyu: Maitho ma mwĩtũũgĩrio na ngoro ya mwĩtĩĩo, o icio tawa wa arĩa aaganu-rĩ, nĩ mehia!
English: A high look and a proud heart, the lamp of the wicked, is sin.

Kikuyu: Mĩbango ya arĩa marĩ kĩyo nĩĩrehaga uumithio, o ta ũrĩa ti-itherũ ihenya inene rĩrehaga ũthĩĩni.
English: The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit; and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.

Kikuyu: Kũgĩa na ũtonga na njĩra ya rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni nĩ kĩbii gĩa gũthira, na nĩ mũtego wa gĩkuũ.
English: Getting treasures by a lying tongue is a fleeting vapor for those who seek death.

Kikuyu: Haaro ya andũ arĩa aaganu ĩkaamakururia, ĩmeherie, nĩgũkorwo nĩmaregaga gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
English: The violence of the wicked will drive them away, because they refuse to do what is right.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ya mũndũ mũhĩtia nĩ njogomu, no mĩthiĩre ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mahĩtia nĩ mĩrũngarũ.
English: The way of the guilty is devious, but the conduct of the innocent is upright.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba gũtũũra nyũmba-igũrũ, koine-inĩ, gũkĩra gũtũũra nyũmba thĩinĩ na mũtumia wa haaro.
English: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop than to share a house with a contentious woman.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu eriragĩria gwĩka o ũũru; ndaiguuagĩra mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake tha.
English: The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũnyũrũrania aaherithio, arĩa maagĩte ũũgĩ nĩmohĩgaga; hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũndũ mũũgĩ aataarwo nĩagĩĩaga na ũmenyo.
English: When the mocker is punished, the simple gains wisdom. When the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa Mũthingu, o we Ngai, nĩonaga nyũmba ya ũrĩa mwaganu, na akarehithĩria ũcio mwaganu mwanangĩko.
English: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked, and brings the wicked to ruin.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩhinga matũ ndakaigue kĩrĩro kĩa arĩa athĩĩni-rĩ, o nake agaakaya na ndone wa kũmwĩtĩka.
English: Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor, he will also cry out, but shall not be heard.

Kikuyu: Kĩheo kĩheanĩtwo na hitho nĩkĩhooreragia marakara, na ihaki ihithe nguo-inĩ rĩhooreragia mangʼũrĩ manene.
English: A gift in secret pacifies anger, and a bribe in the cloak, strong wrath.

Kikuyu: Ituĩro rĩa kĩhooto rĩatuuo, rĩrehaga gĩkeno kũrĩ andũ arĩa athingu, no rĩrehaga kĩmako kũrĩ arĩa mekaga ũũru.
English: It is joy to the righteous to do justice; but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtiganaga na njĩra ya ũũgĩ, akaahurũkĩra kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa arĩa akuũ.
English: The man who wanders out of the way of understanding shall rest in the assembly of the departed spirits.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwendi ikeno nĩagathĩĩna; ũrĩa wothe mwendi ndibei na maguta-rĩ, gũtirĩ hĩndĩ agaatonga.
English: He who loves pleasure will be a poor man. He who loves wine and oil won’t be rich.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu atuĩkaga irĩhi rĩa gũkũũra ũrĩa mũthingu, na ũrĩa ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku agatuĩka irĩhi rĩa gũkũũra ũrĩa mũrũngĩrĩru.
English: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous, the treacherous for the upright.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba gũtũũra werũ-inĩ, gũkĩra gũtũũra na mũtumia wa haaro na kũrakara narua.
English: It is better to dwell in a desert land, than with a contentious and fretful woman.

Kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩ kũrĩ mĩthemba yothe ya irio njega o na maguta, no mũndũ mũkĩĩgu arĩĩaga kĩrĩa gĩothe arĩ nakĩo.
English: There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish man swallows it up.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũthingataga ũthingu na wendani nĩagĩĩaga na muoyo, na ũgaacĩru, na gĩtĩĩo.
English: He who follows after righteousness and kindness finds life, righteousness, and honor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ atharĩkagĩra itũũra inene rĩa andũ arĩa njamba, na akamomora kĩĩhitho kĩa hinya kĩrĩa mehokete.
English: A wise man scales the city of the mighty, and brings down the strength of its confidence.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũmenyagĩrĩra kanua gake na rũrĩmĩ rwake nĩeyeheragia mũtino-inĩ.
English: Whoever guards his mouth and his tongue keeps his soul from troubles.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwĩtĩĩi na mwĩgereri, etagwo “Mũnyũrũria”; ekaga maũndũ make na mwĩtĩĩo mũnene.
English: The proud and arrogant man—“Scoffer” is his name— he works in the arrogance of pride.

Kikuyu: Merirĩria ma mũndũ kĩgũũta nĩmo makaamũũraga, tondũ moko make matiendaga kũruta wĩra.
English: The desire of the sluggard kills him, for his hands refuse to labor.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wothe eriragĩria o makĩria, no arĩa athingu maheanaga mategũtara.
English: There are those who covet greedily all day long; but the righteous give and don’t withhold.

Kikuyu: Igongona rĩa mũndũ mwaganu nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi: ĩ rĩtikĩrĩ magigi makĩria hĩndĩ ĩrĩa rĩrehetwo na ũhinga ngoro-inĩ!
English: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination— how much more, when he brings it with a wicked mind!

Kikuyu: Mũira wa maheeni nĩgũthira agaathira, nake ũrĩa ũmũthikagĩrĩria akaanangwo nginya tene.
English: A false witness will perish. A man who listens speaks to eternity.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu erũagĩrĩra na ũrũme, no mũndũ mũrũngĩrĩru nĩeciiragia ũhoro wa mĩthiĩre yake.
English: A wicked man hardens his face; but as for the upright, he establishes his ways.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũũgĩ, kana ũmenyi, kana kĩrĩra o na kĩrĩkũ kĩngĩhota gũũkĩrĩra Jehova.
English: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Mbarathi nĩĩharagĩrio nĩ ũndũ wa mũthenya wa mbaara, no ũhootani uumaga kũrĩ Jehova. 22
English: The horse is prepared for the day of battle; but victory is with Yahweh. 22

Kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩega nĩrĩakwĩrirĩrio gũkĩra ũtonga mũnene; gũtĩĩka nĩ kwega gũkĩra betha kana thahabu.
English: A good name is more desirable than great riches, and loving favor is better than silver and gold.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa atongu na arĩa athĩĩni harĩ ũndũ ũmwe ũmanyiitithanĩtie; Jehova nĩwe Mũmbi wao othe.
English: The rich and the poor have this in common: Yahweh is the maker of them all.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũbaarĩrĩri onaga ũgwati akehitha, no arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ mathiiaga o na mbere nao magatoonya thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: A prudent man sees danger and hides himself; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.

Kikuyu: Kwĩnyiihia na gwĩtigĩra Jehova kũrehaga ũtonga, na gĩtĩĩo, na muoyo.
English: The result of humility and the fear of Yahweh is wealth, honor, and life.

Kikuyu: Njĩra cia andũ arĩa aaganu irĩ mĩigua na mĩtego, no ũrĩa ũgitagĩra ngoro yake aikaraga kũraya nacio.
English: Thorns and snares are in the path of the wicked; whoever guards his soul stays far from them.

Kikuyu: Menyeria mwana njĩra ĩrĩa agĩrĩirwo nĩkũgera, na ndarĩ hĩndĩ akaamiuma o na aakũra.
English: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa atongu nĩ mathaga arĩa athĩĩni, na ũrĩa ũkombaga nĩ ndungata ya mũmũkoomberi.
English: The rich rule over the poor. The borrower is servant to the lender.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga waganu agethaga thĩĩna, naruo rũthanju rwa marakara make nĩrũkanangwo.
English: He who sows wickedness reaps trouble, and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtaana we mwene nĩakarathimwo, tondũ nĩagayagĩra arĩa athĩĩni irio ciake.
English: He who has a generous eye will be blessed, for he shares his food with the poor.

Kikuyu: Ingata mũnyũrũrania, nayo ngũĩ nĩĩgũkweherera; inegene na irumi o nacio nĩcigũthira.
English: Drive out the mocker, and strife will go out; yes, quarrels and insults will stop.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wendaga ngoro theru na akaaragia maũndũ ma ũtugi, mũthamaki nĩagatuĩka mũrata wake.
English: He who loves purity of heart and speaks gracefully is the king’s friend.

Kikuyu: Maitho ma Jehova nĩmamenyagĩrĩra ũmenyo, no nĩathaahagia ciugo cia ũrĩa ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku.
English: Yahweh’s eyes watch over knowledge, but he frustrates the words of the unfaithful.

Kikuyu: Kĩgũũta kiugaga, “Nja kũrĩ na mũrũũthi!” Kana gĩkoiga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũũragĩrwo njĩra-inĩ!”
English: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside! I will be killed in the streets!”

Kikuyu: Kanua ka mũtumia mũtharia nĩ irima iriku, nake ũrĩa werekeirio mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova agũũaga thĩinĩ wa rĩo.
English: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit. He who is under Yahweh’s wrath will fall into it.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ya mwana yohanĩtio na ũrimũ, no rũthanju rwa kũmũherithia nĩrũkaũingata kũraya nake.
English: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child; the rod of discipline drives it far from him.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhinyagĩrĩria arĩa athĩĩni nĩguo eyongerere ũtonga, na ũrĩa ũheaga itonga iheo, eerĩ nĩmagathĩĩna.
English: Whoever oppresses the poor for his own increase and whoever gives to the rich, both come to poverty.

Kikuyu: Tega matũ, na ũthikĩrĩrie mĩario ya andũ arĩa oogĩ; nyiita na ngoro yaku maũndũ marĩa ngũrutana,
English: Turn your ear, and listen to the words of the wise. Apply your heart to my teaching.

Kikuyu: tondũ nĩ ũndũ mwega hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũkũmaiga ngoro-inĩ yaku, na ũkoragwo namo mothe mĩromo-inĩ yaku.
English: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you, if all of them are ready on your lips.

Kikuyu: Ndaakũmenyithia mo nĩgeetha mwĩhoko waku ũkoragwo harĩ Jehova, wee nĩwe ngũruta maũndũ macio ũmũthĩ, ĩĩ wee nĩwe ngũruta.
English: I teach you today, even you, so that your trust may be in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Githĩ ndikwandĩkĩire thimo mĩrongo ĩtatũ, thimo cia ũtaarani na ũmenyo,
English: Haven’t I written to you thirty excellent things of counsel and knowledge,

Kikuyu: ngakũruta ciugo cia ma na cia kwĩhokeka, nĩgeetha ũkaahota kũhe ũrĩa ũgũtũmĩte macookio mega?
English: To teach you truth, reliable words, to give sound answers to the ones who sent you?

Kikuyu: Ndũkanatunye arĩa athĩĩni indo ciao tondũ wa ũthĩĩni wao, na ndũkanahinyĩrĩrie arĩa abatari ciira-inĩ,
English: Don’t exploit the poor because he is poor; and don’t crush the needy in court;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩakamaciirĩrĩra, na nĩagatunya arĩa mamatunyaga indo ciao.
English: for Yahweh will plead their case, and plunder the life of those who plunder them.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanagĩe ũrata na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ marakara mahiũ, ndũkanagĩe thiritũ na mũndũ ũrĩa ũhiũhaga kũrakara,
English: Don’t befriend a hot-tempered man. Don’t associate with one who harbors anger,

Kikuyu: tondũ wahota kwĩruta mĩthiĩre yake, na ũnyiitwo nĩ mũtego.
English: lest you learn his ways and ensnare your soul.

Kikuyu: Ndũgatuĩke mũndũ wa kũiga kĩndũ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ, kana ũtuĩke wee nĩwe wa kũrũgamagĩrĩra mathiirĩ ma andũ angĩ;
English: Don’t you be one of those who strike hands, of those who are collateral for debts.

Kikuyu: ũngĩkaaga kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩha nakĩo-rĩ, ũgaatunywo ũrĩrĩ waku o ũũkomeire.
English: If you don’t have means to pay, why should he take away your bed from under you?

Kikuyu: Ndũkaneherie ihiga rĩa tene rĩa mũhaka rĩrĩa rĩahaandirwo nĩ maithe manyu ma tene.
English: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone which your fathers have set up.

Kikuyu: Nĩwonaga mũndũ mũũgĩ na wĩra wake? Ũcio agatungata o na mbere ya athamaki; ndagatungata mbere ya andũ matooĩkaine. 23
English: Do you see a man skilled in his work? He will serve kings. He won’t serve obscure men. 23

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa waikara thĩ kũrĩĩanĩra na mwathi wa bũrũri-rĩ, wĩmenyerere nũũ ũcio mũrĩ nake;
English: When you sit to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before you;

Kikuyu: wĩigĩrĩre kahiũ mũmero ũngĩkorwo ũrĩ mũndũ mũkoroku.
English: put a knife to your throat if you are a man given to appetite.

Kikuyu: Ndũkerirĩrie irio ciake irĩ mũrĩo, nĩgũkorwo nĩ irio cia gũkũheenereria.
English: Don’t be desirous of his dainties, since they are deceitful food.

Kikuyu: Tiga kwĩnogia na kwenda gwĩtongia; gĩa na ũũgĩ wa kũhota kwĩrigĩrĩria.
English: Don’t weary yourself to be rich. In your wisdom, show restraint.

Kikuyu: No wone ũtonga, ũcooke ũbuĩrie, nĩgũkorwo ti-itherũ ũtonga nĩwĩmeragia mathagu, na ũkombũka ta nderi yerekeire matu-inĩ.
English: Why do you set your eyes on that which is not? For it certainly sprouts wings like an eagle and flies in the sky.

Kikuyu: Tiga kũrĩa irio cia mũndũ mũkarĩ, ndũkerirĩrie irio ciake cia mũrĩo;
English: Don’t eat the food of him who has a stingy eye, and don’t crave his delicacies,

Kikuyu: tondũ nĩ mũndũ wa arĩa meciiragia o ũhoro wa thogora hĩndĩ ciothe. Akwĩraga atĩrĩ, “Rĩa na ũnyue,” no ngoro yake ndĩrĩ hamwe nawe.
English: for as he thinks about the cost, so he is. “Eat and drink!” he says to you, but his heart is not with you.

Kikuyu: Nĩũgatahĩka kĩrĩa kĩnini ũgaakorwo ũrĩĩte, nacio ngaatho ciaku nĩikoorĩra thĩ.
English: You will vomit up the morsel which you have eaten and waste your pleasant words.

Kikuyu: Ndũkaarie na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, nĩgũkorwo we no anyararire ũũgĩ wa ciugo ciaku.
English: Don’t speak in the ears of a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.

Kikuyu: Ndũkaneherie ihiga rĩa tene rĩa mũhaka, kana ũhatĩke mĩhaka ya mĩgũnda ya ciana cia ngoriai,
English: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone. Don’t encroach on the fields of the fatherless,

Kikuyu: tondũ Mũgitĩri wacio nĩ arĩ na hinya; nĩakamaciirĩrĩra agũũkĩrĩre.
English: for their Defender is strong. He will plead their case against you.

Kikuyu: Ringĩrĩria ngoro yaku gũthikĩrĩria ũrutani, na matũ maku kũigua ciugo cia ũmenyo.
English: Apply your heart to instruction, and your ears to the words of knowledge.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanaage kũherithia mwana; ũngĩmũhũũra na rũthanju-rĩ, ndangĩkua.
English: Don’t withhold correction from a child. If you punish him with the rod, he will not die.

Kikuyu: Mũhũũrage na rũthanju nĩguo ũhonokie muoyo wake kuuma kũrĩ gĩkuũ.
English: Punish him with the rod, and save his soul from Sheol.†

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ngoro yaku ĩngĩgĩa na ũũgĩ-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo ngoro yakwa no ĩkene;
English: My son, if your heart is wise, then my heart will be glad, even mine.

Kikuyu: ngoro yakwa nĩĩgacanjamũka rĩrĩa mĩromo yaku ĩkaaria maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire.
English: Yes, my heart will rejoice when your lips speak what is right.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanareke ngoro yaku ĩiguĩre ehia ũiru, no hĩndĩ ciothe gĩĩaga na kĩyo gĩa gwĩtigĩra Jehova.
English: Don’t let your heart envy sinners, but rather fear Yahweh all day long.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ti-itherũ wee ũrĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro thuutha-inĩ, na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩaku gĩtigaathira.
English: Indeed surely there is a future hope, and your hope will not be cut off.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, thikĩrĩria nĩgeetha ũgĩe na ũũgĩ, na ũige ngoro yaku njĩra-inĩ ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire.
English: Listen, my son, and be wise, and keep your heart on the right path!

Kikuyu: Ndũkegwatanie na andũ arĩa manyuuaga njoohi, kana arĩa makorokagĩra nyama,
English: Don’t be among ones drinking too much wine, or those who gorge themselves on meat;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo arĩĩu na andũ arĩa akoroku matuĩkaga athĩĩni, naguo ũgũũta ũtũmaga mũndũ ehumbe matangari.
English: for the drunkard and the glutton shall become poor; and drowsiness clothes them in rags.

Kikuyu: Thikagĩrĩria thoguo, we ũrĩa wagũciarire, na ndũkanyarare maitũguo, o na aakorwo nĩ mũkũrũ.
English: Listen to your father who gave you life, and don’t despise your mother when she is old.

Kikuyu: Wĩgũrĩre ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma na ndũkanawendie; wĩgĩĩre na ũũgĩ, na ũtaaro, na ũtaũku.
English: Buy the truth, and don’t sell it. Get wisdom, discipline, and understanding.

Kikuyu: Ithe wa mwana mũthingu arĩ gĩkeno kĩnene mũno; nake ũrĩa ũrĩ na mwana mũũgĩ nĩakenagio nĩwe.
English: The father of the righteous has great joy. Whoever fathers a wise child delights in him.

Kikuyu: Thoguo na maitũguo marocanjamũka nĩ ũndũ waku; mũtumia ũrĩa wagũciarire arokena!
English: Let your father and your mother be glad! Let her who bore you rejoice!

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, heaga ngoro yaku, na ũreke maitho maku marũmagĩrĩre njĩra ciakwa,
English: My son, give me your heart; and let your eyes keep in my ways.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũmaraya nĩ irima iriku, nake mũtumia mũtharia nĩ gĩthima gĩkunderu.
English: For a prostitute is a deep pit; and a wayward wife is a narrow well.

Kikuyu: Oohagĩria andũ njĩra-inĩ o ta njangiri, na nĩatũmaga kuongerereke andũ matarĩ ehokeku kĩrĩndĩ-inĩ.
English: Yes, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the unfaithful among men.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũrĩ na haaro? Nũũ ũrĩ na kĩeha? Nũũ ũrĩ hatĩka-inĩ? Nũũ ũrĩ na mateta? Nũũ ũgurarĩtio hatarĩ gĩtũmi? Nũũ ũtunĩhĩtie maitho?
English: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaints? Who has needless bruises? Who has bloodshot eyes?

Kikuyu: Nĩ arĩa matindaga marũmanĩrĩire na ndibei, arĩa mathiiaga magĩcamaga mbakũri cia ndibei ĩrĩa ndukanie.
English: Those who stay long at the wine; those who go to seek out mixed wine.

Kikuyu: Tiga kwĩrorera ndibei rĩrĩa ĩtunĩhĩte, rĩrĩa ĩgũkenga ĩrĩ gĩkombe-inĩ, rĩrĩa ĩkũmerũka wega!
English: Don’t look at the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup, when it goes down smoothly.

Kikuyu: Mũthia-inĩ ĩrũmanaga ta nyoka, ĩgagũtheeca na thumu ta nduĩra.
English: In the end, it bites like a snake, and poisons like a viper.

Kikuyu: Maitho maku makoonaga maũndũ mageni, na meciiria maku meciirie maũndũ ma waganu.
English: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will imagine confusing things.

Kikuyu: Ũgaatuĩka ta mũndũ ũkomete iria-inĩ rĩrĩa inene gatagatĩ, o ta mũndũ ũkomete mũthia wa gĩtugĩ kĩa macua.
English: Yes, you will be as he who lies down in the middle of the sea, or as he who lies on top of the rigging:

Kikuyu: Nĩũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Ngũthĩtwo, no ndinatiihio! Nĩmahũũra, no ndinaigua ũndũ! Ngokĩra-rĩ, nĩguo ngacarie ĩngĩ ya kũnyua?” 24
English: “They hit me, and I was not hurt! They beat me, and I don’t feel it! When will I wake up? I can do it again. I will look for more.” 24

Kikuyu: Tiga kũiguĩra andũ arĩa aaganu ũiru, na ndũkanerirĩrie thiritũ yao,
English: Don’t be envious of evil men, neither desire to be with them;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ngoro ciao ithugundaga o haaro, na mĩromo yao yaragia ũhoro wa kũrehere andũ thĩĩna.
English: for their hearts plot violence and their lips talk about mischief.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba yakagwo na ũũgĩ, naguo ũtaũku nĩguo ũtũmaga ĩĩhaande;
English: Through wisdom a house is built; by understanding it is established;

Kikuyu: ũmenyo nĩguo ũiyũragia tũnyũmba twayo, ũgatũiyũria na indo cia bata, na ũtonga mwega.
English: by knowledge the rooms are filled with all rare and beautiful treasure.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ arĩ ũhoti mũnene, na mũndũ ũrĩ ũmenyo nĩakĩragĩrĩria kũgĩa na hinya;
English: A wise man has great power. A knowledgeable man increases strength,

Kikuyu: ũgĩthiĩ mbaara-inĩ nĩũbatarĩtio nĩgũtaarwo, na nĩgeetha ũhootane nĩũbatarĩtio nĩ aheani kĩrĩra aingĩ.
English: for by wise guidance you wage your war, and victory is in many advisors.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ ũrĩ igũrũ mũno, ndũngĩkinyĩrĩka nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu; rĩrĩa kwagomanwo kĩhingo-inĩ, ndangĩona wa kuuga.
English: Wisdom is too high for a fool. He doesn’t open his mouth in the gate.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũthugundaga ũũru-rĩ, andũ mamwĩtaga mungumania.
English: One who plots to do evil will be called a schemer.

Kikuyu: Mathugunda ma ũrimũ nĩ mehia, na mũnyũrũrania nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ andũ.
English: The schemes of folly are sin. The mocker is detested by men.

Kikuyu: Ũngĩtuĩka wa kũũrwo nĩ hinya hĩndĩ ya thĩĩna, hinya waku kaĩ nĩ mũnini-ĩ!
English: If you falter in the time of trouble, your strength is small.

Kikuyu: Honokia arĩa maratwarĩrĩrio gĩkuũ-inĩ; girĩrĩria arĩa maratũgũũga merekeire gĩthĩnjĩro.
English: Rescue those who are being led away to death! Indeed, hold back those who are staggering to the slaughter!

Kikuyu: Ũngiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũhoro ũcio tũtiraũmenyete,” githĩ ũrĩa ũthimaga ngoro tiwe ũmenyaga? Ũrĩa ũrangagĩra muoyo waku-rĩ, githĩ ndooĩ kana nĩguo? Githĩ ndakaarĩha o mũndũ kũringana na ũrĩa ekĩte?
English: If you say, “Behold, we didn’t know this,” doesn’t he who weighs the hearts consider it? He who keeps your soul, doesn’t he know it? Shall he not give to every man according to his work?

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, rĩa ũũkĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩ mwega; ũũkĩ uumĩte magua-inĩ ũrĩ mũrĩo ũkĩũcama.
English: My son, eat honey, for it is good, the droppings of the honeycomb, which are sweet to your taste;

Kikuyu: Ningĩ menya atĩ ũũgĩ ũrĩ mũrĩo harĩ ngoro yaku; ũngĩgĩa naguo, nĩũkagĩa na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro, na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩaku gĩtigaathira.
English: so you shall know wisdom to be to your soul. If you have found it, then there will be a reward: Your hope will not be cut off.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanoohie mũndũ mũthingu njĩra-inĩ ũtharĩkĩre nyũmba yake ta mũitũ, ndũkanahithũkĩre gĩikaro gĩake;
English: Don’t lay in wait, wicked man, against the habitation of the righteous. Don’t destroy his resting place;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũndũ mũthingu o na angĩgũa maita mũgwanja, agũũaga o agĩũkagĩra, no andũ arĩa aaganu marũndagwo nĩ mũtino.
English: for a righteous man falls seven times and rises up again, but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa thũ yaku yagũa-rĩ, ndũkanamĩkenerere; hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yahĩngwo-rĩ, ndũkareke ngoro yaku ĩkene,
English: Don’t rejoice when your enemy falls. Don’t let your heart be glad when he is overthrown,

Kikuyu: nĩguo Jehova ndakone ũndũ ũcio arakare, akĩhũndũre marakara make kuuma kũrĩ thũ ĩyo.
English: lest Yahweh see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanathĩĩnĩke ngoro nĩ ũndũ wa andũ arĩa ooru, kana wĩrirĩrie kũhaanana na arĩa aaganu,
English: Don’t fret yourself because of evildoers, neither be envious of the wicked;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa mũũru ndarĩ na mwĩhoko wa thuutha-inĩ, na tawa wa ũrĩa mwaganu nĩũkahorio.
English: for there will be no reward to the evil man. The lamp of the wicked will be snuffed out.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, wĩtigĩre Jehova na wĩtigĩre mũthamaki, na ndũkagĩe na ngwatanĩro na andũ arĩa aremi,
English: My son, fear Yahweh and the king. Don’t join those who are rebellious,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo acio eerĩ no mamũrehithĩrie mwanangĩko wa narua, na nũũ ũngĩmenya nĩ mĩtino ĩrĩkũ mangĩrehe?
English: for their calamity will rise suddenly. Who knows what destruction may come from them both?

Kikuyu: O na ici no thimo cia andũ arĩa oogĩ: Kwenda mwena ũmwe gũkĩra ũrĩa ũngĩ ciira-inĩ ti ũndũ mwega:
English: These also are sayings of the wise: To show partiality in judgment is not good.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe wĩraga mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na mahĩtia atĩrĩ, “Wee ndũrĩ na mahĩtia,” kĩrĩndĩ nĩgĩkamũruma, na athũũrwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ.
English: He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous,” peoples will curse him, and nations will abhor him—

Kikuyu: No arĩa matuagĩra andũ arĩa aaganu ciira, nĩmakona wega, na magĩe na kĩrathimo kĩega.
English: but it will go well with those who convict the guilty, and a rich blessing will come on them.

Kikuyu: Icookio rĩa wĩhokeku nĩ ta kĩmumunyano kĩa mĩromo.
English: An honest answer is like a kiss on the lips.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩkia wĩra wa gũtua mwako na nja, na ũhaarĩrie mĩgũnda yaku; thuutha ũcio cooka wĩakĩre nyũmba.
English: Prepare your work outside, and get your fields ready. Afterwards, build your house.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanarute ũira ũigĩrĩre mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku kĩgeenyo, kana ũheenanie na mĩromo yaku.
English: Don’t be a witness against your neighbor without cause. Don’t deceive with your lips.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanoige atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩka o ta ũrĩa anjĩkĩte; ngwĩrĩhĩria harĩ mũndũ ũcio kũringana na ũrĩa eekire.”
English: Don’t say, “I will do to him as he has done to me; I will repay the man according to his work.”

Kikuyu: Ndaahĩtũkĩire mũgũnda-inĩ wa kĩgũũta, ngĩhĩtũkĩra mũgũnda-inĩ wa mĩthabibũ wa mũndũ ũtooĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ;
English: I went by the field of the sluggard, by the vineyard of the man void of understanding.

Kikuyu: na rĩrĩ, mĩigua yamerete kũndũ guothe, na mũgũnda ũkaiyũra riya, naruo rũthingo rwa mahiga rũkamomoka.
English: Behold, it was all grown over with thorns. Its surface was covered with nettles, and its stone wall was broken down.

Kikuyu: Nĩndacũũranirie na ngoro yakwa maũndũ macio ndonire, na ngĩĩruta ũndũ kuumana na ũrĩa ndonire:
English: Then I saw, and considered well. I saw, and received instruction:

Kikuyu: Mũtugo wa gũkoma hanini, gũcooka toro hanini, gũkũnja moko hanini ũhurũke;
English: a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep,

Kikuyu: naguo ũthĩĩni ũgaagũkorerera ta mũitũ, na wagi ũgũkorerere ta mũndũ wĩohete indo cia mbaara. 25
English: so your poverty will come as a robber and your want as an armed man. 25

Kikuyu: Ici nĩ thimo ingĩ cia Solomoni, iria ciacookanĩrĩirio na ikĩandĩkwo nĩ andũ a Hezekia ũrĩa warĩ mũthamaki wa Juda:
English: These also are proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.

Kikuyu: Kũhithĩrĩra ũndũ nĩ ũgooci wa Ngai; nakuo gũtuĩria ũndũ nĩkuo kũgoocithagia athamaki.
English: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing, but the glory of kings is to search out a matter.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa igũrũ kũraihĩte na igũrũ, nakuo thĩ gũkarika mũno-rĩ, ũguo noguo ngoro cia athamaki itangĩtuĩrĩka.
English: As the heavens for height, and the earth for depth, so the hearts of kings are unsearchable.

Kikuyu: Theria betha ĩthire gĩko, na nĩhegũtigara kĩndũ kĩa mũturi wa betha;
English: Take away the dross from the silver, and material comes out for the refiner.

Kikuyu: eheria andũ arĩa aaganu harĩ mũthamaki, na gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene nĩgĩkũrũmio nĩ ũthingu.
English: Take away the wicked from the king’s presence, and his throne will be established in righteousness.

Kikuyu: Ndũgetũũgĩrie harĩ mũthamaki, na ndũkerũgamie gatagatĩ ka anene;
English: Don’t exalt yourself in the presence of the king, or claim a place among great men;

Kikuyu: kaba mũthamaki we mwene akwĩre, “Ambata, ũũke haha,” gũkĩra agũconorithie mbere ya mũndũ ũrĩ igweta. Ũndũ ũrĩa wonete na maitho-rĩ,
English: for it is better that it be said to you, “Come up here,” than that you should be put lower in the presence of the prince, whom your eyes have seen.

Kikuyu: ndũkahiũhe kũũtwara igooti-inĩ, tondũ-rĩ, ũgeeka atĩa marigĩrĩrio-inĩ, mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku angĩkaaruta ũira agũconorithie?
English: Don’t be hasty in bringing charges to court. What will you do in the end when your neighbor shames you?

Kikuyu: Ciirai na mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku we mwene, nĩguo ndũkoimie ndundu ya mũndũ ũcio ũngĩ nja,
English: Debate your case with your neighbor, and don’t betray the confidence of another,

Kikuyu: kana ũiguuo nĩ mũndũ ũngĩ agũconore, ũtũũre na ngumo njũru ĩtagagũthira.
English: lest one who hears it put you to shame, and your bad reputation never depart.

Kikuyu: Kiugo kĩarĩtio hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire nĩ ta matunda ma thahabu marĩ thaani-inĩ ya betha.
English: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in settings of silver.

Kikuyu: Gĩcũhĩ gĩa gũtũ gĩa thahabu, kana ithaga rĩa thahabu ĩrĩa therie, nĩ taguo irũithia rĩa mũndũ mũũgĩ rĩtariĩ gũtũ-inĩ kwa mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguaga.
English: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover to an obedient ear.

Kikuyu: Ta ũhehu wa tharunji hĩndĩ ya magetha, no taguo mũtwari ndũmĩrĩri mwĩhokeku ahaana kũrĩ arĩa mamũtũmaga; nĩatũmaga ngoro cia anene ake ikene.
English: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to those who send him; for he refreshes the soul of his masters.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa matu na rũhuho matarĩ mbura ihaana, no taguo mũndũ ahaana ũrĩa ũrahaga ũrĩa ekũheana iheo na ndaheane.
English: As clouds and wind without rain, so is he who boasts of gifts deceptively.

Kikuyu: Ũkirĩrĩria no ũguucĩrĩrie ũrĩa waathanaga etĩkĩre ũndũ, naruo rũrĩmĩ ruororo no ruune ihĩndĩ.
English: By patience a ruler is persuaded. A soft tongue breaks the bone.

Kikuyu: Ũngĩona ũũkĩ-rĩ, rĩa o wa gũkũigana, ũngĩrĩa mũingĩ mũno nĩũgũtahĩka.
English: Have you found honey? Eat as much as is sufficient for you, lest you eat too much, and vomit it.

Kikuyu: Nyiihia gũthiiaga kwa mũndũ wa itũũra, ũngĩthiĩ kaingĩ no anogio nĩwe agũthũũre.
English: Let your foot be seldom in your neighbor’s house, lest he be weary of you, and hate you.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũheanaga ũira wa maheeni agookĩrĩra mũndũ wa itũũra, ahaana ta njũgũma, kana rũhiũ rwa njora, kana mũguĩ mũũgĩ.
English: A man who gives false testimony against his neighbor is like a club, a sword, or a sharp arrow.

Kikuyu: Kwĩhoka mũndũ ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku hĩndĩ ya thĩĩna, no ta kwĩhoka igego ituĩku, kana kũgũrũ kũrathua.
English: Confidence in someone unfaithful in time of trouble is like a bad tooth or a lame foot.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũinagĩra mũndũ ũrĩ na ngoro nditũ nyĩmbo, no ta mũndũ kwĩruta nguo mũthenya wa heho, kana gũitĩrĩria thiki igata-inĩ.
English: As one who takes away a garment in cold weather, or vinegar on soda, so is one who sings songs to a heavy heart.

Kikuyu: Thũ yaku ĩngĩkorwo ĩhũtiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe irio ĩrĩe; ĩngĩkorwo ĩnyootiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe maaĩ ĩnyue.
English: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat. If he is thirsty, give him water to drink;

Kikuyu: Weka ũguo-rĩ, nĩũkũmĩigĩrĩra makara ma mwaki mũtwe, nake Jehova nĩagakũrĩha.
English: for you will heap coals of fire on his head, and Yahweh will reward you.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa rũhuho rwa gathigathini rũrehaga mbura, noguo rũrĩmĩ rwa njuukũ rũrehaga ũhoro wa marakara.
English: The north wind produces rain; so a backbiting tongue brings an angry face.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba gũtũũra nyũmba-igũrũ koine-inĩ, gũkĩra gũtũũrania nyũmba thĩinĩ na mũtumia wa haaro.
English: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop than to share a house with a contentious woman.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa maaĩ mahehu manyootoraga mũndũ mũnyootu, no taguo ũhoro mwega uumĩte bũrũri wa kũraya wĩkaga.
English: Like cold water to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.

Kikuyu: O ta gĩthima kĩiyũrĩte ndaka, kana itherũkĩro rĩa maaĩ riunjuge, ũguo nĩguo mũndũ mũthingu ahaanaga angĩĩrekereria harĩ andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: Like a muddied spring and a polluted well, so is a righteous man who gives way before the wicked.

Kikuyu: Ti wega kũrĩa ũũkĩ mũingĩ mũno, o na ti ũndũ wa gũtĩĩka mũndũ gwĩcarĩria gĩtĩĩo we mwene.
English: It is not good to eat much honey, nor is it honorable to seek one’s own honor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtangĩhota gwatha ngoro yake atariĩ ta itũũra inene rĩmomoku, na rĩtarĩ na thingo. 26
English: Like a city that is broken down and without walls is a man whose spirit is without restraint. 26

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa tharunji ĩtaagĩrĩire kũgĩa hĩndĩ ya riũa, kana mbura kuura hĩndĩ ya magetha-rĩ, noguo gĩtĩĩo gĩtagĩrĩire mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
English: Like snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.

Kikuyu: Ta kanyoni-ka-nja gekũbatabata, kana thũngũrũrũ ĩkũhiũka-hiũka, noguo kĩrumi gĩtarĩ gĩtũmi gĩtarĩ ha kwĩgwatĩrĩra.
English: Like a fluttering sparrow, like a darting swallow, so the undeserved curse doesn’t come to rest.

Kikuyu: Kĩboko nĩ kĩa mbarathi, matamu nĩ ma ndigiri, na rũthanju nĩ rwa ciande cia andũ arĩa akĩĩgu!
English: A whip is for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the back of fools!

Kikuyu: Ndũkanacookerie mũndũ mũkĩĩgu kũringana na ũrimũ wake, ndũkae gũtuĩka take.
English: Don’t answer a fool according to his folly, lest you also be like him.

Kikuyu: Cookeria mũndũ mũkĩĩgu kũringana na ũrimũ wake, nĩguo ndakeyone taarĩ mũndũ mũũgĩ maitho-inĩ make.
English: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.

Kikuyu: Gũtũma ndũmĩrĩri na guoko kwa mũndũ mũkĩĩgu-rĩ, nĩ ta gwĩtinia magũrũ, kana kũnyua haaro.
English: One who sends a message by the hand of a fool is cutting off feet and drinking violence.

Kikuyu: O ta magũrũ ma gĩthua ũrĩa mathiiaga maniginĩte, noguo thimo ĩhaana ĩkĩario nĩ kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
English: Like the legs of the lame that hang loose, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

Kikuyu: Kũhe mũndũ mũkĩĩgu gĩtĩĩo no ta kuoherera ihiga kĩgũtha-inĩ.
English: As one who binds a stone in a sling, so is he who gives honor to a fool.

Kikuyu: Thimo ĩkĩario nĩ kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu nĩ ta mũigua ũrĩ guoko-inĩ kwa mũrĩĩu.
English: Like a thorn bush that goes into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wandĩkaga mũndũ mũkĩĩgu kana mwĩhĩtũkĩri nĩ ta ũrĩa ũikagia mĩguĩ o ro ũguo agatiihia andũ.
English: As an archer who wounds all, so is he who hires a fool or he who hires those who pass by.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu acookagĩra ũrimũ wake o ta ũrĩa ngui ĩcookagĩrĩra matahĩko mayo.
English: As a dog that returns to his vomit, so is a fool who repeats his folly.

Kikuyu: Wanona mũndũ wĩonaga arĩ mũũgĩ maitho-inĩ make? Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu arĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gũkĩra mũndũ ũcio.
English: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

Kikuyu: Kĩgũũta kiugaga atĩrĩ, “Njĩra-inĩ kũrĩ na mũrũũthi, mũrũũthi mũrĩĩani ũrorũũra njĩra-inĩ cia itũũra!”
English: The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road! A fierce lion roams the streets!”

Kikuyu: Ta ũrĩa mũrango ũtindaga ũkĩgarũrũka ihocio-inĩ ciaguo, noguo kĩgũũta gĩtindaga gĩkĩĩgarũra gĩkomete ũrĩrĩ-inĩ wakĩo.
English: As the door turns on its hinges, so does the sluggard on his bed.

Kikuyu: Kĩgũũta gĩikagia guoko gwakĩo o thaani-inĩ; kĩnogagio nĩgũcooka gũkuoya gĩgũkinyie kanua-inĩ.
English: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish. He is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.

Kikuyu: Kĩgũũta kĩĩonaga kĩrĩ kĩũgĩ maitho-inĩ makĩo, gũkĩra andũ mũgwanja arĩa macookagia ũhoro na ũũgĩ.
English: The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who answer with discretion.

Kikuyu: Mwĩhĩtũkĩri wĩtoonyagia inegene-inĩ rĩtamũkoniĩ no ta mũndũ ũkũnyiita ngui matũ.
English: Like one who grabs a dog’s ears is one who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own.

Kikuyu: Ta mũgũrũki ũgũikia icinga cia mwaki, kana agaikia mĩguĩ ya gĩkuũ,
English: Like a madman who shoots torches, arrows, and death,

Kikuyu: no taguo mũndũ ahaana ũrĩa ũheenagia mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake, agacooka kũmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ itherũ nyuma narĩo!”
English: is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “Am I not joking?”

Kikuyu: Mwaki ũngĩaga ngũ no kũhora ũhoraga; ngarari cingĩaga mũhuhu no gũthira ithiraga.
English: For lack of wood a fire goes out. Without gossip, a quarrel dies down.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa makara magwatagia marĩa mangĩ mwaki, na o ta ũrĩa ngũ ciakanagia mwaki, noguo mũndũ mũũgiti aakagĩrĩria haaro.
English: As coals are to hot embers, and wood to fire, so is a contentious man to kindling strife.

Kikuyu: Ndeto cia mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩ ta twenyũ twega twa irio; tũmerũkaga tũgathiĩ mwĩrĩ wa mũndũ o thĩinĩ biũ.
English: The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels, they go down into the innermost parts.

Kikuyu: Ta ũrĩa indo cia rĩũmba inyorokagio ikahenia na igũrũ, no taguo mĩromo mĩnyoroku ĩhumbagĩra ngoro ĩrĩ na ũũru.
English: Like silver dross on an earthen vessel are the lips of a fervent one with an evil heart.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa rũmena aaragia ũhinga na mĩromo yake, no thĩinĩ wa ngoro yake aiyũrĩtwo no maheeni.
English: A malicious man disguises himself with his lips, but he harbors evil in his heart.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka mĩario yake nĩ ya gũkenania, ndũkanamwĩtĩkie, nĩgũkorwo ngoro yake ĩiyũrĩte maũndũ mũgwanja marĩ magigi.
English: When his speech is charming, don’t believe him, for there are seven abominations in his heart.

Kikuyu: Rũmena rwake no rũhithwo na maheeni, no waganu wake ũkaaguũrio kĩũngano-inĩ.
English: His malice may be concealed by deception, but his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩenja irima, nĩwe ũkaagũa rĩo; mũndũ angĩgaragaria ihiga, rĩkaagaragara rĩmũcookerere.
English: Whoever digs a pit shall fall into it. Whoever rolls a stone, it will come back on him.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni nĩkũmena rũmenete arĩa rũtuuragia, nako kanua ga kwĩyendithĩrĩria karehaga mwanangĩko. 27
English: A lying tongue hates those it hurts; and a flattering mouth works ruin. 27

Kikuyu: Tiga kwĩraha nĩ ũndũ wa rũciũ, nĩgũkorwo ndũũĩ kĩrĩa mũthenya ũngĩrehe.
English: Don’t boast about tomorrow; for you don’t know what a day may bring.

Kikuyu: Reke mũndũ ũngĩ akũgaathĩrĩrie, no ti kanua gaku mwene; mũndũ ũngĩ nĩakũgaathĩrĩrie, no ti mĩromo yaku mwene.
English: Let another man praise you, and not your own mouth; a stranger, and not your own lips.

Kikuyu: Ihiga nĩ iritũ na mũthanga nĩ mũrigo, no kũrakario nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu nĩ kũritũ gũkĩra icio cierĩ.
English: A stone is heavy, and sand is a burden; but a fool’s provocation is heavier than both.

Kikuyu: Marakara matirĩ tha, namo marũrũ moinaga ta mũiyũro; no ũiru-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩhota kũwĩtiiria?
English: Wrath is cruel, and anger is overwhelming; but who is able to stand before jealousy?

Kikuyu: Kaba kũrũithio nĩ mũndũ ũtekũhithĩrĩra ũndũ gũkĩra wendo mũhithe.
English: Better is open rebuke than hidden love.

Kikuyu: Nguraro cia gũtihio nĩ mũrata no ciĩhokwo, no imumunyano nyingĩ cia mũgũthũũri ti cia kwĩhokwo.
English: The wounds of a friend are faithful, although the kisses of an enemy are profuse.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũhũũnu athũũraga o na ũũkĩ, no mũndũ mũhũũtu, o na kĩndũ kĩrũrũ aiguaga kĩrĩ mũrĩo.
English: A full soul loathes a honeycomb; but to a hungry soul, every bitter thing is sweet.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũũrĩte gwake mũciĩ ahaana ta nyoni ĩtiganĩirie gĩtara kĩayo.
English: As a bird that wanders from her nest, so is a man who wanders from his home.

Kikuyu: Maguta manungi wega na ũbumba irehagĩra ngoro ya mũndũ gĩkeno, na noguo wega wa mũrata wa mũndũ wĩkaga akĩmũhe ũtaaro wake wa ma.
English: Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart; so does earnest counsel from a man’s friend.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanateanĩrie mũrata waku kana mũrata wa thoguo, na ndũgathiĩ kwa mũrũ wa maitũguo rĩrĩa mũtino wagũkora: nĩ kaba mũndũ wa itũũra ũcio ũrĩ hakuhĩ, gũkĩra mũrũ wa nyina na mũndũ ũrĩ kũraya.
English: Don’t forsake your friend and your father’s friend. Don’t go to your brother’s house in the day of your disaster. A neighbor who is near is better than a distant brother.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, tuĩka mũndũ mũũgĩ, ũgĩkenagie ngoro yakwa; na niĩ hotage gũcookagĩria mũndũ o wothe ũngĩĩnyarara.
English: Be wise, my son, and bring joy to my heart, then I can answer my tormentor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ oonaga ũgwati ũgĩũka akehitha, no arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ mathiiaga o na mbere, nao magatoonya thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.

Kikuyu: Oya nguo ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũgamagĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ; mĩige ĩrũgamĩrĩre thiirĩ ũcio wa mũndũ-wa-nja wa kũngĩ.
English: Take his garment when he puts up collateral for a stranger. Hold it for a wayward woman!

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩrathima mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake rũciinĩ tene anĩrĩire-rĩ, ũndũ ũcio ũngĩtuĩka taarĩ kĩrumi.
English: He who blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning, it will be taken as a curse by him.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia wa haaro ahaanaine na maaĩ maratata mategũtigithĩria hĩndĩ ya mbura;
English: A continual dropping on a rainy day and a contentious wife are alike:

Kikuyu: kũgeria kũmũgirĩrĩria nĩ ta kũgeria kũgirĩrĩria rũhuho, kana kũgeria gũkumbatĩria maguta na guoko.
English: restraining her is like restraining the wind, or like grasping oil in his right hand.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kĩgera kĩnooraga kĩrĩa kĩngĩ, no taguo mũndũ ohĩgagia ũrĩa ũngĩ.
English: Iron sharpens iron; so a man sharpens his friend’s countenance.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩmagĩra mũkũyũ nĩakaarĩa maciaro maguo, na mũndũ ũrĩa ũtungataga mwathi wake nĩagatĩĩo.
English: Whoever tends the fig tree shall eat its fruit. He who looks after his master shall be honored.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mũndũ onaga ũthiũ wake maaĩ-inĩ, no taguo ngoro ya mũndũ yonanagia ũrĩa mũndũ atariĩ.
English: Like water reflects a face, so a man’s heart reflects the man.

Kikuyu: Gĩkuũ na Mwanangĩko itiiganagia, na o namo maitho ma mũndũ matiiganagia.
English: Sheol† and Abaddon are never satisfied; and a man’s eyes are never satisfied.

Kikuyu: Nyũngũ nĩ ya gũthererio betha, na kĩrugutĩro nĩ gĩa gũthererio thahabu, no mũndũ athimagwo na igweta rĩrĩa agwetagwo narĩo.
English: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace for gold; but man is refined by his praise.

Kikuyu: O na ũngĩhũũrĩra mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ndĩrĩ-inĩ, ũmũhũũre na mũũthĩ ta ngano-rĩ, ndangiumwo nĩ ũrimũ wake.
English: Though you grind a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with grain, yet his foolishness will not be removed from him.

Kikuyu: Thũũrimaga mahiũ maku na kĩyo, ũmenyagĩrĩre wega ndũũru ciaku;
English: Know well the state of your flocks, and pay attention to your herds,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ũtonga ndũtũũraga nginya tene, o nayo thũmbĩ ti ya gũkinyĩra njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: for riches are not forever, nor does the crown endure to all generations.

Kikuyu: Nyeki ĩrĩa nyũmũ rĩrĩa yatũgũtwo na nyeki njerũ ĩkunũke-rĩ, nayo ĩrĩa ya irĩma-inĩ yũnganio na ĩinũkio-rĩ,
English: The hay is removed, and the new growth appears, the grasses of the hills are gathered in.

Kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo tũgondu nĩtũgaatũma ũgĩe na nguo, nacio mbũri itũme ũgĩe na gĩa kũgũra mũgũnda.
English: The lambs are for your clothing, and the goats are the price of a field.

Kikuyu: Nĩũkagĩa na iria rĩingĩ rĩa mbũri rĩa gũkũigana na rĩa kũnyuuo nĩ andũ a nyũmba yaku, o na rĩa gũtũũria airĩtu aku a wĩra. 28
English: There will be plenty of goats’ milk for your food, for your family’s food, and for the nourishment of your servant girls. 28

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu moraga o na gũtarĩ mũndũ ũramathingata, no arĩa athingu momagĩrĩria ta mũrũũthi.
English: The wicked flee when no one pursues; but the righteous are as bold as a lion.

Kikuyu: Bũrũri ũngĩĩhia, nĩũgĩaga na aathani aingĩ, no mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku na akagĩa na ũmenyo nĩatũmaga bũrũri ũkindĩrie.
English: In rebellion, a land has many rulers, but order is maintained by a man of understanding and knowledge.

Kikuyu: Mwathani ũrĩa ũhatagĩrĩria andũ arĩa athĩĩni ahaana ta mbura ya rũhuho ĩrĩa ĩtatigagia irio.
English: A needy man who oppresses the poor is like a driving rain which leaves no crops.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matirikaga watho magaathagĩrĩria andũ arĩa aaganu, no arĩa marũmagia watho magianaga nao.
English: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked; but those who keep the law contend with them.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa ooru matiiguaga maũndũ ma kĩhooto, no arĩa marongoragia Jehova nĩmataũkagĩrwo nĩ mo biũ.
English: Evil men don’t understand justice; but those who seek Yahweh understand it fully.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba mũndũ mũthĩĩni ũrĩa ũthiiaga na mĩthiĩre ĩtarĩ ũcuuke, gũkĩra gĩtonga gĩthiiaga na mĩthiĩre mĩogomu.
English: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than he who is perverse in his ways, and he is rich.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmagia watho nĩ mwana ũrĩ na gũkũũrana, no mwana ũrĩa ũthiiaga na thiritũ ya arĩa akoroku aconorithagia ithe.
English: Whoever keeps the law is a wise son; but he who is a companion of gluttons shames his father.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũingĩhagia ũtonga wake na ũndũ wa gũkombithia mbeeca na gwĩtia uumithio mũkĩru aũigagĩra mũndũ ũngĩ, ũrĩa ũkaiguĩra athĩĩni tha.
English: He who increases his wealth by excessive interest gathers it for one who has pity on the poor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũregaga kũigua watho, o na mahooya make nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi.
English: He who turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩhĩtithia andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩguo magere njĩra ya ũũru, akaagũa mũtego-inĩ wake we mwene, no ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũcuuke, wega nĩguo ũgaatuĩka igai rĩake.
English: Whoever causes the upright to go astray in an evil way, he will fall into his own trap; but the blameless will inherit good.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtongu no eyone taarĩ mũũgĩ maitho-inĩ make, no mũndũ mũthĩĩni ũrĩa ũũĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ amuonaga o ũrĩa atariĩ.
English: The rich man is wise in his own eyes; but the poor who has understanding sees through him.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa arĩa athingu maatoorania, nĩkũgĩaga na ndũhiũ nene; no rĩrĩa arĩa aaganu maatũũgĩra, andũ nĩ kwĩhitha mehithaga.
English: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory; but when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhithaga mehia make ndagaacagĩra, no ũrĩa ũmoimbũraga na agatigana namo nĩaiguagĩrwo tha.
English: He who conceals his sins doesn’t prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtigagĩra Jehova hĩndĩ ciothe, no ũrĩa ũmagia ngoro yake nĩkũgũa agũũaga thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: Blessed is the man who always fears; but one who hardens his heart falls into trouble.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu agĩatha andũ matarĩ na ũhoti, nĩ ta mũrũũthi ũkũrarama, kana nduba ĩgũticũka.
English: As a roaring lion or a charging bear, so is a wicked ruler over helpless people.

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũtarĩ na ũtaũku nĩahinyagĩrĩria andũ mũno, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũthũire uumithio ũtagĩrĩire nĩagakenera matukũ maingĩ ma muoyo wake.
English: A tyrannical ruler lacks judgment. One who hates ill-gotten gain will have long days.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũritũhĩre nĩ thakame ya mũndũ ooragĩte, egũtũũra ehithaga nginya gũkua gwake; mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndakanamũteithĩrĩrie.
English: A man who is tormented by blood guilt will be a fugitive until death. No one will support him.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mĩthiĩre yake ĩtarĩ ũcuuke aikaraga arĩ mũgitĩre, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũthiiaga na njĩra cia waganu akaagũa o rĩmwe, hubu!
English: Whoever walks blamelessly is kept safe; but one with perverse ways will fall suddenly.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩmaga mũgũnda wake arĩĩkoragwo na irio nyingĩ, no ũrĩa ũhanyũkanagia na maũndũ hatarĩ arĩhũũnaga o thĩĩna.
English: One who works his land will have an abundance of food; but one who chases fantasies will have his fill of poverty.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwĩhokeku arĩrathimagwo mũno, no ũrĩa wĩriragĩria kũhiũha gũtonga ndakaaga kũherithio.
English: A faithful man is rich with blessings; but one who is eager to be rich will not go unpunished.

Kikuyu: Gũtĩĩra mũndũ maũthĩ ti wega: no mũndũ no eke ũũru nĩ ũndũ wa kenyũ ka irio.
English: To show partiality is not good, yet a man will do wrong for a piece of bread.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkarĩ nĩeriragĩria mũno gũtonga, na ndooĩ atĩ etereirwo nĩ ũthĩĩni.
English: A stingy man hurries after riches, and doesn’t know that poverty waits for him.
