Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũithagia ũngĩ nĩagatuĩka wa kwendwo nĩwe thuutha-inĩ, gũkĩra mũndũ wa rũrĩmĩ rwa kwĩyendithĩrĩria.
English: One who rebukes a man will afterward find more favor than one who flatters with the tongue.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtunyaga ithe kana nyina kĩndũ kĩao, agacooka akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ti ũũru ndeka,” ũcio marĩ rũmwe na ũrĩa wanangaga.
English: Whoever robs his father or his mother and says, “It’s not wrong,” is a partner with a destroyer.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkoroku aarahũraga ngarari, no mũndũ wĩhokete Jehova nĩakagaacĩra.
English: One who is greedy stirs up strife; but one who trusts in Yahweh will prosper.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩĩhokaga we mwene nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, no ũrĩa ũthiiaga na njĩra cia ũũgĩ aikaraga arĩ mũgitĩre.
English: One who trusts in himself is a fool; but one who walks in wisdom is kept safe.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũheaga athĩĩni ndarĩ kĩndũ akaaga, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũmahingagĩra maitho nĩarĩĩrumagwo kaingĩ.
English: One who gives to the poor has no lack; but one who closes his eyes will have many curses.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ arĩa aaganu maatuĩka a gwathana, andũ nĩ kwĩhitha mehithaga; no rĩrĩa arĩa aaganu mathira, arĩa athingu nĩmatheeremaga. 29
English: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous thrive. 29

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũikaraga omĩtie ngingo thuutha wa kũrũithio maita maingĩ, akaanangwo o rĩmwe, na aage kĩhonia.
English: He who is often rebuked and stiffens his neck will be destroyed suddenly, with no remedy.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ arĩa athingu matheerema, andũ nĩmakenaga; no rĩrĩa andũ arĩa aaganu maathana, andũ nĩgũcaaya macaayaga.
English: When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice; but when the wicked rule, the people groan.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wendete ũũgĩ atũũragia ithe na gĩkeno, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũceeraga na maraya nĩgũitanga aitangaga ũtonga wake.
English: Whoever loves wisdom brings joy to his father; but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũrĩ matuĩro ma kĩhooto aikaragia bũrũri ũrĩ mwĩkindĩru, no ũrĩa ũkorokagĩra mahaki nĩ kwananga awanangaga.
English: The king by justice makes the land stable, but he who takes bribes tears it down.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩyendithagĩrĩria harĩ mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake na maheeni, nĩ wabu aambagĩra magũrũ make.
English: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu ategagwo nĩ mehia make we mwene, no mũndũ mũthingu ainaga na agakena.
English: An evil man is snared by his sin, but the righteous can sing and be glad.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩmamenyagĩrĩra kĩhooto kĩa arĩa athĩĩni, no arĩa aaganu matirũmbũyagia kĩhooto kĩao.
English: The righteous care about justice for the poor. The wicked aren’t concerned about knowledge.

Kikuyu: Anyũrũrania matũmaga itũũra inene rĩrĩmbũke ta mwaki, no andũ oogĩ nĩkũniina maniinaga marakara.
English: Mockers stir up a city, but wise men turn away anger.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ angĩthiĩ igooti-inĩ na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, mũndũ mũkĩĩgu arĩrĩmbũkaga nĩ marakara na akanyũrũrania, naguo thayũ ũkaaga.
English: If a wise man goes to court with a foolish man, the fool rages or scoffs, and there is no peace.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maitaga thakame nĩmathũire mũndũ mwĩhokeku, no andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru macaragia ũrĩa mangĩtiirĩrĩra muoyo wake.
English: The bloodthirsty hate a man of integrity; and they seek the life of the upright.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu aitũrũraga marakara make mothe, no mũndũ mũũgĩ aikaraga amarigĩrĩirie, akahoorera.
English: A fool vents all of his anger, but a wise man brings himself under control.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wathanaga angĩthikĩrĩria maheeni-rĩ, anene ake othe matuĩkaga andũ aaganu.
English: If a ruler listens to lies, all of his officials are wicked.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthĩĩni na mũndũ mũhinyanĩrĩria harĩ ũndũ ũmwe ũmahaananĩtie: Jehova nĩwe ũtũmaga maitho mao eerĩ moone.
English: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: Yahweh gives sight to the eyes of both.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki angĩtuĩra athĩĩni ciira na kĩhooto-rĩ, gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene gĩtũũraga kĩrũmĩte nginya tene.
English: The king who fairly judges the poor, his throne shall be established forever.

Kikuyu: Rũthanju na kũrũithio ciĩkagĩra mũndũ ũũgĩ, no mwana wa kũrekererio aconorithagia nyina.
English: The rod of correction gives wisdom, but a child left to himself causes shame to his mother.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ aaganu matheerema, noguo mehia matheeremaga, no andũ arĩa athingu nĩmakerorera igwa rĩao.
English: When the wicked increase, sin increases; but the righteous will see their downfall.

Kikuyu: Herithagia mũrũguo, na nĩagatũma ũgĩe na thayũ; nĩagatũma ũgĩe na gĩkeno ngoro-inĩ.
English: Correct your son, and he will give you peace; yes, he will bring delight to your soul.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩa gũtarĩ na ũguũrio, kĩrĩndĩ gĩticookaga kwĩrigĩrĩria; no gũkena nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmagia watho.
English: Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint; but one who keeps the law is blessed.

Kikuyu: Ndungata ndĩngĩhera nĩ kwarĩrio gũtheri; o na ĩngĩtaũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ndĩngĩũrũmbũiya.
English: A servant can’t be corrected by words. Though he understands, yet he will not respond.

Kikuyu: Wanona mũndũ ũhiũhaga kwaria? Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu arĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kũmũkĩra.
English: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩnania ndungata yake kuuma wĩthĩ wayo, marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩĩkamũrehera kĩeha.
English: He who pampers his servant from youth will have him become a son in the end.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa marakara aarahũraga ngarari, nake mũndũ wa marũrũ agĩĩaga na mehia maingĩ.
English: An angry man stirs up strife, and a wrathful man abounds in sin.

Kikuyu: Mwĩtĩĩo wa mũndũ nĩũmũnyiihagia, no mũndũ ũrĩa wa ngoro ya kwĩnyiihia nĩagatĩĩo.
English: A man’s pride brings him low, but one of lowly spirit gains honor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa kũgĩa thiritũ na mũici nĩ muoyo wake mwene athũire; ehĩtithagio na ndangĩruta ũira.
English: Whoever is an accomplice of a thief is an enemy of his own soul. He takes an oath, but dares not testify.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra mũndũ gũtuĩkaga mũtego, no ũrĩa wĩhokaga Jehova aikaraga arĩ mũgitĩre.
English: The fear of man proves to be a snare, but whoever puts his trust in Yahweh is kept safe.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmacaragia kũiguuo nĩ mũnene, no mũndũ oonaga kĩhooto kuuma kũrĩ Jehova.
English: Many seek the ruler’s favor, but a man’s justice comes from Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu monaga andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku ta kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi; nao andũ arĩa aaganu moonaga andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru ta kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi. 30
English: A dishonest man detests the righteous, and the upright in their ways detest the wicked. 30

Kikuyu: Ĩno nĩyo mĩario ya Aguri mũrũ wa Jake, na nĩ ndũmĩrĩri nditũ: Mũndũ ũcio eerire Ithieli, akĩĩra Ithieli na Ukali atĩrĩ:
English: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, the revelation: the man says to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal:

Kikuyu: “Niĩ ndĩ mũndũ mũriitu gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ; ndirĩ na ũtaũku ta wa mũndũ.
English: “Surely I am the most ignorant man, and don’t have a man’s understanding.

Kikuyu: Ndiĩrutĩte kũgĩa na ũũgĩ, o na kana ngamenya Ũrĩa Mũtheru.
English: I have not learned wisdom, neither do I have the knowledge of the Holy One.

Kikuyu: Nũũ wanambata igũrũ na agacooka agaikũrũka gũkũ thĩ? Nũũ wanacookereria rũhuho ngundi-inĩ ciake? Maaĩ namo-rĩ, nũũ ũrĩ wamoohania na nguo yake ya igũrũ? Nũũ wahaandire ituri ciothe cia thĩ? Etagwo atĩa, nake mũriũ etagwo atĩa? Akorwo nĩũũĩ-rĩ, ndũkĩnjĩĩre!
English: Who has ascended up into heaven, and descended? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has bound the waters in his garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son’s name, if you know?

Kikuyu: “Kiugo gĩothe kĩa Ngai gĩtirĩ mahĩtia; nĩwe ngo ya arĩa moragĩra harĩ we.
English: “Every word of God is flawless. He is a shield to those who take refuge in him.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanongerere ciugo ciake, ndakae gũkũrũithia wonekane ũrĩ wa maheeni.
English: Don’t you add to his words, lest he reprove you, and you be found a liar.

Kikuyu: “Nĩngũkũhooya maũndũ meerĩ, Wee Jehova; ndũkanarege kũhe mo mbere ya ngue:
English: “Two things I have asked of you. Don’t deny me before I die.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, njehereria maũndũ matarĩ ma ma, na ma maheeni, cindaihĩrĩrie mũno; ningĩ ndũkaahe ũthĩĩni kana ũtonga, no ũũheage irio cia o mũthenya.
English: Remove far from me falsehood and lies. Give me neither poverty nor riches. Feed me with the food that is needful for me,

Kikuyu: Ndikaagĩe na indo nyingĩ mũno, ngũkaane, njũũrie atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova nũũ?’ Kana thĩĩne mũno, njiye, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩmenererie rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai wakwa.
English: lest I be full, deny you, and say, ‘Who is Yahweh?’ or lest I be poor, and steal, and so dishonor the name of my God.

Kikuyu: “Ndũgacambie ndungata kũrĩ mũnene wayo, ndĩgagĩkũrume, nawe ũrĩhio ũũru ũcio.
English: “Don’t slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you be held guilty.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ kũrĩ andũ marumaga maithe mao o na matangĩrathima manyina mao;
English: There is a generation that curses their father, and doesn’t bless their mother.

Kikuyu: kũrĩ andũ arĩa meyonaga marĩ atheru maitho-inĩ mao, o rĩrĩa matathambĩtio gĩko kĩao;
English: There is a generation that is pure in their own eyes, yet are not washed from their filthiness.

Kikuyu: nĩ arĩa marĩ maitho ma mwĩtĩĩo, o arĩa maroraga andũ manyiira;
English: There is a generation, oh how lofty are their eyes! Their eyelids are lifted up.

Kikuyu: nĩo arĩa magego mao mahaana ta hiũ cia njora, na ngambucu ciao ihaana ta tũhiũ twa gũtambuura arĩa athĩĩni, mahuke thĩ, na arĩa abatari, mathire andũ-inĩ.
English: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords, and their jaws like knives, to devour the poor from the earth, and the needy from among men.

Kikuyu: “Thambara ĩrĩ airĩtu eerĩ. Matitigithagĩria kuuga, ‘Tũhei! Tũhei!’ “Kũrĩ indo ithatũ itarĩ hĩndĩ ihũũnaga, o na nĩ inya itarĩ hĩndĩ ingiuga, ‘Nĩndaigania!’
English: “The leech has two daughters: ‘Give, give.’ “There are three things that are never satisfied; four that don’t say, ‘Enough!’:

Kikuyu: Nacio nĩ mbĩrĩra, na nda thaata, na thĩ tondũ ndĩrĩ hĩndĩ ĩiganagia maaĩ, na mwaki tondũ ndũrĩ hĩndĩ uugaga, ‘Nĩndaigania!’
English: Sheol,† the barren womb, the earth that is not satisfied with water, and the fire that doesn’t say, ‘Enough!’

Kikuyu: “Riitho rĩa mũndũ ũrĩa ũnyũrũragia ithe, na akarurũka kũiguaga nyina, rĩgaakũũrwo nĩ mahuru ma kĩanda-inĩ, na rĩkaarĩĩo nĩ nderi.
English: “The eye that mocks at his father, and scorns obedience to his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, the young eagles shall eat it.

Kikuyu: “Kũrĩ maũndũ matatũ mangegagia mũno makĩria, o na nĩ mana marĩa niĩ itakuũkagĩrwo nĩmo:
English: “There are three things which are too amazing for me, four which I don’t understand:

Kikuyu: namo nĩ mũthiĩre wa nderi rĩera-inĩ, na mũthiĩre wa nyoka rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga, na mũthiĩre wa marikabu ĩrĩ iria-inĩ gatagatĩ, na mũthiĩre wa mũndũ makĩendana na mũirĩtu.
English: The way of an eagle in the air, the way of a serpent on a rock, the way of a ship in the middle of the sea, and the way of a man with a maiden.

Kikuyu: “Ũyũ nĩguo mũthiĩre wa mũndũ-wa-nja mũtharia: Arĩĩaga na akehuura mĩromo, akoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Ndirĩ ũndũ mũũru njĩkĩte.’
English: “So is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth, and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’

Kikuyu: “Kũrĩ maũndũ matatũ matũmaga thĩ ĩthingithe, o na nĩ mana ĩtangĩĩtiiria:
English: “For three things the earth trembles, and under four, it can’t bear up:

Kikuyu: namo nĩ ndungata ĩtuĩkĩte mũthamaki, na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ahũũnĩte irio,
English: For a servant when he is king, a fool when he is filled with food,

Kikuyu: na mũndũ-wa-nja ũtangĩendeka ahikĩte, na ndungata ya mũirĩtu ĩtuĩkĩte mũtumia wa mũthuuri wa mũmĩathi.
English: for an unloved woman when she is married, and a servant who is heir to her mistress.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ kũrĩ nyamũ inya nini gũkũ thĩ, no nĩ njũgĩ mũno:
English: “There are four things which are little on the earth, but they are exceedingly wise:

Kikuyu: Thigiriri-rĩ, nĩ ciũmbe itarĩ hinya, no nĩciĩigagĩra mũthiithũ wa irio hĩndĩ ya riũa;
English: The ants are not a strong people, yet they provide their food in the summer.

Kikuyu: ikami nĩ ciũmbe itarĩ ũhoti, no nĩciĩthondekagĩra ciikaro ciacio ndwaro-inĩ cia mahiga;*
English: The hyraxes are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks.

Kikuyu: ngigĩ itirĩ mũthamaki wacio, no nĩitwaranaga hamwe irĩ mbũtũ;
English: The locusts have no king, yet they advance in ranks.

Kikuyu: njagathi no inyiitwo na rũhĩ, no ĩtũũraga thĩinĩ wa nyũmba cia athamaki.
English: You can catch a lizard with your hands, yet it is in kings’ palaces.

Kikuyu: “Kũrĩ indo ithatũ irĩ mũkinyũkĩrie mũgaacĩru, o na nĩ inya ngaacĩru rĩrĩa igũthiĩ:
English: “There are three things which are stately in their march, four which are stately in going:

Kikuyu: nĩ mũrũũthi, arĩ guo ũrĩ hinya mũno gatagatĩ ka nyamũ iria ingĩ ciothe, na ndũngĩeherera nyamũ ĩrĩkũ kana ĩrĩkũ njĩra;
English: The lion, which is mightiest among animals, and doesn’t turn away for any;

Kikuyu: nĩ njamba ya ngũkũ ĩrĩa ĩkinyũkagia na nguthi, na thenge, na mũthamaki mũthiũrũrũkĩrie nĩ mbũtũ ciake cia ita.
English: the greyhound; the male goat; and the king against whom there is no rising up.

Kikuyu: “Angĩkorwo nĩwĩtuĩte mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, na ũgetũũgĩria, kana ũkorwo nĩũthugundĩte gwĩka ũũru-rĩ, humbĩra kanua gaku na rũhĩ!
English: “If you have done foolishly in lifting up yourself, or if you have thought evil, put your hand over your mouth.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa iria rĩthũcagwo, rĩkoima thiagĩ, na o ta ũrĩa iniũrũ rĩthioragwo rĩkoira thakame-rĩ, ũguo noguo gũthogotha marakara kũrehaga ngũĩ.” 31
English: For as the churning of milk produces butter, and the wringing of the nose produces blood, so the forcing of wrath produces strife.” 31

Kikuyu: Ĩno nĩyo mĩario ya Mũthamaki Lemueli: nĩ ndũmĩrĩri nditũ ĩrĩa aarutirwo nĩ nyina:
English: The words of King Lemuel—the revelation which his mother taught him:

Kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee mũrũ wakwa, ngũkwĩra atĩa, o wee mwana wa nda yakwa, ngũkwĩra atĩa o wee mwana wa mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa,
English: “Oh, my son! Oh, son of my womb! Oh, son of my vows!

Kikuyu: atĩrĩ, ndũkanaitange hinya waku na andũ-a-nja, kana ũhe ũhoti waku acio manangaga athamaki.*
English: Don’t give your strength to women, nor your ways to that which destroys kings.

Kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee Lemueli, athamaki matiagĩrĩirwo nĩkũnyua ndibei, ti mũtugo wa athamaki kũnyua ndibei, anene matiagĩrĩirwo nĩ kwĩrirĩria njoohi,
English: It is not for kings, Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, nor for princes to say, ‘Where is strong drink?’

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha matikanyue mariganĩrwo nĩ ũrĩa watho uugaga, nao magithie arĩa othe ahinyĩrĩrie kĩhooto kĩao.
English: lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the justice due to anyone who is afflicted.

Kikuyu: Heaga arĩa marathira njoohi, na ndibei kũrĩ arĩa marĩ na ruo rũnene rwa ngoro;
English: Give strong drink to him who is ready to perish, and wine to the bitter in soul.

Kikuyu: reke manyue mariganĩrwo nĩ ũthĩĩni wao, na matige gũcooka kũririkana kĩeha kĩao.
English: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

Kikuyu: “Arĩrĩria arĩa matangĩhota kwĩyarĩrĩria, warĩrĩrie kĩhooto kĩa arĩa othe anyamaarĩku.
English: Open your mouth for the mute, in the cause of all who are left desolate.

Kikuyu: Maarĩrĩrie, na ũmatuĩre ciira na kĩhooto; rũĩrĩra kĩhooto kĩa arĩa athĩĩni na arĩa abatari.”
English: Open your mouth, judge righteously, and serve justice to the poor and needy.”

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ngatha-rĩ, angĩoneka kũ? Nĩgũkorwo thogora wake nĩũkĩrĩte mũno ũrĩa wa ruru iria ndune.
English: †Who can find a worthy woman? For her value is far above rubies.

Kikuyu: Mũthuuri wake nĩamwĩhokete biũ, na ndarĩ kĩndũ kĩa bata angĩaga.
English: The heart of her husband trusts in her. He shall have no lack of gain.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ũcio, matukũ mothe ma muoyo wake, no maũndũ mega ekaga mũthuuriwe, na ndangĩmwĩka ũũru.
English: She does him good, and not harm, all the days of her life.

Kikuyu: Ethaga guoya wa ngʼondu na ndigi cia ũguta, agacirutĩra wĩra na kĩyo, na moko make.
English: She seeks wool and flax, and works eagerly with her hands.

Kikuyu: Ahaana ta marikabu cia wonjoria, tondũ athũgũraga irio ciake kuuma kũraya.
English: She is like the merchant ships. She brings her bread from afar.

Kikuyu: Okĩraga rũciinĩ kũrĩ o na nduma; akahe andũ a nyũmba yake irio, na akagaĩra ndungata ciake cia airĩtu mawĩra mao.
English: She rises also while it is yet night, gives food to her household, and portions for her servant girls.

Kikuyu: Ecũũranagia ũhoro wa mũgũnda mũna, akaũgũra; kuumana na ũthũkũmi wake, ahaandaga mũgũnda wa mĩthabibũ.
English: She considers a field, and buys it. With the fruit of her hands, she plants a vineyard.

Kikuyu: Ehotoragĩra wĩra wake erutanĩirie; moko make marĩ hinya wa kwĩrutĩra wĩra.
English: She arms her waist with strength, and makes her arms strong.

Kikuyu: Nĩatigagĩrĩra atĩ wonjoria wake nĩũrĩ na uumithio, naguo tawa wake ũraaraga ũgĩakana.
English: She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp doesn’t go out by night.

Kikuyu: Anyiitaga mũtĩ wa kuogotha ndigi na guoko, nakĩo gĩcũni kĩa ndigi agakĩnyiitĩrĩra na ciara ciake.
English: She lays her hands to the distaff, and her hands hold the spindle.

Kikuyu: Nĩataanahagĩra andũ arĩa athĩĩni, nao arĩa abatari akamatambũrũkĩria moko make.
English: She opens her arms to the poor; yes, she extends her hands to the needy.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa tharunji yagũa-rĩ, we ndamakaga atĩ nĩĩgũthĩĩnia andũ a nyũmba yake; nĩgũkorwo othe nĩahumbe nguo cia rangi mũtune.
English: She is not afraid of the snow for her household, for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

Kikuyu: Nĩatumaga indo cia kwara ũrĩrĩ wake; nake ehumbaga nguo cia gatani ĩrĩa njega, na cia rangi wa ndathi.†
English: She makes for herself carpets of tapestry. Her clothing is fine linen and purple.

Kikuyu: Mũthuuri wake nĩaheagwo gĩtĩĩo kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra inene, harĩa aikaraga marĩ na athuuri a bũrũri.‡
English: Her husband is respected in the gates, when he sits among the elders of the land.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ũcio nĩatumaga nguo cia gatani agaciendia, na agatwaragĩra onjoria mĩcibi.
English: She makes linen garments and sells them, and delivers sashes to the merchant.

Kikuyu: Hinya na ũtĩĩku nĩcio nguo iria ehumbaga; nĩakenaga ategwĩtigĩra matukũ marĩa magooka.
English: Strength and dignity are her clothing. She laughs at the time to come.

Kikuyu: Aaragia ciugo cia ũũgĩ, naruo rũrĩmĩ rwake rũrutanaga maũndũ ma ũtugi.
English: She opens her mouth with wisdom. Kind instruction is on her tongue.

Kikuyu: Nĩarũmbũyagia maũndũ ma nyũmba yake, na ndarĩĩaga irio atanogeire.
English: She looks well to the ways of her household, and doesn’t eat the bread of idleness.

Kikuyu: Ciana ciake ciarahũkaga, cikoiga arorathimwo; o nake mũthuuri wake nĩamũgaathagĩrĩria, akamwĩra atĩrĩ:
English: Her children rise up and call her blessed. Her husband also praises her:

Kikuyu: “Andũ-a-nja aingĩ manatuĩka ngatha, no wee nĩũmakĩrĩte othe.”
English: “Many women do noble things, but you excel them all.”

Kikuyu: Ngoga nĩ cia kũheenania, naguo ũthaka nĩ wa tũhũ; no mũtumia ũrĩa mwĩtigĩri Jehova nĩ wa kũgaathĩrĩrio.
English: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain; but a woman who fears Yahweh, she shall be praised.

Kikuyu: Mũrĩhei mũcaara wa wĩra ũrĩa arutĩte, naguo wĩra wake ũrekwo ũmũgaathĩrĩrie kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra rĩrĩa inene.
English: Give her of the fruit of her hands! Let her works praise her in the gates!
